`import time
from datetime import datetime
import can
from can.interfaces.etas import EtasBus
from can import Message

from can.io.blf import BLFWriter

bitrate = 500000

timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
log_filename = f'can_log_{timestamp}.blf'
print(timestamp)

configs = can.interface.detect_available_configs(interfaces="etas")
for c in configs:
    print(c)`
    
**I tried to use detect_available_configs to initialize the output of the identified device, but for now the problem seems to be that I am directly stuck in this function. And I successfully installed the driver and BOA**

title: Adding driver support for custom hardware
author: S-adnan556
body:
Hello, I’m working on adding support for a custom CAN hardware interface to Busmaster. So far, I’ve made UI changes by adding an enum for my driver, adding the driver name in the driver list in sg_ListDIL (Sources/Kernel/BusmasterDriverInterface/DIL_CAN.cpp), and loading the .dll in the DILC_SelectDriver() function. I successfully built and ran Busmaster by copying and renaming an existing .dll for my driver. My custom driver appears in the GUI, and if I connect it using the copied .dll (e.g., PEAK), it works for that hardware.

Now, I’ve copied and renamed the CAN_Vector_XL and included it in the project. I want to make changes to it so that it can work or my custom board. I need guidance on the next steps to enable communication with my custom hardware. Any documentation or pointers would be greatly appreciated, as I'm not sure how to proceed.
body_text:
Hello, I’m working on adding support for a custom CAN hardware interface to Busmaster. So far, I’ve made UI changes by adding an enum for my driver, adding the driver name in the driver list in sg_ListDIL (Sources/Kernel/BusmasterDriverInterface/DIL_CAN.cpp), and loading the .dll in the DILC_SelectDriver() function. I successfully built and ran Busmaster by copying and renaming an existing .dll for my driver. My custom driver appears in the GUI, and if I connect it using the copied .dll (e.g., PEAK), it works for that hardware.

title: Very basic CAN database problem (3.2.2)  "end bit of signal is out of message length"
author: etuer
body:
I create a new message, 8 bytes long, in the CAN dbf editor.
The first "signal" should be starting at bit 0, 16 bits long, unsigned int, motorola type.

It doesn't allow it!  The error is "end bit of signal is out of message length"
It will allow if it's Intel format.  Or if the signal length is reduced to 8 bits.


Then the next signal, which should be starting at bit 16 (or byte 2, bit 0), in fact is sent starting at byte 1.

Am I misunderstanding something basic, or does this just not work?
body_text:
I create a new message, 8 bytes long, in the CAN dbf editor.
The first "signal" should be starting at bit 0, 16 bits long, unsigned int, motorola type.

It doesn't allow it!  The error is "end bit of signal is out of message length"
It will allow if it's Intel format.  Or if the signal length is reduced to 8 bits.


Then the next signal, which should be starting at bit 16 (or byte 2, bit 0), in fact is sent starting at byte 1.

Am I misunderstanding something basic, or does this just not work?

comments.author: B2B-Rohit
comments.body:
when working with motorola type your end bit becomes the start bit for the busmaster and your start bit becomes the end bit.
for example if your signal is 16 bit long and starts at the 0th bit in intel hex you should provide "BYTE Index" to 1 and "Start Bit" to 0. This feature is pretty confusing though I guess we have to work this way for now.

I only install it on new PCs and then it works as intended.

Maybe you can manually just remove it.
Maybe it is a Win11 issue. As most network problems nowadays.

title: ISO 14299 incorrect diagnostic message send
author: dskibarko
body:
The UDS messages should by a 8 bytes of CAN data allways. Not used bytes should be filled by paging data (0xAA value)/ It is a strong requirements but BusMaster can't support it.
For example command "Default diagnostic session" supported only this **(0x02)  0x10 0x01** but not support strong ISO 8 bytes data  **(0x02) 0x10 0x01  0xAA 0xAA 0xAA 0xAA 0xAA**.
I try to do manualy and observe strange multiframe transmitted instead of my data. Behavior is on the picture
![BusMasterOpenSessionIssue](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/assets/20500604/153a2b17-b481-4f42-bf9f-f33fed690ce8)

comments.author: reymor
comments.body:
Hello @dskibarko

1. BusMaster does not have support any more. The last release was 7 years ago.
![image](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/assets/39070043/b5914dff-a71c-475a-bde5-4f40556482cb)
2. I do not understand your point here, maybe I'm wrong here but you are trying to send 8 bytes. This means you will need to use ISO-TP. 

In your example, what could be "wrong" is that they do not 'clean' or 'set' the memory to a known value (AA, 00, or whatever), but you are trying to send 8 bytes which mean that this does not fit into 1 frame. 

You could take [this](https://www.csselectronics.com/pages/uds-protocol-tutorial-unified-diagnostic-services) as reference. 

![image](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/assets/39070043/1715e5ee-eb88-4b44-a499-c6f150b1de3c)

When the payload exceeds 7 bytes, it needs to be split across multiple CAN frames. This is your case you are trying to send 8 bytes. You will need 2 CAN frames

![image](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/assets/39070043/68a96d0b-f592-449f-8f65-b12a5f6fbc8b)

This trace was is saying is: 
1. First message (FF)
1 --> FF (First frame)
008 --> Size (8bytes)
Your data: 10 01 AA AA AA AA

2. Flow control message (FC)
03 01 00 00 00 00 00 00 (send by your ECU)

3. Consecutive Frame (CF)
2 --> CF (Consecutive frame)
1 --> index
Your remaining data (AA AA)

This reassemble into 10 01 AA AA AA AA AA AA --> Your original message

The other part is just the same as in the FF (This is, I think that it is "wrong"). However, I have seen this on some products.

About the padding, I'm not really sure, I've seen implementation in which they used 00s or AAs as you mention. However, I think that by ISO14299 they do not specify this as AAs. 

Maybe, your misunderstanding is that when you put the databytes it means the bare frame. However, it means the data in UDS over CAN context.

comments.author: dskibarko
comments.body:
Hello @reymor !

Fine behavior should be same:
   User define data for send diagnostic message  **10 01 хх хх хх хх хх**  (7 bytes for  diagnostic session 1 command send) and Busmaster should send to ECU **eight** data bytes **02 10 01 хх хх хх хх хх** 
   Were 02 first byte is f lenght of diagnostic message (not CAN frame! CAN frame DLC should be 8), 
  and 10 is a open diagnostic session ID according ISO-14299 with parameter 01. 
   The eight data bytes for CAN-frame and requriments to use paged bytes is defined ISO 15765 -3/-4.
![ISO_15765-3_8bytes](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/assets/20500604/653eb1d8-7c66-4542-ad17-358dc4e3d4f9)


By the way, the Busmaster should to understund the first byte of data and if it a UDS service value (in this case the value 10 is a diagnostic session control service ID) do not send multiframe.

comments.author: reymor
comments.body:
As I said, it is said padded. It does not said padded with 0xAA. Anyway, this is not a "big" problem. 

Here, I do not understad, if I set this to 10 01 

![image](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/assets/39070043/157f1f4b-b8f6-4e93-abe6-8df4d9e70d5f)

It sent `02 10 01 00 00 00 00 00` which what you said. What is the problem with that?

You said that it shall be `02 10 01 AA AA AA AA AA`, right? I think that in this case both are correct.

title: Unable to set scale factor less then 0.000001
author: evilruff
body: Then defining signals for J1939 messages and setting a signal factor less then 0.000001 (for example 0.00000001 is requred to Positional NMEA2000 messages) it drops to 0.0000000. However after saving database to file it's possible to adjust scale factor to needed value and it will work perfectly then activated, just until you try to load file into editor again.

comments.author: reymor
comments.body:
Well, I think that we have a different view of what CANOpen is. 

Having the message buffer and doing an interpreation is NOT support for CANOpen. This is exactly the same idea with UDS, you could say, you can make the payload with the requirements, etc, but it is NOT support for UDS. 

If we talk in the context of other open source tools, like Wireshark. You have an ethernet trace, and if you are using a propietary protocol, which is not supported by Wireshark, it does not mean that you are not able to see the data. You will see the data, but without any meaning, that what that propietary protocol means to you by that. You could offcourse create a dissector, etc. But clearly Wireshark does not support your propietary protocol. 

In this case, off course you can receive CAN messages, but without any meaning because Busmaster does NOT support CANopen. Could you interprent this data? Off course, you could but it does not mean that Busmaster support CANopen.

![WhatsApp Image 2024-01-16 at 19 30 01_91feb399](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/assets/123000600/2bd0c571-5896-4f8e-86a6-d1bc46cc9483)

Can someone please suggest where I am making a mistake?

comments.author: quantum8
comments.body:
Is the error continuously being transmitted? I.E.: is the time for the error message incrementing, or is it once off. If once off then you should be able to clear the message window and it won't return.

If it does then you either have a hardware or software configuration issue.

A Bit error is:
```When the bit received is not the same as the bit transmitted. Every node that receives the data reads it bit-by-bit from the Bus and compares the transmitted bit value with the received. If the bit transmitted and the bit received are not of the same value, a "bit error" occurs.```

My first thought for hardware issue is perhaps you haven't terminated correctly. Is there termination at both ends of the bus? You need 120ohm at both ends, not just one end. Sometimes you can get away with no termination, or just one but that's usually on a short cable run at low transmission rates, and certainly not in production.

comments.author: gxurma
comments.body:
I  sometimes get similar errors, when i think that the device communicates with a for example  1Mbit/s but it really is only doing 500kbit/s.

Have a look at the lines with an oscilloscope, an check the bit speed there. Also the signal quality.

 

At least you already have at least 1 message seen 😃

 

Just also check the datasheet and manual of your device, that you want to talk to.

And set it first to a slow speed like 250k or 500k.

 

Bye,

Martin

title: Novo Bus Analyzer
author: reymor
body:
Hi everyone,

I have forked the BUSMASTER project and I named **Novo Bus Analyzer** . I truly believe in open source and I think that BUSMASTER is a really good idea. There are not many options in this field and I think that it is worthwhile trying to keep a project like this. 

The first thing that I did was to compile the documentation which I post here: 

[BUSMASTER documentation](https://reymor.github.io/documentation/2023/12/06/busmaster-docs.html)

The other thing that I did was to compile the project from source code, by now I think that I had the development environment. 

I would like to say also that I am not a GUI developer. In addition, my area of expertise is not exactly these kind of programs. However, I would like to start this project and to see what happens. 

Trying to compile the documentation and the project itself I had the opportunity to read some code and trying to understand the real state of the project. My initial thoughts is that BUSMASTER is not a easy project as maintainer. I face a problem with the compilation process and it was kind of difficult trying to figure out where was the problem. 

As I said, I truly believe in the open source community. I think that the effort done in BUSMASTER was amazing and I would like to continue this with Novo Bus Analyzer. I have some ideas to improve and add new features, some of them are difficult like porting to Linux. However, I will start fixing some bugs that were reported and in that way trying to understand more the project. This will take me time. I will try to read all the issues that are open here and in some cases try to fix them. If there is somebody which would like to join me in this adventure I would appreciate that. Also, if you think that there is any feature which would be something interesting to add just open an issue and I would try to address the implementation.

[Novo Bus Analyzer](https://github.com/reymor/novobusmaster)

Best regards,

comments.author: rusoku
comments.body:
I spent a lot of time on the source code of this project. I developed several CAN and LIN drivers for the BUSMASTER program and TouCAN Rusoku adapters. And in my opinion it is better to make similar software again from zero but on more modern GUI framework base like Qt or similar.It also would work under windows, linux, macos.
IMHO

comments.author: reymor
comments.body:
Hello @rusoku 

Thanks for you feedback I really appreciate that. I saw that your post on these adapters. I would say that first I thought exactly the same. I mean I am not an expert on this things. However, I have spent time on really big projects like linux kernel, android source code, etc. When I saw the code and the dependencies I though maybe it is better to write this from scratch having in mind the cross-platform. 

As I said this was my first idea and I was not really sure about that. So, I just decide to start this journey and to understand more the product. 

I think that the one of the first thing is trying to change the build system from Visual Studio to some cross platform build system like cmake. There are modules that seems to have that support. In addition, there are modules which are written using Qt. However, there are more written using MFC. 

The reason that I choose to migrate the project to some modern GUI instead of writing one from scratch is that this project seems to have some community. There are people that use this software like you point out that you add support for new hardware. With this in mind I thought that maybe makes sense trying to migrate the project but in the road we could try to fix some bugs and add new support for new hardware. The ideas behind the modular approach does not seem really odd and to me it seems that they started this migration. I saw this paper [BUSMASTER – An Open Source Tool](https://uwispace.sta.uwi.edu/server/api/core/bitstreams/aaf02ee1-bc1d-4046-9d51-152e0ffb9b9f/content) and it seems that there was progress in this direction of cross-platform, but it seems that they lost the support or maybe they conclude that it was better to write a new software from scratch and start selling it XD.

I'm with you when you said that it seems better to start a project from scratch. However, I saw that I could start the migration from MFC to Qt and in the mid-time they can live together as it is right now. Regarding this migration, I saw the roadmap as: 
 
- Migrate from Visual Studio to some cross plataform build system (e.g cmake)
- Migration from MFC to Qt
- Clean the project to be able to compile in Linux/Windows

These are really difficult tasks. These are not easy things. The idea in the middle is trying to fix and add support to new hardware. I'm not really sure about this, and maybe I lost my path in the middle it is something that I'm going to see. Also, that's the reason that the feedback that you tell me it is really important for me and I'm trying to keep everything in my mind. 

If you are open to share your work, I will be happy to add and start creating the first realese of Novo BUSMASTER with the HW that you added support :). Also, you could open a merge request in Novo BUSMASTER

comments.author: gxurma
comments.body:
Hi Guys!
As a user of Busmaster, hacking some CAN bus, I recommend following:

Start from scratch. too many hidden things that hinder correct function. For example, I always struggled with setting up the filtering functions. Sometimes it worked, manly not, sometimes it worked when I did not want it etc. 

Use Python. That´s usable on all platforms.
Today PyQt5 would be good to use. No need to deal with all these build craziness. The moment you decide to use any build system, there pop up 10 new and you are immediately outdated.
Designer for the Qt design, and any text editor for the python part. Visual code is good enough.

Use a hardware abstraction layer, where it gets to the communication to the drivers. That makes it extendable.

I recommend also writing the DBC editor from new.

I was happy to have Busmaster as a tool when i needed it, for example the node simulation was meant good, but gets strange when having more nodes.
I recommend to use here python too.

actually it is a pity that busmaster was abandoned since 2017.

Yours sincerely,
Martin

In fact, I have started to develop a similar open source program based on Qt6 and C++ and I would like to support more protocols like CANopen, VSCP, D-PDU  etc...

comments.author: arihannu
comments.body: Hi, our company based in Germany is going most likely to start fixing some of the issues we have with Busmaster, so we are looking at this possibility, but we are not sure should we also upstream our changes here. But since this project has nobody to merge the pull requests, is there any point to upstream? License is GPL, so that states that any changes made should be contributed back here. Unless I misunderstood.

comments.author: vguttmann
comments.body:
@arihannu So this is licensed under the GPLv3, so as long as you fulfill a couple of requirements, you're good to go.
First, no sublicensing (e.g. changing to a more prohibitive license).

What you're referring to is the clause on modifications. You're good regarding that as long as you make the code of your fork openly accessible (since the upstream project can't be required to merge your contributions). Just fork the project on GitHub, and keep access open, and that should be it.

If you plan to distribute binaries based on this software (or your fork), you need to make the full source code available to anyone who asks.

If you create a consumer device based on GPLv3 code, you also need to provide instructions on installing and updating said software.

But generally, there shouldn't be anything in the GPL (be that v2 or v3) that would stop you from forking a project, and counting to develop it, as long as you keep source code open.

comments.body:
> Hello guys, I had a fork of [BusMaster](https://github.com/reymor/novobusanalyzer)
> 
> Feel free to send a pull request. In general, I move the build system to cmake and I was trying to mix qt with mfc. In general, it has the same base with some changes and a new drive from TouCAN. I face some problem regarding the qt and mfc integration and I was thinking that maybe it was better to rewrite some core system or just continue with mfc

Are you planning to rewrite the UI in Qt?

comments.author: reymor
comments.body:
That was the idea. Trying to support cross-platform and I thought that maybe Qt was a good choice. It's true that I got into some problems like:

- The database manager was close source from version 3.x onwards and there are issues with that and I cannot solve them. I was trying to contact ETAS/Bosch/Old maintainers and I got answer from old maintainers but they don't have access to the source code anymore. So, here the only solution that I saw is to rewrite the component.
- There are other close source components like using the peakcan close drivers
- In general the source code is a bit messy because in some point they started to rewrite from a monolithic application to a more modular approach and it seems to me that they didn't have the time to finish the job.

So, in the meantime my goals changed like thinking: ok, maybe it is not a bad idea to rewrite from scratch or just to maintain in MFC and keep functionality. Also, UDS functionality is not clever and a bit messy.

In meantime, I discover this other project ([ecu-bus](https://github.com/ecubus/EcuBus-Pro)) which seems pretty cool and I was trying to maybe work together with that project, at the end both are trying to the same provide the functionality that is provided by expensive can tools. The 'problem' with that project is that is written in electron which i don't have experience with

title: Suggest engage the Home Assistant community to encurage an integration for BUSMASTER and other CAN Bus OBD-II/OBD2 adapters
author: Hedda
body:
I would like to suggest and recommend that a BUSMASTER developer(s) try to engage with the Home Assistant community to encourage a Home Automation "integration" (also referred to as "integration component" and "custom component") for BUSMASTER protocol/API specifically and/or a generic integration for CAN Bus OBD-II/OBD2 (OBD/EOBD+CANbus) adapters which supports the same APIs and protocols for third-party integrations.

Check out feature request discussion -> https://community.home-assistant.io/t/support-for-can-bus-peak-ixxat-canable-etc/249639/

Home Assistant is an open-source home automation software application and is currently the world's most popular platform for home automation enthusiasts, primarily because it makes it relatively easy for developers to make third-party integrations for it so it, therefore, has more third-party integrations than other home automation framework, and it is also one of the most active Python-based projects on GitHub.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Home_Assistant

https://github.com/home-assistant/

https://www.home-assistant.io/

Home Assistant community members have requested support for different OBD-II/OBD2 adapters for a long time in order to both use it as a device tracker and/or presence detection of their cars and trucks (when it connects you to your Wi-Fi at home) as well as get data information about things like battery and fuel level status which is useful in home automation use cases like sending status notifications when the car/truck arrive at home or when battery or fuel is low.

See example:

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/development_index/

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/dev_101_services/

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/creating_component_index/

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=e3VwPb72Bbg

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6LHeoUS1R40

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_yVzgrGt1ok

Here are by the way some somewhat similar sensor integrations for HA, most of these only offer "sensors" and "binary sensors" but some of these have offered some type of device tracker and/or presence detection as well:

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/#car

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/torque/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/bmw_connected_drive/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/dacia/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/mazda/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/nissan_leaf/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/subaru/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/volvooncall/

https://brands.home-assistant.io/_/nsw_fuel_station/logo.png

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/openevse/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/juicenet/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/traccar/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/tractive/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/owntracks/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/fleetgo/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/mqtt_room/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/device_sun_light_trigger/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/asuswrt/

There are also more integrations for other non-car related bus protocols which present sensors and binary sensor, etc. entities to Home Assistant, ex:

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/velbus/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/ubus/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/modbus/

Personally, I would love to have a new integration which combines sensors, binary sensors, and device tracker and/or presence detection (person tracking) for knowing vehicles location prioritized if there is  developer interest in that:

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/device_tracker/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/#presence-detection

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/person/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/proximity/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/ping/

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/zone/

title: Test Automation Executor no Response on Execution
author: dietWall
body:
We are trying to implement an automated test for a custom CAN Board. 

As CAN Transceiver we are using the innomaker usb2can with the canary patch.

After defining a test with the steps Send/Wait/Verify we are trying to run the test once. The process we are using is: 
 - Go to Test Automation Executor
 - Load Testfile (Generated with Test Automation Editor)
 - Right Click to Test Suite and press execute
 

The result is: The test Step Table is filled, and no Result is presented. (See attached file). We also don´t see any messages arriving at hardware, despite the fact that all messages sent with via Transmit Window are arriving and our hardware responds correctly to them.

Expected result is: The test is executed and result are presented in UI.

![2022-08-25 08_37_37-Window](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47270180/186592784-45823b44-4aba-4014-b870-341aea56247f.png)



This would be a great help for us and spare many hours for test automation.
body_text:

Expected result is: The test is executed and result are presented in UI.

![2022-08-25 08_37_37-Window](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47270180/186592784-45823b44-4aba-4014-b870-341aea56247f.png)



This would be a great help for us and spare many hours for test automation.

title: How to connect a ManCats T200 labeled I-view Bosch interface without USB?
author: brainbubblersbest
body:
Hello. In my Company we have a few hundred outdated Bosch I-view Interfaces. These Days produced by Teradyne Systems which was buyed by Bosch in like 2014.

These Interfaces have already an internal 2Channel Can Logger configurable over the web interface.
Since these interfaces don't have USB and only Wlan and LAN interfaces, I wonder how these can be connect to busmaster? I don't find any MVCI Connection Manager or anything else on the Bosch Website.

Can anyone explain if this is Possible?
This would be a nice no cost feature for enthusiastic Electric  Service Employees since MAN Truck and Bus doesn't see the need for a can analyzer in the Service Stations.

But in reality lot of my colleagues would find sense for such a tool . I would share this in official over Diagnostic expert Groups even if my Company don't want that.
I 100% Support free Information and the Open Source mentality.
![Screenshot_20220811_110657_com android chrome](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48190425/184454660-f6b3b48e-1e5b-4305-abc4-16148c91e0e7.jpg)
![IMG_20220811_110906](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48190425/184454725-f0df6c50-7ff1-4346-9d51-a1beb4589feb.jpg)

title: Debug Assertion Failed
author: jxtony
body:
Hello,
If I open Release\Busmaster.exe(3.2.2) to select driver CAN_PEAK_USB, busmaster works fine.
If I open Debug\Busmaster.exe(3.2.2) to select driver CAN_PEAK_USB, it throws "Debug Assertion Failed“ (wincore.cpp LINE: 288)
I tried to debug, but I couldn't find the root cause, I only find that the debug failure comes out after below sentence in MainFrm.cpp.

`_if(S_FALSE == g_pouDIL_CAN_Interface->DILC_SetHardwareChannel(m_asControllerDetails,m_dwDriverId, false))_`

title: Scale factor not working (anymore!?)
author: Gosa7777
body:
I have been using Busmaster 3.2.2 for years now and the scaling of signals was fine. It seems now that scale factors below 1 are not working at all anymore. 
I pulled some older dbf files which I know worked, but they also produce no scaling, not in transmit nor in receive.

Any ideas?
body_text:

comments.author: Gosa7777
comments.body:
I got a sudden insight: I changed the decimal symbol from . to , some time ago in the WIN10 regional settings. It seems that this causes Busmaster to not correctly consider decimal points anymore (I tried outting a coma, but it does not accept it. 
Changing back to US regional setting with dot as decimal separator fixed the issue

title: Replay not respecting recording timings despite configuration
author: marinaalice
body: Hello, I have a recorded file from BUSMASTER that I want to replay. The time stamps imply a certain duration, as well as a simulation video that recorded the session. My replay is three time slower then expected. Attached my configuration. Thank you for any explanation you may provide. Best regards, Marina
body_text: Hello, I have a recorded file from BUSMASTER that I want to replay. The time stamps imply a certain duration, as well as a simulation video that recorded the session. My replay is three time slower then expected. Attached my configuration. Thank you for any explanation you may provide. Best regards, Marina

comments.author: TaekGu-Lee
comments.body:
Hello.

I too have the same issue.
Logged messages are sent 3x slower on replays.
If you replay the 20ms cycle CAN message recorded with BUSMASTER, it will be transmitted every 60ms.

It is the same whether using PEAK CAN or running simulation mode.

thank you.

ex) Log file : period 20ms => Replay every 60ms.
*Log file
15:41:10:5179 Rx 1 0x387 s 6 00 00 00 00 00 00
15:41:10:5378 Rx 1 0x387 s 6 00 00 00 00 00 00

*Message Window
00: 00 : 00: 0568 Tx 1 s 0x387 WHL_PUL11 6 00 00 00 00 00 00
00: 00 : 00: 0625 Tx 1 s 0x387 WHL_PUL11 6 00 00 00 00 00 00

comments.author: ghidoli
comments.body:
Hello,
tested in 3.2.2 and in 2.6.0. Replay do not respect timestamp in "log" file.
In my case, in simulation mode, I got a 2.31 multiplier (in total duration time of an quite "low rate" log file)
Also using peak CAN USB I got similar results. 
I understand that windows is not a realtime OS... but I'm pretty sure it is possible to obtain more accurate replay time.

body:

i am using COM API function "SendCANMSg" in python and currently i am getting error 
"raise AttributeError("%s.%s" % (self._username_, attr))

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

import ctypes as ctypes
import struct as struct

import win32com.client

#myBusMaster.LoadConfiguration(CONF_FILE)

CanDataFD = ctypes.c_uint8 * 64

title: Busmaster does not work correctly. For example, when trying to send PGN=0xfeec, Type=Broadcast, From=0xfe, To=0xf3, DLC=18, Prio=7, Data=0102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f10112a, then Busmaster will create a TPCM with data bytes:
author: wsgq007
body:
Busmaster does not work correctly. For example, when trying to send PGN=0xfeec, Type=Broadcast, From=0xfe, To=0xf3, DLC=18, Prio=7, Data=0102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f10112a, then Busmaster will create a TPCM with data bytes:

20 12 00 03 ff f3 fe 00

ie for a PGN fef3, instead of feec. Setting To=0xec will, as a workaround, give a correct TPCM, but then the TPDT will still be wrong and have an ID with DA=0xec. J1939/21 is pretty clear about what DA should be set to: "Global (DA = 255) for TP.CM.BAM data transfers"

_Originally posted by @astrand in https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/issues/916#issuecomment-230123400_
body_text:
Busmaster does not work correctly. For example, when trying to send PGN=0xfeec, Type=Broadcast, From=0xfe, To=0xf3, DLC=18, Prio=7, Data=0102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f10112a, then Busmaster will create a TPCM with data bytes:

20 12 00 03 ff f3 fe 00

ie for a PGN fef3, instead of feec. Setting To=0xec will, as a workaround, give a correct TPCM, but then the TPDT will still be wrong and have an ID with DA=0xec. J1939/21 is pretty clear about what DA should be set to: "Global (DA = 255) for TP.CM.BAM data transfers"

_Originally posted by @astrand in https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/issues/916#issuecomment-230123400_

body_text:

title: CAN diagnostic: Request diagnostic service difficult
author: EricXiaoke
body:
We always have to set the CAN diagnostic configuration.
Then send diagnostic.

I think if we can do as below, it will be powerful.
1. write the diagnostic service to excel according to specified format
2. busmaster loads the diagnostic excel
3. we can click the service like CANoe diagnostic console.
4. it can generate the auto test script like diva
Thanks.
body_text:
We always have to set the CAN diagnostic configuration.
Then send diagnostic.

I think if we can do as below, it will be powerful.
1. write the diagnostic service to excel according to specified format
2. busmaster loads the diagnostic excel
3. we can click the service like CANoe diagnostic console.
4. it can generate the auto test script like diva
Thanks.

comments.author: alesiem
comments.body: I fully aggree, could someone fix that please?!

comments.author: EricXiaoke
comments.body:
I tried to add the functions to the busmaster compile project.
However, it compiled failed.
So we can add our tester.cpp to Node Simulation to implement the functions
1.load excel(csv) in tester.cpp
  implement it in visual studio first, after it is ok, move it to tester.cpp
2.develop CAN diagnostic message send and receive component
  implement it in visual studio first
    use FreeRTOS queue to simulate CAN message send and receive
    implement tester and server tp layer(FF SF FC CF)
    implement tester and server uds layer
    load the diagnostic excel, tester send queue to server queue
  move the visual studio code to tester.cpp by replace send and receive interface  with queue
3.develop WinApi
   develop WinApi in visual studio first, then move it to tester.cpp
4.develop diva test
   make the test algorithm in visual studio, then move it to tester.cpp

title: Call to setSourceAddress not respected on J1939 (v3.2.2)
author: ghost
body: When making a call to setSourceAddress() on the id of a J1939_MSG in a J1939 node simulation it does not affect the CAN ID of the message transmitted on the bus. The actual transmitted message always have the source address that matches whatever was used when the node was added to the simulated system.
body_text: When making a call to setSourceAddress() on the id of a J1939_MSG in a J1939 node simulation it does not affect the CAN ID of the message transmitted on the bus. The actual transmitted message always have the source address that matches whatever was used when the node was added to the simulated system.

title: Interfacing Busmaster with CAN module like mcp2515 and arduino
author: krish97git
body:
Hey, anyone tried to interface the busmaster with CAN module like mcp2515 and arduino or anything similar?
If so what is the procedure?
body_text:
Hey, anyone tried to interface the busmaster with CAN module like mcp2515 and arduino or anything similar?
If so what is the procedure?

comments.author: yegorich
comments.body: You'll need for example a USB-to-CAN adapter attached to mcp2515. This way you can communincate with Arduino via a CAN bus.

comments.author: krish97git
comments.body:
Don't I need specific drivers for BUSMASTER to successfully run it using arduino and MCP2515
I dint get any drivers for that, so arises the question.

comments.author: yegorich
comments.body: AFAIK you cannot attach Arduino directly to BUSMASTER. So you if have an application that is using CAN on Arduino and Arduino interfaces with CAN through MCP2515 (SPI bus) and you want to use BUSMASTER to sniff on the CAN bus then you need an interface already supported by BUSMASTER. Like for example this [one](http://www.visionsystems.de/produkte/usb-can-plus-usb-can-plus-iso.html).

comments.author: viperidae
comments.body:
Is there any plans for adding support to connect to serial devices that use the slcan interface?
https://github.com/linux-can/can-utils
https://elinux.org/Bringing_CAN_interface_up#SLCAN_based_Interfaces

It's relatively straight forward to implement slcan on an arduino

comments.author: FrankGerlachFlex
comments.body: Did you try to delete the folder created by the installer ? I had the problem that it would not install after a partial package download. So I deleted everything and successfully re-ran the mingw installer.

comments.author: toowaki
comments.body:
Thank you for your reply.

Unfortunately, I cannot download MinGw at my office due to security.  So, the problem has not been solved yet.

But, busmaster seems to work without MinGw.
I understand that MinGw is used for node simulation.
If I don't use node simulation, is it okay if I don't install MinGw?

comments.author: FrankGerlachFlex
comments.body:
If you just want to monitor the CAN bus, I guess you will not need Mingw and the gcc/g++ compiler. 
But if you want to write your own Rest Bus Simulation (with emission, processing etc. of CAN messages) , you will need mingw to compile your C++ Simulation Code.

You should talk to your corporate security for an Exception. Or bring your personal laptop and do all the BUSMASTER work on that laptop.

PS: Or can you ask the IT guys to do the install for you ? Mingw and g++ and pretty much standard tools without any known security issues (except regular patches, as any complex software). g++ is widely used at Google, Facebook, Android and also major stock exchanges.

comments.author: toowaki
comments.body:
I understand what MinGw is used for.
I'll consult with my IT staff to install MinGw.

comments.author: yegorich
comments.body:
What do you want to achieve? Do you have a CAN program running on imx6 and you want to debug CAN communication or do you want to use imx6 board as a CAN-to-PC converter?

For the first case you need a CAN interface. It can be a PCI-to-CAN card, USB-to-CAN or network-to-CAN converter. Such interfaces are well supported by BUSMASTER (PEAK USB, Kvaser CAN or VScom CAN-API to name few).

Peter

For ES581.4 I did the following which gives occasional functionality on windows 10;
> Download and install HSP_11.13.0
> In HSP, Update ETAS ES581.4 firmware
> In HSP, Go to Drivers\ES581\ES581.4
> Install USB Driver
> Install 'legacy' BOA ED Driver
>Reboot

This works for me about in in 5 times of launching BUSMaster. I also use Vector VX16xx kit which always works fine, first time.

title: DatabaseEditor: Wrong "Max cannot be less than min" when editing signal
author: sthlm58
body:
`Steps to reproduce:`

It tried adding a signal to a message and edit it with Signal Details dialog:
* Type: unsigned int
* Length: 8 bits
* Min value: **B4**
  * Which is 180 in DECIMAL
* Max value: **EA**
  * Which is 234 in DECIMAL

Then I press **OK** to accept the dialog.

`Actual result (incorrect):` Popup with text: Maximum value cannot be less than minimum value!

`Expected result:` The values should be accepted

`Version tested:` v3.2.2

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2481102/55396232-b3400d80-5543-11e9-9f98-d9b365625672.png)

title: Sending Waveform Signals
author: duperoumat
body:
Hi,
I'm trying to debug a CAN Bus and I'd like to send a changing message on the bus (I'm trying to display a changing speed on a cluster).
I can send one message with no issues, but when I configure and enable a waveform, nothing is sent.
I configured the waveform, chose the right address from the database, started the connection, and enabled the waveform, but nothing happened.
Here is what the waveform looks like:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48526975/54297448-2b578b00-45b7-11e9-97ff-2374513b6848.png)
The 0x123 message is sent by the cluster to tell it's on and allows me to make sure it is connected.

Thanks for your help!

comments.author: ellis-me2
comments.body: Figured this out!  I had build tools from the yagarto project installed because they were required for a different project I was working on.  I uninstalled those tools and then was able to build the Busmaster project.  Note that I had the TDM GCC path before the yagarto tool in my path variable but that didn't matter - I had to actually uninstall the yagarto tools to get this to work.

title: Silent Uninstall of 3.2.2 not working
author: ostpol
body:
Hi,
I use PowerShell scripts to deploy Busmaster via SCCM.

I run a silent uninstall of Busmaster 3.0.0 with:
`Start-Process "C:\Program Files (x86)\BUSMASTER_v3.0.0\uninst.exe" -ArgumentList "/S" -Wait -PassThru`
Works as intended.

When I try to silently uninstall 3.2.2 with:
`Start-Process "C:\Program Files (x86)\BUSMASTER_v3.2.2\uninst.exe" -ArgumentList "/S" -Wait -PassThru`
nothing happens.

If I omit "/S" the uninstaller starts in interactive mode. Nullsoft documentation also says "/S". Any ideas on this? I need a way to uninstall the application without user interaction.

Thanks!

title: Message handler not working.

1. I wanted this busmaster to work with our product using USB.
2. Integrated my USB project in busmaster.
3. Wrote a some tx/rx dummy script by creating the CAN node.

             1. I opened two busmaster on my PC.
             2. One busmaster loaded with one script and other busmaster loaded with another script.

                 respective messsage handler also getting executed from script.

                 window but message handler not getting executed.

Any help or suggestion will be appreciated. Thanks!

title: Node simulation issue (busmaster v3.2.2)
author: ciarcode
body:
I have a problem with the node simulation of busmaster. I follow the normal procedure for the generation of a new CAN node:

1. I've written the node in C in the editor of busmaster
2. I've built the node (the built generates successfully the .dll (but it does not generate the .def)
3. Since the .def was not generated automatically, I write by myself the DEF file.
4. Click on "connect", but at this point the error: "Unable to initialise handler functions for execution. The DLL  ......... is unloaded".

But, I do not understand how I am supposed to load this DLL. I am not able to find any "load" button. It does not exist! I already tried to build the node after the (manually) creation of the DEF file, and the build is always ok, but once I click on CONNECT, the same issue appears.

title: Values in Message Window not smoothly updating
author: jaydeetech
body:
Hi, Using interface hardware  'Kvaser Pro' or 'PEAK P-CAN USB'  or 'PEAK P-CAN USB Pro'   and WIN 10 with Busmaster 3.2.2.
Once connected, the display of data in the message window is quite erratic.
If I move between various screen and menu options I can get something displayed to show in the message window but the values do not smoothly live update.    
The Statistics windows shows the units to receiving data just fine. 

However, if you move the windows vertical scroll bar the values will momentarily update, then stop. It appears the data is just not getting written to the window correctly.
Is this a known issue and is there any work around? 
Thanks, J.

title: Wrong signal description in DBF to DBC conversion.
author: mauro-ongaro
body:
I noticed that in DBF to DBC conversion we have that a signal description as:
[START_SIGNALS] DSB_Language,3,4,2,U,4,0,0,0.000000,1.000000,,
[VALUE_DESCRIPTION] English,0
[VALUE_DESCRIPTION] Italian,1
[VALUE_DESCRIPTION] Spanish,2
[VALUE_DESCRIPTION] French,3
[VALUE_DESCRIPTION] German,4
Is translated into:
 VAL_ 544 DSB_Language 0 "German,4" 0 "French,3" 0 "Spanish,2" 0 "Italian,1" 0 "English,0" ;
Instead of:
 VAL_ 544 DSB_Language 4 "German" 3 "French" 2 "Spanish" 1 "Italian" 0 "English" ;

I found some messages with a particular IDs logged on my log file. This ID is 0x2001 which is not supposed to be in the communication. Searching the Internet I found that it is the way Busmaster uses to log errorframes (akc error).

Moreover I found that Busmaster uses a similar way to log othe error.

I would like to know where I can find any exahustive documentation about the format used for logging error frame. I searched help.pdf file whitout finding any entry, hence I would like to know if there exists any other specific document.

title: Convert *.ACS to *.LOG incorrect if source log-file have CAN-id names from CAN-message database
author: dskibarko
body:
1) CAN-messages database is associate to project
2) Source *.asc have mixed ID-frames - Value and symbol names flrom database. 
For example:
     0.037019 1  MMI_BRAKE_CANHS_RNr_01              Rx   d 4 34 00 00 08  Length = 160000 BitCount = 84 ID = 1638
   0.037629 1  **657**                                 Rx   d 2 C0 C0  Length = 124000 BitCount = 66 ID = 1623
   0.041052 1  EMM_CANHS_R_01                      Rx   d 8 82 00 FF FF 09 00 00 C4  Length = 232000 BitCount = 120 ID = 529
   0.041232 1  EMM_CANHS_R_02                      Rx   d 2 00 00  Length = 130000 BitCount = 69 ID = 532
   0.041386 1  SSCU_CANHS_R_02                     Rx   d 1 98  Length = 104000 BitCount = 56 ID = 536
   0.041564 1  MMI_SSCU_CANHS_R_02                 Rx   d 2 08 00  Length = 128000 BitCount = 68 ID = 1274
   0.041720 1  MMI_EMM_CANHS_R_01                  Rx   d 1 02  Length = 106000 BitCount = 57 ID = 1649
   0.042720 1  **45C**                                 Rx   d 6 00 00 00 FE 00 00  Length = 204000 BitCount = 106 ID = 1116
   0.042938 1  BCM_CANHS_R_09                      Rx   d 4 FF 94 FF C4  Length = 158000 BitCount = 83 ID = 432
3) Message "Conversion is OK" appear but only messages 0x657 and 0x45C (were no symbol names of CAN-ID in ASC-log) present in result LOG-file after conversion

title: Untrustable timer in node simulation
author: GuillaumeHM
body:
I have a node script which uses a 100ms timer. I use this timer to count seconds based on count of timer handler call. So I consider one second is reached when timer handler has been called 10 times.

But, I can see a average derivation about 10% at each handler call (average time between calls is almost 110ms). So it is hours of derivation after days running. I didn't had this problem before, or at least not with this magnitude. I have a VLC instance running on the PC which I didn't had before so I suspect CPU charge to influence the magnitude.

I solved the problem by implementing a real timer inside my handler (the handler is now only a frequently running function which checks my own timer, 100ms check frequency is good enough for me).

Can you check Busmaster timer implementation and see if it is reloaded from last ended value or a kind of *now time* ? Because in the second case *now time* could include the handler execution time and *next end time* would be *100ms + handler exec time*.  This could explain the behavior regarding windows scheduler and VLC concurrent process.

Busmaster 3.2.0 and 3.2.2
body_text:
I have a node script which uses a 100ms timer. I use this timer to count seconds based on count of timer handler call. So I consider one second is reached when timer handler has been called 10 times.

But, I can see a average derivation about 10% at each handler call (average time between calls is almost 110ms). So it is hours of derivation after days running. I didn't had this problem before, or at least not with this magnitude. I have a VLC instance running on the PC which I didn't had before so I suspect CPU charge to influence the magnitude.

I solved the problem by implementing a real timer inside my handler (the handler is now only a frequently running function which checks my own timer, 100ms check frequency is good enough for me).

Can you check Busmaster timer implementation and see if it is reloaded from last ended value or a kind of *now time* ? Because in the second case *now time* could include the handler execution time and *next end time* would be *100ms + handler exec time*.  This could explain the behavior regarding windows scheduler and VLC concurrent process.

Busmaster 3.2.0 and 3.2.2

comments.author: Nit2Nat
comments.body:
@jogo- The Busmaster development team’s “wish-list” for usability & functionality features keep getting bigger based on constant feedback from the users. :)

Since we haven’t had enough helping hands via code contributions, implementing a lot of them with a small team was quite an effort. Hence the focus was to prioritize stability, fix any major bugs and try to implement the less effort-intensive features over a period of time.

comments.author: Nit2Nat
comments.body:
With the release of v3.2.x, idea was to ensure stability on Win10 and the plug-in architecture that allows users to create plug-in extensions to use with open source tool. This is the reason for no new releases since v3.2.2, but the project is very much alive :)

And with contributions from keen community developers, it would be possible to implement the wish-list much faster.

title: Wrong timing for TX Messages on CAN
author: rickyi89
body:
All the 3.x versions seems to be affected by a timing issue for what concerns TX messages, even in simulation mode. If I set a periodic message on CAN, for example 2000ms, it is delayed by a 1.45 factor, which means that the message is sent about every 2.9s instead 2s (5.7s if period is 4s, 145ms if period is 100ms and so on). I've found this issue in all our company computers, all running Windows 10.
RX messages show correct timings.

comments.author: GuillaumeHM
comments.body:
Busmaster is very sensible to desynchronization on multiple subjects :
1. PATH variable to select the right mingw compiler if multiple installed.
2. 32/64 bits version of windows/busmaster/mingw
3. Database cache files at C:\ProgramData\BUSMASTER\General\DBCache. They are generated when you associate a dbf to your project. I remember that cache format was broken with a new version, can't remember which one exactly. So it must be regenerated if generated with a previous version.
4. node.cpp and node.def

I think your problem is around the fourth point. Try to use the Busmaster code editor to (re)declare (meaning resynchronize) your functions/events using the left side panel and don't forget to save def file with cpp one. Other files are generated from those two.

With hope to help you.

title: sort function broken outside of interpret mode
author: richClubb
body:
The sort function is broken when interpret mode is not set.

When interpret mode is not set the sort will sort the message list, but the underlying objects in the message window do not get sorted so there is a disconnection between the displayed sorted list of messages and their corresponding message.

I've tracked it down to line 3253 in MsgFrmtWnd.cpp where it checks strIsInterpret, and correspondingly sets the m_bInterPretMsg flag, which is used in the conditional on line 1699 in the same file. If the conditional is not set the list is sorted but the underlying message entries are not correspondingly sorted.

Unsure as to why this has happened, or how best to solve it. I'm not sure if the conditional on line 1699 is actually necessary. Will continue looking into the commits for the sort functionality

Detected in version 3.2.2

title: SetTimeout function not working
author: JimmyC1984
body:
Hi,

I try to use the SetTimeout API function in node simulation, however when I try to build the file the compiler tells me SetTimeout is not declared. 
Does anyone have the same issue?
Thank you!
body_text:
Hi,

I try to use the SetTimeout API function in node simulation, however when I try to build the file the compiler tells me SetTimeout is not declared. 
Does anyone have the same issue?
Thank you!

comments.author: nostar
comments.body:
There is no SetTimeout() function in the CAN node sim API.  Do you mean SetTimerVal()?
```
GCC_EXTERN /*extern "C"*/ BOOL GCC_EXPORT SetTimerVal(char*,UINT);
```

comments.author: JimmyC1984
comments.body:
Hi,

Sorry didn't mention it clearly, the SetTimeout API is in J1939 CAN application.
When I use the Node simulation, I insert the SetTimeout function and during the build, the compiler shows SetTimeout not declared.
Thank you!

comments.author: nostar
comments.body:
Ah, I see.  Looks like they typo'd the function name in the header file https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/blob/master/Sources/BUSMASTER/Application/SimulatedSystems/include/BMJ1939Defines.h

```
GCC_EXTERN UINT GCC_EXPORT SetTimout(BYTE, UINT);
```

If your comfortable with the Busmaster source, you can fix the typo in the header and rebuild LibWrapper_J1939.a and replace the installed version, using the batch script in /Sources/BUSMASTER/Application/SimulatedSystems/include/ .  Otherwise, you are outta luck untill they fix it.

title: How to add ethernet support ?
author: steffsommer
body:
Hello,

I would like to send CAN packets from a microcontroller to a worksation running busmaster via Ethernet, to be able to transmit can messages without using extra hardware. How should I start my approach ? 
I have already read the Design document, but still find it hard to find a starting Point. As far as I understand I have to create a new Controller Interface, at best by duplicating one of the existing ones (e.g. CAN_ETAS_BOA) and rewrite some of the code to create a socket-based communication with my microcontroller. 
Can someone offer me some hints at which parts of the code I should have a look at or are there other documents that describe the interface in a more detailed manner ?
body_text:
Hello,

I would like to send CAN packets from a microcontroller to a worksation running busmaster via Ethernet, to be able to transmit can messages without using extra hardware. How should I start my approach ? 
I have already read the Design document, but still find it hard to find a starting Point. As far as I understand I have to create a new Controller Interface, at best by duplicating one of the existing ones (e.g. CAN_ETAS_BOA) and rewrite some of the code to create a socket-based communication with my microcontroller. 
Can someone offer me some hints at which parts of the code I should have a look at or are there other documents that describe the interface in a more detailed manner ?

Created a Send Request CAN Frame in Test Automation executor when the case is executed using the Test suite executor, the data in the frame is changing dyanamically
for: in Dbf created a CAN Frame 0x740 and payload as Data00 8bits startbit 0 Byteindex 0 little endian, Data01 8bits startbit 0 Byteindex 1 little endian.... till Data07 8bits startbit0 Byteindex 7 little endian.
created a test case from Dbf file and data as 0x04 0x22 0xf1 0x86.
when the above test case is executed using Test suite executor the data in payload is changing dynamically across the different executions

comments.author: itiserik
comments.body:
+1 for me here. When I try to send something using the test automation executor it seems that it sums up the values of the payload and that is send as byte[0] in the can message.

Can you show us the code for this version (GPL...)?

comments.author: itiserik
comments.body:
Tried to debug somewhat into it:
In TestSuiteExecutorGUI/TSExecutionCAN.cpp:596 the function GetSignalList is called. On every invocation of the test this function returns (into signalList) another list of signals with a changed order. (The ascending pointer determines the order)

Observing the code it is assumed that signalList should have the same order as ouSendData.m_odSignalDataList

The function GetSignalList is probably defined in DBManager.dll on a private github somewhere.

I looked into TXWindow/CANTxMsgItem.cpp: 363 and there a sortByStartBit function is used to keep the order consistent. A trick like this should be performed on TSExecutionCAN.cpp...

title: Message window can't display any messages after reconnecting once open main diagnostic window

1. If never open Main Diagnostic Window after launch BUSMASTER, message window will display message normally when enable connecting, it's still ok when disable/ enable connecting repeatedly.

title: Cannot run simulated node.
author: ernieskaggs
body:
Busmaster 3.2.1
I can build a simulated node just fine, it says ".dll created successfully", but then when I try to connect the program hangs up indefinitely.

comments.author: ellis-me2
comments.body:
Hi Arthur,

Figured this out - at least for my system!  I had build tools from the yagarto project installed because they were required for a different project I was working on.  I uninstalled those tools and then was able to build the Busmaster project.  Note that I had the TDM GCC path before the yagarto tool in my path variable but that didn't matter - I had to actually uninstall the yagarto tools to get this to work.

Perhaps you'll be able to build as well if you uninstall all other build tools that might conflict with TDM.

Regards,
Matt

body:
BusMater 3.2.1
Windows 10

I have dbc file with
`BO_ 2158845952 ODIAction_CntrStack_LS: 8 RadioHead_LS
 SG_ ODIAC_FUCID : 15|8@0+ (1,0) [0|0] ""  SBZA_L_LS,FCM_LS,PDIM_LS,EOCM_F_LS,UPA_LS,BCM_LS,IPC_LS,ECC_LS,MSM_LS,FCP_LS,APA_LS,UHP_Low,ONSTAR_LS
 SG_ ODIAC_DspMID : 31|8@0+ (1,0) [0|0] ""  SBZA_L_LS,FCM_LS,PDIM_LS,EOCM_F_LS,UPA_LS,BCM_LS,IPC_LS,ECC_LS,MSM_LS,FCP_LS,APA_LS,UHP_Low,ONSTAR_LS
`
 I convert it to dbf
`[START_MSG] ODIAction_CntrStack_LS,11362304,8,5,0,X,RadioHead_LS
[START_SIGNALS] ODIAC_FUCID,8,2,0,U,255,0,0,0,1,,,SBZA_L_LS,FCM_LS,PDIM_LS,EOCM_F_LS,UPA_LS,BCM_LS,IPC_LS,ECC_LS,MSM_LS,FCP_LS,APA_LS,UHP_Low,ONSTAR_LS
`

Then BusMaster decode the message correctly

title: BUSMASTER: release process
author: yegorich
body:
So far BUSMASTER provides final releases only, i.e. without release candidates. This has a big drawback that the users cannot test it before the final release and report issues. Can the release process be altered to provide at least two or three release candidates?

Another issue is that git history only shows huge commits between releases. This makes the process of bug hunting almost impossible because you don't know the atomic changes of a file in question. You cannot use `git bisect` etc. Is it possible to provide all commits between the releases?
body_text:
So far BUSMASTER provides final releases only, i.e. without release candidates. This has a big drawback that the users cannot test it before the final release and report issues. Can the release process be altered to provide at least two or three release candidates?

Another issue is that git history only shows huge commits between releases. This makes the process of bug hunting almost impossible because you don't know the atomic changes of a file in question. You cannot use `git bisect` etc. Is it possible to provide all commits between the releases?

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body: It sounds good. Installer will be uploaded before release for the users to test and provide feedback from the next version onwards.

title: Test executor only sends messages on simulated channel (not on IXXAT VCI)
author: itiserik
body:
BUSMASTER version 3.2.1
Steps to reproduce:
-Setup a BUSMASTER connected to a simulated channel
-Associate with a .dbf (CAN_Database.dbf)
-Open Test Automation Editor with (SampleTestSetupFile.xml)
-Verify that DataBase Path points to the same CAN_Database.dbf
-Open Test Automation Executor
-Add SampleTestSetupFile.xml
-Execute
-Verify in message window that Tx message is send out.
-Disconnect from channel
-Switch driver to IXXAT VCI (set baudrate etc)
-Connect to channel
-Open Test Automation Executor
-Execute
-No message is transmitted!

Transmission from Transmit Window is still possible.

Do I miss something here?
Can someone try to reproduce this on a development environment?

Thanks in advance!
body_text:
BUSMASTER version 3.2.1
Steps to reproduce:
-Setup a BUSMASTER connected to a simulated channel
-Associate with a .dbf (CAN_Database.dbf)
-Open Test Automation Editor with (SampleTestSetupFile.xml)
-Verify that DataBase Path points to the same CAN_Database.dbf
-Open Test Automation Executor
-Add SampleTestSetupFile.xml
-Execute
-Verify in message window that Tx message is send out.
-Disconnect from channel
-Switch driver to IXXAT VCI (set baudrate etc)
-Connect to channel
-Open Test Automation Executor
-Execute
-No message is transmitted!

Transmission from Transmit Window is still possible.

Do I miss something here?
Can someone try to reproduce this on a development environment?

Thanks in advance!

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body:
Is it happening only when transmitted with development environment ? 
How is it with the installation ? Could you please check and update ?

comments.author: itiserik
comments.body:
Yeah I found the issue:
At Sources/BUSMASTER/CAN_IXXAT_VCI/ClientList.cpp:123 I needed to add:
pdwClientID = GetClient(pacClientName)->dwClientID;

Can you integrate this?

title: CAN Replay with Peak USB does not send any messages without loop
author: Dietrich87
body:
If I try to replay a CAN .log file with a PEAK USB interface, it does not send any message on the bus. Even if I connect some other logger to the CANBUS to check if there is something. If I choose the "simulation" it works fine, the Message Window shows the messages from the .log file.

I got it working using two PEAK USB interfaces. One is sending the messages, the other one is receiving. Even if I check the bus between the two interfaces with another device, all the messages from the .log file appear. With this configuratiuon it works fine, but I don't think it is intended wo work like this.

I was checking the documentation, but I still cannot find any reason for this behaviour.
body_text:
If I try to replay a CAN .log file with a PEAK USB interface, it does not send any message on the bus. Even if I connect some other logger to the CANBUS to check if there is something. If I choose the "simulation" it works fine, the Message Window shows the messages from the .log file.

I got it working using two PEAK USB interfaces. One is sending the messages, the other one is receiving. Even if I check the bus between the two interfaces with another device, all the messages from the .log file appear. With this configuratiuon it works fine, but I don't think it is intended wo work like this.

I was checking the documentation, but I still cannot find any reason for this behaviour.

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body:
Is there any filter applied to the configuration in which messages are not appearing from the log ? Replay is independent of the hardware used.

Please send us the configuration and the log file to check the issue.

there is no filter applied. Using the "Simulation" instead of the PEAK Hardware shows all the messages.

title: Question about BUSMASTER and RTR messages (in a *.dbf file)
author: pedro-caetano
body:
Using BUSMASTER 3.2.0 (win7 x64)


Procedure steps: 

1. Tools> CAN DBF Editor> Open>
 
2.  Load test.dbf

###########################
test.dbf:

```
//******************************BUSMASTER Messages and signals Database ******************************//

[DATABASE_VERSION] 1.3

[PROTOCOL] CAN

[BUSMASTER_VERSION] [3.2.0]

[NUMBER_OF_MESSAGES] 1

[START_MSG] request_data,512,1,0,0,R
[END_MSG]
```
###########################

3. Trace window output  >> "test.dbf Is Invalid DBF File"


Follow-up queries:

- Is this 'test.dbf' file invalid?
- When creating a message in the editor, the RTR type isn't supported by BUSMASTER's user-interface (only options are 'Standard' or 'Extended') but is it not supported at all by the application?  
(there are some references to RTR in 'MsgInterpretation.cpp' but I am unclear on what happens in DBManager.dll)

With the bugfix **CAN_MHS.dll** file Busmaster throws immediately an error "Auflistung der Hardwareschnittstellen fehlgeschlagen" (hardware interface listing failed) as soon as I pick the driver **MHS Tiny-CAN** from the list. Also the channel configuration box is not launched as it is with the original dll.
???

Where do I find this firmware version ?
After installation of TinyCan_431.exe I've found a folder tiny_can/TCanProg containing the program tcanprog.exe.  But once started there is no way to choose a firmware. The program gives me the following output:

**Konfigurationsdatei "flash.ini" geladen
Datei Liste "files.ini" Datenbank geladen
Konfigurationsdatei "flash.ini" geladen
Connecting... Ok
Verbindung zur Hardware hergestellt
Informationen zur Hardware: Tiny-CAN I Hardware-Revision: 1.0
   Programm: Tiny-CAN I (Version: 1.210)
Programmdatei "Tiny-CAN1.mhs" geladen
Informationen zur Datei:

Flash Speicher nicht geloescht
Flash Clear  ... Ok

But in LIN Transmit window there is no check box or something so we can select the header or response part to transmit. 
So i need help because i dont know how to transmit the slave response part from LIN transmit window after reception of Header part from Master (Actual ECU)
Can you please kindly give the solution for this problem as early as possible.
body_text:
I have new version of BUSMASTER i.e. BUSMASTER v3.2.0
I am trying to to transmit the slave response from BUSMASTER transmit window after reception of header part from master.
But in LIN Transmit window there is no check box or something so we can select the header or response part to transmit. 
So i need help because i dont know how to transmit the slave response part from LIN transmit window after reception of Header part from Master (Actual ECU)
Can you please kindly give the solution for this problem as early as possible.

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body:
To transmit Slave response for a header request, add message in 'LIN->Transmit Window' and select 'Message Type' as 'Slave'. Use 'Data Byte View' or 'Signal Details' view to update data bytes.

By default, 'Message Type' will be selected as 'Slave'.

title: Rx extended frames aren't displayed using NSI hardware
author: MelissaA74
body:
Hello,

I can't receive extended type messages with a MuxyBox2 from NSI. 

Description of the procedure:
1.	I open Busmaster
2.	I set the driver selection on NSI CAN-API 
3.	I select the two first channels.
4.	I set the extended acceptance filter mode for the two channels in the filter option of the channel configuration window. 
5.	I create a cyclic extended frame in the transmit window.
6.	I connect the hardware interface.
7.	The transmitted frames are well displayed in the Message Window but not the received frames (I precise that I have loopbacked the Muxybox2 channels to use only one PC). 
8.	The transmitted frames are counted in the Bus Statistics but not the received frames.

I am currently using the latest version of Busmaster 3.2. And I know that the Rx extended messages were well displayed in the 3.0 version.

Thank you in advance. 

Best regards,

Mélissa
body_text:
Hello,

I can't receive extended type messages with a MuxyBox2 from NSI. 

Description of the procedure:
1.	I open Busmaster
2.	I set the driver selection on NSI CAN-API 
3.	I select the two first channels.
4.	I set the extended acceptance filter mode for the two channels in the filter option of the channel configuration window. 
5.	I create a cyclic extended frame in the transmit window.
6.	I connect the hardware interface.
7.	The transmitted frames are well displayed in the Message Window but not the received frames (I precise that I have loopbacked the Muxybox2 channels to use only one PC). 
8.	The transmitted frames are counted in the Bus Statistics but not the received frames.

I am currently using the latest version of Busmaster 3.2. And I know that the Rx extended messages were well displayed in the 3.0 version.

Thank you in advance. 

Best regards,

Mélissa

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body:
Hello,
We assume the frames are sent in channel 1 and expected to receive in channel 2
Do you receive any error frames ?
What is the behavior with BusMaster v3.0.0 ?

comments.author: MelissaA74
comments.body:
Hello,

Thank for your answer. Yes indeed, the frames are sent from channel 1 and received  by channel 2. The behaviour can be observed from the v3.1.0, and the CAN Channel selection window enhancement that has been made.

I have finally resolved the issue. Because of the channel selection window enhancement, the call to nSetApplyConfiguration() function had been deleted in the CAN_NSI project.  I have also found some other bugs I will submit you soon in a pull request.

Mélissa

comments.author: jogo-
comments.body:
I don't see any waiting pull request for the above commit.
Let's hope these changes won't be lost/forgotten in next release...

body_text:

I work on a Japanese Workstation and the installer seems to detect that since the application's title is changed. However I am not given a choice as to the locale I want to use.

I haven't tested earlier versions.

Same issue remains at V3.2.1 and same workaround (removing the "JPN" folder) works.

Note that, this issue was once fixed before, but revived again. See:
https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/issues/476
https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/issues/629

comments.author: Nit2Nat
comments.body: It was retained since we were still evaluating Japanese language support. We have closed this topic and from the subsequent version, the JPN folder will not be part of the release. English text will be displayed for all OS languages.

comments.author: ghost
comments.body: Thank you for your feedback. I suppose , "no support for Japanese language" is much better than "not working at Japanese environment at all". I also support your decision.

comments.author: Nit2Nat
comments.body:
you are welcome and thank you for supporting the decision. Over last few years, we had feedback from quite a few users of Japanese OS and other language users that they are comfortable working with English text version rather than having issues/bugs associated with dedicated language versions. 

When we integrated Windows 10 x64 ENG support, it was planned to gradually discontinue other language versions.

title: Delay in updating data bytes when connected to LIN PEAK hardware in Windows 10
author: RBEI-busmaster
body:
1.Open BusMaster.
2.Select driver selection as peak and import ldf file in channel configuration.
3.Configure both  master and slave for one db message in Tx Window.
4.Connect LIN Network.
5.update data byte of slave message in connected mode and Check in Message Window.
6.The updated data is transmitted with a delay
body_text:
1.Open BusMaster.
2.Select driver selection as peak and import ldf file in channel configuration.
3.Configure both  master and slave for one db message in Tx Window.
4.Connect LIN Network.
5.update data byte of slave message in connected mode and Check in Message Window.
6.The updated data is transmitted with a delay

title: Busmaster 3.1.0 failed to recognise IXXAT CAN-to-USB V2 under VCI driver V4
author: natthapolvanasrivilai
body:
Busmaster 3.1.0 can only connect to the IXXAT USB-to-CAN V2 when VCI V3 is installed.
It shows "driver selection failed" when use with VCI V4.

link to VCI V4: https://www.ixxat.com/support/file-and-documents-download/drivers/vci-v4-driver
link to VCI V3: https://www.ixxat.com/support/file-and-documents-download/drivers/vci-v3-driver-download
body_text:
Busmaster 3.1.0 can only connect to the IXXAT USB-to-CAN V2 when VCI V3 is installed.
It shows "driver selection failed" when use with VCI V4.

link to VCI V4: https://www.ixxat.com/support/file-and-documents-download/drivers/vci-v4-driver
link to VCI V3: https://www.ixxat.com/support/file-and-documents-download/drivers/vci-v3-driver-download

comments.author: jbiefm
comments.body:
I have the same issue.
For compatibility issues with other code, I would prefer to use VCI V4.
Is there an idea whether and/or when this will be addressed, i.e. VCI V4 will be supported/recognized?

comments.author: bodegaard
comments.body:
My new Windows 10 machine will refuse to boot if there's unsigned drivers installed. Even getting an unsigned driver installed in the first place is a hastle. The problem reoccurs every time windows does an update and I've got to do a workaround every time to get the signature check re-disabled.

VCI V4 driver has a signed version, however VCI V3 does not. This means that I cannot use my USB-CAN Compact with busmaster without this work around.

I'm currently using Busmaster 3.2.2

comments.author: polyene
comments.body:
I'm also having this issue. My new Windows 10 PC with secure boot refuses to boot with the VCI3 driver installed. All other programs that I have that were designed for VCI3 seems to work fine with the VCI4 driver, so I don't know why BusMaster doesn't. 

This seems like a critical issue for anyone using the IXXAT interfaces, since you're sooner or later forced to upgrade to VCI4.

I'm also using Busmaster 3.2.2.

comments.author: bodegaard
comments.body:
I've found a work around for now! There's a specific version that works. see the faq.

"

> Q: Why the VCI V3.5.4.4543 does not run with the Windows 10?
> Please deactivate the Secure-Boot in the BIOS or use the "VCI V4.0.482.0 Attestation Signed" or use the VCI 3.5.2.4268.

"

[link](https://www.ixxat.com/support/faq-and-tips/driver-and-api/vci-driver-installation-faq)

comments.author: polyene
comments.body:
Thank you bodegaard, the workaround worked fine for me!

But I'm still hoping to see VCI4 support in the future to make the installation process easier.

title: Crash when selecting IntrepidCS NeoVI
author: joshanne
body:
Precondition: IntrepidCS NeoVI Fire Red w/ ICS vehicle spy and drivers already installed (tested+confirmed)
Action: Open Busmaster 3.1.0, select CAN--> Driver Selection --> IntrepidCS NeoVI.
Observed Behaviour: Application crashes, log files saved (and attached)
Expected Behaviour: Application does not crash, selects driver for ICS tool, and works.

[BUSMASTER_CRASH_LOG.zip](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/files/741234/BUSMASTER_CRASH_LOG.zip)
body_text:
Precondition: IntrepidCS NeoVI Fire Red w/ ICS vehicle spy and drivers already installed (tested+confirmed)
Action: Open Busmaster 3.1.0, select CAN--> Driver Selection --> IntrepidCS NeoVI.
Observed Behaviour: Application crashes, log files saved (and attached)
Expected Behaviour: Application does not crash, selects driver for ICS tool, and works.

[BUSMASTER_CRASH_LOG.zip](https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/files/741234/BUSMASTER_CRASH_LOG.zip)

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body:
BusMaster uses icsneo40.dll to communicate with IntrepidCS hardware.
Please provide the version of ‘icsneo40.dll’ installed in your PC, which will be available at either of these locations ‘C:\Windows\System32’ or ‘C:\Windows\SysWOW64’
 
To get the version, please right click on the .dll, select ‘Properties’ and go to ‘Details’ tab.

comments.author: joshanne
comments.body: The version listed in the DLL is 3.7.0.114.

comments.author: intilinux
comments.body: I have the same issue with icsneo40.dll version 3.8.0.105.

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body: Please check with v3.2.2 beta uploaded in the transient repository https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster-transient

comments.author: joshanne
comments.body:
Fantastic! I no longer have access to the NeoVi but do have access to ValueCAN3 (which exhibited the same crash) - I can confirm the application does not crash.

Of interest though, the reported serial number is 50000 out. My units are SN: #87XXX and Busmaster reports the SN as being 37XXX. ie. the serial number is correct in the final 4 digits, but the first digit is off. 

Functionally, I am unsure this will affect anything, but  just a heads up.

Cheers,

title: BTR calculation error
author: PPSXIN
body:
On the preview version like v3.0.0, when the Baud Rate is set to 125000 bps, the BTR0 will be 0x47 and BRP will be 8, it's ok to work.
but on the version v3.1.0, if the Baud rate is set to 125000 bps, by default the BTR0 will be 0x41 and BRP will be 2, it can't be connected with CAN bus. If these parameters are changed manually as the preview version, it's ok.

title: LIN LDF file path issues
author: nostar
body: The LDF path is saved as an absolute path in the Cluster Configuration.  The working directory of a Busmaster simulation should be considered where the cfx file resides, and all paths for other files should be relative to that directory.
body_text: The LDF path is saved as an absolute path in the Cluster Configuration.  The working directory of a Busmaster simulation should be considered where the cfx file resides, and all paths for other files should be relative to that directory.
