title: All hardware networks created by Busmaster for Peak hardware are never removed
author: nostar
body: Upon close of Busmaster or de-selecting the peak driver, or a channel, or whenever a Peak hardware network *should* be removed, it never is.
body_text: Upon close of Busmaster or de-selecting the peak driver, or a channel, or whenever a Peak hardware network *should* be removed, it never is.

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body:
Please let us know if the issue is with CAN Peak or LIN Peak. 
To de-activate Peak hardware for CAN, switch to Simulation.

comments.author: nostar
comments.body: This is with the CAN Peak.  Along with the virtual network that is created and never should be  #1018, all of the hardware networks created by Busmaster are never released, and can only be removed using the PCAN Nets Configuration tool that comes with the Peak Developers API toolkit.

title: Peak virtual network always created when Peak CAN driver selected and never removed
author: nostar
body: A virtual network is always created by Busmaster when the Peak driver is selected, and never removed.
body_text: A virtual network is always created by Busmaster when the Peak driver is selected, and never removed.

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body: Could you please provide more details, as it is not reproducible with v3.1.0.

comments.author: nostar
comments.body:
It's pretty obvious.  Open Busmaster, select Peak hardware.  A virtual network is created by busmaster before any peak hardware has been discovered.  This has been the case since Busmaster 1.0.

I recently spoke with Nithin on the phone, and he led me to believe that peak hardware was not available to anyone at ETAS.  If this is the case, then you cannot replicate this bug.

title: No ability to change hardware configuration on a single channel device
author: nostar
body: In 3.1 the 'Channel Selection' was renamed to 'Channel Configuration', and what used to be 'Channel Configuration' is now an advanced button on the channel selection dialog.  The problem is that the Channel Configuration button is not active for a single channel device, so now there is no way to change configuration settings, such as baud rate, on a single channel device.  This is true for Peak and Vector hardware, and IXXAT hardware according to #1016
body_text: In 3.1 the 'Channel Selection' was renamed to 'Channel Configuration', and what used to be 'Channel Configuration' is now an advanced button on the channel selection dialog.  The problem is that the Channel Configuration button is not active for a single channel device, so now there is no way to change configuration settings, such as baud rate, on a single channel device.  This is true for Peak and Vector hardware, and IXXAT hardware according to #1016

title: Trace window shows unexpected messages while enabling node simulation after disabling it
author: RBEI-busmaster
body:
1. Add a node and message hanlder to it And trace some messages inside the handler for LIN
2.Add the same frame and response in tx wnd
3.Transmit the messahe by disabling the nodes
4.Enable the nodes and and connect
5.Trace window is printing the trace messages which was added in the message handler

title: Issue with CAN Diagnostic Windows
author: longtranchau
body:
I have an issue with CAN Diagnostic Windows. It seems that Busmaster 3.x.x (both 3.0.0 and 3.1.0) can't handle multi-frame response from ECU. After ECU sent first frame, Busmaster didn't send flow control frame and the communication was cancelled.

![busmaster 3 0 0](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8691192/20910031/146c3b56-bb9a-11e6-9a45-6d3d89e42d80.PNG)

If I use Busmaster 2.6.4, it's ok. Busmaster 2.6.4 can handle multi frame response from ECU.

![busmaster 2 6 0](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8691192/20909988/c9f7d1a2-bb99-11e6-8d2a-e469303a31c5.PNG)

Regards,
Long Tran

title: Install error message on non-admin Windows machines
author: GR8DAN
body:
Is it possible for the installer to complete on a Windows machine where the user does not have machine admin rights without an error message being displayed..

At the moment the installer will try and put icsneo40.dll into C:\Windows\system32. This causes the installer to display an error message if the logged in user does not have machine admin rights, You can choose to ignore the messsage (twice) to complete the install. It would be better if an install option allowed icsneo40.dll to be installed only if it was required.

title: CAN Diagnostics Flow Control
author: ddeussen
body:
I am trying to pull trouble codes from an ECU through the CAN-Diagnostics function. It seems like the flow control is not working. The ECU responds that there are 11 bytes of data but Busmaster does not confirm the first received message through flow control so that the ECU can send the remaining data in a second message.

Also when saving the Busmaster configuration the CAN-Diagnostics-Settings are not saved. Every time I open the program I have to go through all the settings again.

I am using Busmaster 3.0 with an ES581.3 on a 250kbps CAN Bus.

title: Possible to add a close source plugin?
author: grandangelo
body:
Good morning,

I'm working for a test bench designer company. We have developed internally a CAN driver using a CAN port natively available on a uPC used in our devices.

I've been using Busmaster in my previous job and found it really useful. I would like to propose to my boss the use of this tool, but open sourcing any code unluckily does not seem an option.

Thus my question: is it possible to add support for a CAN hardware device without releasing the source code?

Many thanks for any answer or suggestion.
Andrea
body_text:
Good morning,

I'm working for a test bench designer company. We have developed internally a CAN driver using a CAN port natively available on a uPC used in our devices.

I've been using Busmaster in my previous job and found it really useful. I would like to propose to my boss the use of this tool, but open sourcing any code unluckily does not seem an option.

Thus my question: is it possible to add support for a CAN hardware device without releasing the source code?

Many thanks for any answer or suggestion.
Andrea

comments.author: Nit2Nat

Hi Andrea,

Greetings! The source code distribution depends on how the component (in this case, your custom HW support) integration & distribution is made. Perhaps you could write to us at BUSMASTER@in.bosch.com to understand the plan. Thanks

title: Database editor: how do I 'wildcard' message IDs to accept from all Source Addresses, Priorities etc.?
author: tomgreenflir
body:
I am looking at BM to see if it can help us with NMEA2000 field diagnosis. Perhaps I'm being dense but I cannot find out how I can create Database entries where Message ID can be 'wild-carded' to accept the same message from any Source Address or with any Priority. Essentially I think I need a mask, like in issue 414.

Example: I receive NMEA2000 PGN 127245 (0x1F10D) from address 0xCD with priority 2 (0x9F10DCD.) If I write my Database entry Message ID=0x9F10DCD, the message is recognised as Rudder type in the Message window, but this will only work if the message's Source Address (LS byte) is 0xCD and Priority=2. I don't want to have to write separate DB entries for all possible Source Addresses(!). Writing the DB MessageID=0x1F10D00, 0x1F10DFF, 0xFFF10DFF etc. all result in the message type not being recognised in traffic in the Message Window.

How can I write a DB entry so that the first 7 and last 8 bits of the Message ID are don't-care values?

Other than that, BM looks really powerful and I would love to be able to use it more.

I converted my CAN dbc to dbf using BUSMASTER converter tool.
When I assigned this dbf to BUSMASTER,it shows no message and signal in the busmaster. Eventhough the  dbf file is no blank.
please help.
body_text:
Hello,
I converted my CAN dbc to dbf using BUSMASTER converter tool.
When I assigned this dbf to BUSMASTER,it shows no message and signal in the busmaster. Eventhough the  dbf file is no blank.
please help.

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body:
To analyze the issue, please provide the below details:
1. BusMaster Version used 
2. Send .dbc file used to BUSMASTER@in.bosch.com

comments.author: whitefalk
comments.body:
Hi,
This may be the same as #838
I had a similar issue, and I opened the DBC in CANdb++ editor, and saved it, and then it worked.

This is not the solution though. There is some kind of name abbreviation scheme in dbc that is not considered by the converter tool. There is a list of all message names but later a trunkated name appears, sometimes with the ending _0000 and _0001 etc.. Sometime the name is cut.

So CANdb++ editor resolved this, when I saved it again it resolved all these, and the full name is used now. My 800 kB dbc grew to about 900kB.

I can't currently attach an example, because it was from some proprietary project.

UPDATE:
I checked several things. So we have a tool that generates DBC files for us, and I wanted to create a dbf from it.

I thought the issue was that the signal names were sometimes more than 32 characters long.. this is not the case.

However in the beginning of the file there is a listing:

NS_:
 NS_DESC_
 CM_
 BA_DEF_
 BA_
 VAL_
 CAT_DEF_
etc..

the issue in my case turned out to be that our generated file had a single space in front of NS_DESC_, CM_ etc.. and CANdb++ editor would use a single \t character. This difference causes the conversion to fail.

comments.author: jogo-
comments.body: @whitefalk just have a look at the PR #1184

title: GCCDLLMakeTemplate_CAN not found
author: ones1
body:
Hello BM community,

when I load my BM-config-file with VS, I get this message in the output window:
"C:\WINDOWS\system32\GCCDLLMakeTemplate_CAN was not found"

but when I use BM alone to load the config-file it works as it should.

does someone knows about this problem?
I already copied the file to my system32 folder without result.

I would be grateful about any hint!
body_text:
Hello BM community,

when I load my BM-config-file with VS, I get this message in the output window:
"C:\WINDOWS\system32\GCCDLLMakeTemplate_CAN was not found"

but when I use BM alone to load the config-file it works as it should.

does someone knows about this problem?
I already copied the file to my system32 folder without result.

I would be grateful about any hint!

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body:
Hi,
Please use the latest BusMaster v3.0.0. 
Please Copy the below folders/files to BUSMASTER\BIN\Debug or BUSMASTER\BIN\Release based on the configuration type(Debug/Release) used
1. BUSMASTER\Application\SimulatedSystems
2. BUSMASTER\Application\GCCDLLMakeTemplate

I'll try it out! 
Thank you!

title: Panel for Node Simulation needed
author: PeterSchmutt
body:
During Node Simulation, we need a panel/GUI to interact with the user (e.g. an Automotive Dasboard). We know such a feature from Vector CANalyzer pro. 
- Can such a feature be implemented in further versions?
- Is it already possible to attach for example a Visual Basic GUI by some SW interface?

comments.body: Duplicate to #84 and Panel support will be tracked using the same. Hence closing the issue.

title: J1939 Mux Ids are not handled.
author: Squall-DA
body: J1939 allows for the transmission of mux-id bytes so that one byte of the CAN message can contain different data based on the current mux-id. Currently if a muxed CAN message is received by BUSMASTER, signals are populated with data from different mux-ids.
body_text: J1939 allows for the transmission of mux-id bytes so that one byte of the CAN message can contain different data based on the current mux-id. Currently if a muxed CAN message is received by BUSMASTER, signals are populated with data from different mux-ids.

title: _Unwind_Resume undefined reference
author: alberto77
body:
Dear all,
I am trying to compile .cpp file for node simulation. I run Busmaster v3.0.0 and TDM gcc installed according to Busmaster documentation. I got the following error message:
- test.o:test.cpp <omissis> undefined reference to _Unwind_Resume.

Serching the Internet I found some issue about the use of latter gcc version, greater than 4.8.1. Unfortunately default TDM installation is bounded to gcc version 5.1.0. I unstalled TDM and modified the default installation set-up to force gcc 4.8.1 installation.

It was still not possible to compile, but I got different errors:
- undefined reference to __gxx_personality_v0;
- undefined reference to _Unwind_sjLj_Register;
- undefined reference to _Unwind_sjLj_Unregister;

Hence I tried with MinGw installation. Right now, latter version of MinGw is greater is 4.9.3 (I do not remember, I unstalled it because I had a lot of problem even in compilig trivial cpp files.

Eventually, I decided to restore original configuration installing TDM gcc with default installation setup and gcc path stored in PATH environment variable.

comments.body:
BusMaster v3.0.0 supports 'MinGW TDM1 GCC v4.8.1' available at https://sourceforge.net/projects/tdm-gcc/files/TDM-GCC%20Installer/Previous/1.1309.0/tdm-gcc-4.8.1.exe/download.

Please refer section ‘Additional Installation->MinGW Installation using TDM-GCC Installer’ in the user manual for the installation steps.

title: Busmaster .log to .asc conversion bug (timestamps)
author: CANberryy
body:
Hi,
I noticed another bug in the conversion of .log into .asc files with the implemented Konverter. 
The timestamps in the .log file don't match the timestamps in the .asc file, when exceeding 1 hour.
After every hour (3600s, 7200s, ...) in the .log file the konverted result is +60s ( 3660s, 7260s +60s = 7320s, ...)

I checked it with several .log-files, it's always the same. Doesn't matter if one large .log file or several parts.

Example:
.log-file:
...
00:59:59:9905 Rx 1 0x306 s 8 8E 2A C4 2A BD 2A A7 2A 
00:59:59:9907 Rx 1 0x307 s 8 CD 2A D7 2A BC 09 C6 09 
00:59:59:9919 Rx 1 0x390 s 8 BC 09 C6 09 00 00 BC E5 
01:00:00:0000 Rx 1 0x306 s 8 8F 2A C4 2A BD 2A A8 2A 
01:00:00:0002 Rx 1 0x307 s 8 CE 2A D7 2A C4 09 CD 09 
01:00:00:0014 Rx 1 0x390 s 8 C4 09 CD 09 00 00 C6 E5 
**_END DATE AND TIME 26:7:2016 18:25:22:312**_
**_[STOP LOGGING SESSION]**_

=> .asc-file:
date Tue Jul 26 5:25:22 pm 2016
base hex  timestamps absolute
no internal events logged
// version 7.1.0
...
3599.990500 1 306 Rx d 8 8E 2A C4 2A BD 2A A7 2A 
3599.990700 1 307 Rx d 8 CD 2A D7 2A BC 09 C6 09 
3599.991900 1 390 Rx d 8 BC 09 C6 09 00 00 BC E5 
3660.000000 1 306 Rx d 8 8F 2A C4 2A BD 2A A8 2A 
3660.000200 1 307 Rx d 8 CE 2A D7 2A C4 09 CD 09 
3660.001400 1 390 Rx d 8 C4 09 CD 09 00 00 C6 E5 
End TriggerBlock

comments.author: WillieShih
comments.body:
I have same problem.
I found the source code Log_Asc_Parser.y have error.

https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/blob/master/Sources/BUSMASTER/Format%20Converter/LogAscConverter/Log_Asc_Parser.y

The fourteenth row of the function nGetAscTimeStamp
original
unTotalSec = nHours_3660 + nMin \* 60 + nSec;
correct
unTotalSec = nHours_3600 + nMin \* 60 + nSec;

Please release the Patch update this issue.
Thanks

comments.author: CANberryy
comments.body: Yea that's it! Thank you very much!

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body: Fix is available as part of v3.1.0

comments.author: Nit2Nat
comments.body:
Busmaster is currently supported on Win 7 x64. Support for x64 versions of 8.1 and 10 are planned in subsequent releases. Do you get the same behavior even when run in Win7 compatibility mode ?

With 3.0 there was a major architectural over-haul.  This is currently done for LIN & CAN. We plan to extend the same to FlexRay functionalities in the future.

But in case, you would require any further information regarding FlexRay features and availability, please contact busmaster@in.bosch.com.

title: Message window interprets values wrong
author: schnurksel
body:
The Message window interprets the message values wrong while at the same moment the same value is interpreted inside the signal watch window right.
Example: The Raw value is 0x3E0 at the signal window and the message window.
The signal window interprets this value right as as -32 while the message window shows 992.
See attached image.
![busmaster](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6735897/15553818/34045010-22c1-11e6-9aa4-324e78521686.jpg)
The value is a 10 Bit signed value with offset 0. The message windows shows it as an unsigned value (1024 - 32 = 992).

title: Rapresentation of negative signed 16 int
author: riodda
body:
I did a database with a 2 bytes signed int with a factor of 0,01, if i send on the canbus the value -1.23*100=-123 as FF 88 on both cases little endian and big endian, busmaster shows me 654.130 instead of -1.23.
Now if i see the database the channel GPS_Roll is shown as 16 bit byte index 2 start bit 0 int, but in the mask is defined as byte index 1 !! Therfore i can't undestand if byte index in the masks starts from 0 and in the dbf starts from 1 or there is a discrepancy in how the byte index is handled or simply the rapresentation is wrong.

[START_MSG] GPS_Ahrs,772,8,4,1,S
[START_SIGNALS] GPS_Roll,16,2,0,I,32767,-32768,0,0.000000,0.010000,°,
[END_MSG]

title: support filtering based on CAN-ID and mask
author: teschi
body:
In the "_Configure Filter List_" window, under "_Filter Attributes_" provide:
- A filter based on a CAN-ID and a Mask
- maybe an invert checkbox

Explanation:
With 29-Bit CAN you often want to filter on specific ECU where you would display everything to and 
from an ECU. Something like display me everything with:
- ID=00002300, MASK=0000FF00
- or ID=00000023, MASK=000000FF

Or only want to see diagnosis, with
- ID=00DA0000, MASK=00FF0000

You cannot use ranges for this, when the lower bits are arbitrary
body_text:

Seams that replay fails when there is a zero byte length message is in your logfile.

title: Unable to Connect to PEAK PCAN - USB
author: BrosDs
body:
Hi all, I've recently brought a PEAK System PCAN-USB (the IPEH-002021 model available on the page http://www.peak-system.com/PCAN-USB.199.0.html?&L=1), installed all the drivers and so, but I cannot see anything from the BUSMASTER interface. The steps I've made are the following:
1. Turn on the car
2. Open BUSMASTER and select CAN > DRIVER SELECTION > PEAK USB
3. Click onto CAN > CONNECT

At this point I think I could see the all the traffic on the CANbus but it shows nothing.

Is something wrong with this?

Thanks
body_text:
Hi all, I've recently brought a PEAK System PCAN-USB (the IPEH-002021 model available on the page http://www.peak-system.com/PCAN-USB.199.0.html?&L=1), installed all the drivers and so, but I cannot see anything from the BUSMASTER interface. The steps I've made are the following:
1. Turn on the car
2. Open BUSMASTER and select CAN > DRIVER SELECTION > PEAK USB
3. Click onto CAN > CONNECT

At this point I think I could see the all the traffic on the CANbus but it shows nothing.

Is something wrong with this?

Thanks

comments.author: BrosDs
comments.body: Resolved: for some unknown reasons Busmaster wasn't recognizing the PCAN-USB as a device. A fresh installation resolved all the problems

title: Transmission of J1939 message
author: snehasethi
body:
Hi Team,

I am unable to get the broadcast option in the Msg type drop down in J1939 Tx Message window. Kindly let me know what needs to be done.
body_text:
Hi Team,

I am unable to get the broadcast option in the Msg type drop down in J1939 Tx Message window. Kindly let me know what needs to be done.

comments.author: BGSW-Srinibas
comments.body: message type will be determined by the PF Value in PGN. For  more details ,Please refer #205.

comments.author: astrand
comments.body:
Busmaster does not work correctly. For example, when trying to send PGN=0xfeec, Type=Broadcast, From=0xfe, To=0xf3, DLC=18, Prio=7, Data=0102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f10112a, then Busmaster will create a TPCM with data bytes:

20 12 00 03 ff f3 fe 00

ie for a PGN fef3, instead of feec. Setting To=0xec will, as a workaround, give a correct TPCM, but then the TPDT will still be wrong and have an ID with DA=0xec. J1939/21 is pretty clear about what DA should be set to: "Global (DA = 255) for TP.CM.BAM data transfers"

title: Busmaster Survey
author: Nit2Nat
body:
Hello all,

After release of Busmaster v2.6.4, the core development team now has focus on improving the database manager, back-end architecture & UX improvement

We would like your inputs to plan support for new protocols/Hardware and understand areas of improvement in the tool.

Requesting your valuable time to fill out the survey formulated to evaluate the tool. 
Total Questions: 10, Survey time: 5-8Mins (approx..)

https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/5YZDB3Y

Please feel free to provide us an honest feedback so that the development goes in-line with what the user-base demands. Kindly use v2.6.4 as a reference.

We would also love to have more users contributing source codes of any enhancements or new features developed to be made available on Github for the benefit of the larger community.

With considerable number of Busmaster veterans on the community, it would be great for us to have a helping hand in bug-fixing & testing on the community.

Please feel free to write to us at BUSMASTER@in.bosch.com if you wish to contribute code or support.

Thanks & Regards
Team Busmaster

title: LIN as Master Node
author: RegisStadel
body:
If I try to emulate a Master Node with BusMaster to send frames to my ECU, which is a Slave on the Bus, I cannot give any data (the data zone is greyed out in the Transmit window).
Why ?
body_text:
If I try to emulate a Master Node with BusMaster to send frames to my ECU, which is a Slave on the Bus, I cannot give any data (the data zone is greyed out in the Transmit window).
Why ?

comments.author: BGSW-Srinibas
comments.body:
Hello ,

Master frame contains only frame header .Hence Dlc and data bytes are disabled.
For more details please go through the LIN specification.

Thanks
Srinibas

title: Log file splitting count and bzip2 compression
author: gxurma
body:
One big reason why I started to use Busmaster was the possibility of endlessly log and split the logfile to 50MB chunks. In the new version I see some nice improvements to be able to determine the splitting. But I also see a maximum number of logfiles, which is limited to 999. That is not good. It would stop my logging after 1 week...

Can we please also make this limit switchable or at least to limit it to 999999?
I want to log endlessly.

It would be useful to add a bzip2 compression already to the application. (for our application it means a 1:10 ratio!)

title: Time format
author: AutologicAU
body:
Busmaster expresses time in the format hh:mm:ss:ms.

When exporting this to excel it is next to impossible to deal with

I suggest that within busmaster and for export that the internal time format should be hh:mm:ss.ms

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
To apply Filter on log, follow the below steps:
1. Configure filters using 'CAN->Filter Configuration'
2. Apply the filter to log using 'Configure Logging' window. 'Configure Logging' window can be launched via menu 'CAN->Logging->Configure'
3. Enable Filters for logging using 'CAN->Logging->Enable Filters'

Similar way apply the filters for Replay also. For more details please refer help 'CAN->Configuration->Log and Replay' and 'CAN->Enable/Disable Filter'

title: Busmaster wont start in CAN FD mode with ES523 consistently
author: nostar
body: Yesterday while trying to get Peak hardware to do CAN FD (see #885 ) I tried an ES523 and it did CAN FD great with 500k nominal and 2MB data.   It only worked after changing txDelayCompensation from OFF to ON.  Today I'm trying to start it in CAN-FD mode and it refuses to do so, it just keeps starting in standard CAN mode.
body_text: Yesterday while trying to get Peak hardware to do CAN FD (see #885 ) I tried an ES523 and it did CAN FD great with 500k nominal and 2MB data.   It only worked after changing txDelayCompensation from OFF to ON.  Today I'm trying to start it in CAN-FD mode and it refuses to do so, it just keeps starting in standard CAN mode.

comments.author: nostar
comments.body:
An ES523.1 with the older of the shown firmware versions works fine in CAN-FD mode, but the ES523.1 with the latest firmware will not start in CAN-FD mode.

![es523_old](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3847102/12096502/be484856-b2e3-11e5-8b19-18759fa965fb.png)

![es523_new](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3847102/12096509/c97bb94c-b2e3-11e5-93f2-552a19145b57.png)

comments.author: nostar
comments.body: If one level of firmware works correctly, and others do not (tried again with even newer firmware from INCA 7.2 with same results), how is this a support issue?

comments.author: nostar
comments.body: The problem is a matter of ISO vs Non-ISO CAN-FD modes.  The older firmware only supports Non-ISO and the newer firmware only supports ISO.  I'm told by ETAS that software switching is a feature coming soon to INCA, so I assume the feature will follow in Busmaster?

comments.author: nostar
comments.body:
As of INCA 7.1.10/1 The ability exists to select CAN FD Frame format of ISO or Non ISO.  I am only able to use the ES523 with Busmaster using the desired mode by opening INCA first, selecting the desired mode, and Initializing hardware and leaving INCA open.  Then I can open Busmaster and Connect to the ES523 in the desired mode.

The option to select the CAN FD Frame format needs to be added to the Busmaster BOA driver channel configuration.

title: Main Diagnostic Window not working
author: hfabian
body:
hello,

I want to send diagnostic frames with Busmaster in CAN.

In the Main Diagnostic Window I can not insert the Address for Source, Target and the CAN ID.
![2015-12-17 12_36_54-main diagnostic window](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/9262927/11868816/e8eb54c2-a4ba-11e5-92b2-54bc1dc8bf42.png)

How can I send Diagnostic Frames?
body_text:
hello,

I want to send diagnostic frames with Busmaster in CAN.

In the Main Diagnostic Window I can not insert the Address for Source, Target and the CAN ID.
![2015-12-17 12_36_54-main diagnostic window](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/9262927/11868816/e8eb54c2-a4ba-11e5-92b2-54bc1dc8bf42.png)

How can I send Diagnostic Frames?

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
Hello,
Please use 'CAN->Diagnostics->Settings' window to set 'Diagnostic Standard', 'Interface' and 'CAN ID' values. Source Address and Target Address fields in the 'Main Diagnostic Window' will be enabled only for '11bit Extended' and '29bit' interfaces specified in the 'Settings' window.

If the interface is selected as '11bit Normal', the source address will be taken from 'CAN ID Request to ECU' field in the 'Settings' window.
For more details, please refer help 'CAN->Diagnostics' section

title: Software error problem
author: SpacemanAJ
body:
Hello,

we installed the Version 2.6.3 on different kind of PC models and images.

The person who installed the software never get any problems but if other users logs into the PC the software starts but if you open Converter and will close it an error comes up.
This comes not on all machines but very often. 

So it looks like that if a systemaccount with rights is installing the SW everything works.
If a user installed the SW it works for him but sometime on some machines not for other user who will log on to this PC.

Is this known?
What can we check?

![error](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/13742731/11649679/e07657f0-9d81-11e5-87e3-1db1f030fb29.jpg)

OS: Windows 7 32 and 64 bit

title: Reading back its own Tx messages
author: WarnerS
body:
BUSMASTER seems to be reading back its own Tx messages, showing them also as Rx.
Showing the Rx message as Tx, if we _know_ we sent them might be useful. That way we can read back corruptions. Maybe print corrupted (Rx != Tx) messages in some color.
This is on ES581.3, if it matters.

title: Improve display/handling of many signals for signal graph
author: WarnerS
body:
While being limited to 10 signals in one signal graph window might be fine, it would be nice to then have multiple signal windows.
The workspace required for multiple windows will get tight, so adding tabs to the workspace to group your windows would be very nice.
As an alternative a grouped list of signals to display in the one signal window would also be ok and might prove to be implemented easier. You then switch between many groups of predefined signals and do not need to delete and readd signals.
body_text:

title: DBF files do not support Float32/64 data types
author: MuddyPuddles4325
body: When creating an new DBF file or converting a DBC file I noticed there is not an option for choosing an IEEE floating point number.
body_text: When creating an new DBF file or converting a DBC file I noticed there is not an option for choosing an IEEE floating point number.

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body: Currently BusMaster supports data types only for Raw values. Physical values will be calculated based on the offset, factor and Signal Length etc taken from database.

comments.author: MuddyPuddles4325
comments.body: Yes but there is no way to calculate the physical value of a Floating point number (CANalyzer, etc support this option to view physical values) as this is an allowed datatype in a DBC file. It would be a nice improvement in the future to support this data type.

comments.author: TheOtherMarcus
comments.body: This should be reopened. Busmaster doesn't display physical float values correctly when DBF is converted from DBC.

body_text:
We tried using below BUSMASTER version with VN8970 hardware which was procured without a CANalyzer license since we were informed that BUSMASTER does not require vector license.
But while trying to connect, hardware is not getting detected i.e; only virtual channel 1&2 are visible.

![Uploading VN8970_Not_detected_in_Busmaster.png…]()

comments.author: nostar
comments.body:
When you say VN8970 you mean the VN891x base unit with the VN8970 plugged into it right?  Thats a complete realtime computer system running CANoe/CANalyzer via its own RTOS.  We have a couple of these units as well, and essentially when up and running, 2 machines running 2 separate instances of the CANoe/CANalyzer software are communicating back n forth.  I dont see that ever being supported by busmaster.

The Vector XL driver for busmaster supports all of the 'passive' vector hardware.  I use it regularly with PCI(CanBoardXL), PCMCIA(CanCardXL), and USB(CanCaseXL, VN16xx) hardware.

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body: Available with v2.6.4

title: Database CAN ID order,sorting and identification
author: AutologicAU
body:
I have been building a database for some time and have two issues.
1. My database should show in the database tree firstly the Hex message I.D. and then the signals in it, or shorthand notation of same.
    Currently I need to look in the right hand pane message by message to see the HEX I.D, altough I have started appending the I.D to the name of the message.
1. My database is not in order, under the CAN specification the messages with the lowest I.D. are messages of higher priority.
    As I add messages they add to the bottom of the tree and I cannot segregate, move nor rearrange the messages as I see fit.  It would be nice if I could drag the messages around so that I can get them in the correct order.
   Thank you

comments.author: AutologicAU
comments.body:
Further a CAN message in  a motor vehicle can be present on different CAN buses with  different baud rates thus we may need to be able to have more than one message in any parent database
This could be done by having a treed structure with sub branches in the parent database with different bus systems segregated by type or bus system
That way individual messages or whole branches could be exported together to desired functional child databases thus saving huge amounts of time by not having to reinput messages each time

title: support of external editor for node simulation source code ?
author: BEG-Katzenberger
body:
Using the build-in node simulation editor is quite horrible: no auto indent, source highlighting and other uncountable features which makes coding so comfortable.

I tried to use Eclipse for editing but it seems that the build-in editor is not able to handle a formatted code file.

So please improve the the build-in code editor features significantly or give the user the possibility to choose his favourite editor ...
body_text:
Using the build-in node simulation editor is quite horrible: no auto indent, source highlighting and other uncountable features which makes coding so comfortable.

I tried to use Eclipse for editing but it seems that the build-in editor is not able to handle a formatted code file.

So please improve the the build-in code editor features significantly or give the user the possibility to choose his favourite editor ...

comments.author: nostar
comments.body: The built in editor is certainly not code worthy, but I use external editors with no problem.  I just create a new file, add all of the handlers I think I may need, then save and close the editor.  From then on I always use an external editor.  It's important to keep all of the busmaster generated comments in place though and create new ones around new handlers, for example, if you decide not to create new handlers in the built in editor.  My code editors of choice is Notepad++ for windows and Puma for linux.

comments.author: BEG-Katzenberger
comments.body:
Using an other editor after creating the "frame" is not a problem - you are right.
But changing this frame afterwards is not so easy. 

For example changing the time of an existing timer handler needs a manual adaptation in the corresponding *.def file by renaming the handler function name.
This took me several hours because the dll is generated without errors but could not be loaded from BusMaster ...

title: J1939 Message Handlers are not executed.
author: NorseMN
body:
I have installed 2.6.3 and can't get any message handlers to execute.
I created Message handlers for PGNID, PGNName and PGN_All.
I can clearly see in the Message window that my target message is being received.
All handlers are enabled.
Each message handler is reduced to contains only a Trace instruction.
The PGN_All handler responds to address claim messages.
Otherwise, nothing appears in the Trace Window.
What am I doing wrong?

My primary message of interest is J1939 Proprietary message with PGN 0xFF14.
Even though this is a broadcast message, could BusMaster think 14 is a DA and ignore the message?

Also, I can't find any way to change the ID of a node in BusMaster.
And, the manual describers User Programs, but no such item exists on any menu.
Please help!
Thanks.

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
Hi,
Currently, destination address is considered for broadcast message. Hence, please specify the 'Preferred Address' as '14' for PGN 0xFF14 while creating node in the Node Simulation and check if the handler is executing.

comments.author: NorseMN
comments.body:
I think BusMaster is failing to recognize a broadcast proprietary message.
The 29-bit identifier used in J1939 is structured in the following way.
Priority = 3 bits
Reserved = 1 bit
Data page = 1 bit
PDU Format (PF) = 8 bits
PDU Specific (PS) = 8 bits
Source Address (SA) = 8 bits

In general:
-  When PS = FF, it's a broadcast message.
-  Otherwise, it's (mostly) a destination specific message and PS is a destination address (DA)

But, there are also proprietary messages that are either destination specific or broadcast.
-  When PF = FE, the message is a destination specific proprietary message.
-  When PF = FF, it's a proprietary broadcast message and PS is a message sub-ID.

The message I'm trying to handle is a proprietary broadcast message.
So the 14 is simply a message sub-ID value, and NOT a destination address.

As far as changing the preferred Node address, I'm puzzled.
It seems that I can only set this address when I create a node.
Then, the only way to change it is to delete the node and create a new node.
When I do that, I lose all of the node definition.
It's a lot of work to recreate each handler function manually.
I think I saw an image of the Node Details screen in you manual that shows the Node Name and Node address as editable fields on this screen,
But for me, they are only displayed values.
Thanks for your help.

title: passing data between DLLs
author: nostar
body: Is there a way to pass data between simulated nodes?  I want to pass data received via CAN to my LIN simulation without using COM.
body_text: Is there a way to pass data between simulated nodes?  I want to pass data received via CAN to my LIN simulation without using COM.

comments.author: nostar
comments.body:
For now my solution is to use C file I/O to open a binary file, repeatedly seek to 0, and overwrite the same 2 bytes over and over every 100ms from the CAN node as the message handler is called.  I open the same file from the LIN node as read-only and read it every 100ms or so (as needed).   This works much better than I thought it would (no file locking issues between nodes, etc), but it is most certainly a hack.

More discussion on the concept of global variables and perhaps a protocol independent simulation environment would be helpful here.  If I was trying to pass data between 2 CAN channels of the same interface for example, this would be a non-issue since I could access them from the same node DLL.

comments.author: nostar

comments.author: Nit2Nat
comments.body:
Hi Sandy,

Please find the response to your queries 
1. Is it possible to start the FlexRay Bus with the Busmaster? If yes, how can I do this?
   Current version does not support this feature. This feature will be implemented as part of an extended add-on for FlexRay in the future
2. Why is it not possible to select 2 FlexRay channels, although on ES583.1 two channels are available?
   The ES583.1 has single FlexRay node with channels A & B. For the moment Busmaster only supports A & B on single FlexRay node. We shall test this once again and revert to you.
3. Why ist it not possible to ad a new frame in the Configure Transmission Messages Window?
   ECUs from the Cluster Configuration Window to be selected first. The frames defined for the selected ECUs would be available in the Tx window to be added.
4. How can I log the traffic on the FlexRay and replay it?
   Current version does not support this feature. This feature will be implemented as part of the extended add-on as mentioned in response to Query 1.

We shall keep you updated once the above features are made available in the extended add-on.

Regards
Nithin

title: Transmission of CAN messages are not possible with CAN FD configuration.
author: Naveenkumar28
body:
The can 2.0 specification frames are not able to transmit when a controller is configured as CAN FD interface.
The option to switch between CAN 2.0 or CAN FD frames are not available.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Open Bus Master 2.6.3.
2) Configure CAN FD controller.
3) Configure and transmit the messages.
4) The messages are appeared in the another node as CAN FD frames. Not able to transmit the CAN Frames.

Since, the CAN FD is the extension of CAN protocol specification. The nodes can be configured as CAN or CAN FD.
body_text:

2) Configure CAN FD controller.
3) Configure and transmit the messages.
4) The messages are appeared in the another node as CAN FD frames. Not able to transmit the CAN Frames.

Since, the CAN FD is the extension of CAN protocol specification. The nodes can be configured as CAN or CAN FD.

title: With the latest version of Busmaster (v2.6.3), I had to make some corrections to the project setting to build it for busmaster_debug.
author: PatRuelle
body:
After making Busmaster the Single startup project  I aslo had change the LIN_Kvaser project: in the busmaster_debug section: change Additional Options in Linker section from:
"$(SolutionDir)/BIN/Libs/Release/DataTypes.lib"
"$(SolutionDir)/BIN/Libs/Release/Utils.lib" %(AdditionalOptions)
to:
"$(SolutionDir)/BIN/Libs/$(OutDir)DataTypes.lib"
"$(SolutionDir)/BIN/Libs/$(OutDir)Utils.lib" %(AdditionalOptions)
- Project > Properties > Common Properties > Project Dependencies > Projects > LIN_Kvaser
  Add 'Depends on:' DataTypes and Utils

title: Old value is sent twice in CAN Bus or two different values at the same time
author: arildojr
body:
Hi,

Follows the reproduction of the problem:

1 -  Send a CAN Message through BUSMASTER (in my case 2.6.2, Baud 500k, Channel 1) using a ES581.3 (with 120 ohms resistor) - Doesn´t matter if you perform with or without channels in loop back.
2 - If you are in loop back for example (again, this condition is not required to reproduce), no errors will be shown and the message will be sent successfully in BUSMASTER environment.
3 - However, if you are expecting to receive this message sent in the CAN Bus through your own software you will get two messages instead of just one. For example, you send a message with value "1", afterwards you send the same message with value "2", in your software you will either receive twice the value "1" or receive one time the value "1" and one time value "2" "at the same time", I mean in the same task. Therefore, the solution is to execute the same task again, this is to send value "2" again to receive this value in your software consuming the CAN Bus. In my case I am using a CAN2Bluetooth sensor (ELM327, I tried with different models of sensors also) able to get CAN messages in a CAN Bus and send the message to my application in a tablet. I was told about this issue also using ECUs sending data to a ECU software.

Regards,

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
Please let us the know hardware used (ES581.2 or ES581.3).
There is no such specific configuration required in BusMaster related to OBD 2, as BusMaster communicates with ES581 and it depends on the connectivity from ES581 to OBD port.
To confirm the same, you could perform a test by connecting ES581 channels in loop back (with 120 ohm resistor) and check if you still receive error messages on transmission.

title: Destination address representation is wrong in message window for PDU 2 format messages.
author: rpamparthi
body:
The PDU format (PF) determines whether the message can be transmitted with a destination address or if the message is always transmitted as a broadcast message.

The interpretation of the PDU specific (PS) field changes based on the PF value:

If the PF is between 0 and 239, the message is addressable (PDU1) and the PS field contains the destination address.
If the PF is between 240 and 255, the message can only be broadcast (PDU2) and the PS field contains a Group Extension.
The Group extension expands the number of possible broadcast Parameter Groups that can be represented by the identifier.

The term Parameter Group Number (PGN) is used to refer to the value of the Reserve bit, DP, PF, and PS fields combined into a single 18 bit value.

Example: The ID 0xCF004EE can be divided into the following fields in table 2.

0x0C    0xF0    0×04   0xEE
000 011 0   0   11110000    00000100    11101110
— Prio    R   DP  PF  PS  SA

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/13457388/8837317/5d2a39ec-308b-11e5-9442-7375d507ea6c.png)

body_text:

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba

In LDF file, Frame will have signals mapped, receivers of the frames are the receivers of signals mapped to the respective frame. For more details, please load the .ldf file in BUSMASTER 'LDF Editor' and refer 'LIN Network View' to see the Publisher (Transmitter) and Subscribers (Receivers) of the Frames and Signals.

To invoke 'LDF Editor', please refer help section 'LIN->LIN Database Editor'

title: Spurious CAN messages displayed
author: avogra
body:
Short description:
I receive a lot of spurious / unreasonable can-messages when starting to send CAN messages from Busmaster (see screenshot). It looks like the internal bus error counter would be displayed in the can message window.

Environment:
Version is 2.6.2, Operating system is Windows 7 x64 german, CAN-Interface is Vector VN1630A with CANpiggy 1054mag and Vector driver version 8.9.22.

Detailed description:
My DUT is initially in sleep mode and to be woken by CAN. I load the configuration and setup CAN interface and channel. I then start the configuration with "Start stop sending messages" disabled. Then I enable sending messages. After some errorframes (due to startuptime of my dut) communication is working fine.
But for every CAN message sent (or tried) by the Busmaster, i get displayed one CAN message with unreasonable ID. It starts at ID 0x808 and increases in steps of 0x800 up to >= 0x7F00. As soon as communication is error free, i still get a spurious message for each msg sent but with IDs decreasing in steps of 0x100. When the ID reaches 0x0, no more spurious messages are displayed
![screenshot_020](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/12623449/7832782/e672837c-0461-11e5-86f4-bda47a693ceb.jpg)
body_text:
Short description:
I receive a lot of spurious / unreasonable can-messages when starting to send CAN messages from Busmaster (see screenshot). It looks like the internal bus error counter would be displayed in the can message window.

Environment:
Version is 2.6.2, Operating system is Windows 7 x64 german, CAN-Interface is Vector VN1630A with CANpiggy 1054mag and Vector driver version 8.9.22.

Detailed description:
My DUT is initially in sleep mode and to be woken by CAN. I load the configuration and setup CAN interface and channel. I then start the configuration with "Start stop sending messages" disabled. Then I enable sending messages. After some errorframes (due to startuptime of my dut) communication is working fine.
But for every CAN message sent (or tried) by the Busmaster, i get displayed one CAN message with unreasonable ID. It starts at ID 0x808 and increases in steps of 0x800 up to >= 0x7F00. As soon as communication is error free, i still get a spurious message for each msg sent but with IDs decreasing in steps of 0x100. When the ID reaches 0x0, no more spurious messages are displayed
![screenshot_020](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/12623449/7832782/e672837c-0461-11e5-86f4-bda47a693ceb.jpg)

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body: Bus Error occurs only if there is a mismatch in baud rate/Channel configuration parameters or if resistor is missing. Does these errors occur only if the device is in sleep mode initially and woken by CAN later? What happens if the device is already in wake up mode does it still receive errors?

comments.author: avogra
comments.body:
When the device is awake and i start sending messages from Busmaster, there are no bus errors and no extra messages.

I expect to get some bus errors until the the device is awake as there is noone else setting the ACK-bit. What I am surprised about are those messages in between the errors and after each TX message.

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body: Messages between errors and each Tx message should be transmitted by the device/ECU in response to the Tx message. Tool displays all the message received on the CAN bus. As we don't have the setup, could you please check if the these messages are transmitted by device.

comments.author: avogra
comments.body:
These messages are not transmitted by the device. It is not able to send extended messages (IDs go up to 7Fxx). When I use the same setup with Vector Canoe, those messages are not visible.

Just a guess from my side: Is it possible, that the Vector driver transmits information about the bus error state and Busmaster is interpreting this information as CAN messages? the sequence of "IDs" would fit, if the high byte is the error counter: increase by 4 for every unsuccessful message, decrease by 1 for every successful message.

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body: Messages with ID (0x6A04, 0x7204, 0x7A04, ...) are the error messages received from the hardware. BusMaster follows convention for error ids as (0x6A04, 0x7204, 0x7A04, ...), hence the error message with the same id will not be seen in CANOe.

comments.author: BGSW-Srinibas
comments.body: Error ids should not be displayed as like actual messages (Tx/Rx).

title: Ackownledge management
author: bluehangel
body: Add Ackownledge management in CAN section/settings.
body_text: Add Ackownledge management in CAN section/settings.

comments.author: bluehangel
comments.body:
CAN Extended frame format
ACK slot:
The acknowledge slot is used to acknowledge the receipt of a valid CAN frame. Each node that receives the frame without finding an error, transmits a dominant level in the ACK slot and thus overrides the recessive level of the transmitter. If a transmitter detects a recessive level in the ACK slot it knows that no receiver found a valid frame. A receiving node may transmit a recessive to indicate that it did not receive a valid frame, but another node that did receive a valid frame may override this with a dominant. The transmitting node cannot know that the message has been received by all of the nodes on the CAN network.

http://www.wikiwand.com/en/CAN_bus#/ACK_slot

body:
Hi,

Do you know if the BUSMASTER tool has the ability to read CAN signals via one hardware interface, and repeat them out to another hardware interface(while filtering out some messages) ?

body_text:

Thanks in advance!

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
Hi,
You can use 'Node Simulation' feature in BusMaster with the following steps:
1. Configure 'Message Handlers' in Node Simulation editor, for the messages to be repeated. Message Handler will get executed on reception of the respective message
2. Received message details will be available in parameter RxMsg, configure the m_ucChannel (to point to new hardware) 
3. Use SendMsg(..) API to send the message to different channel connected to new hardware

For more details, please refer help section 'CAN->API Reference'

title: Load DLLs upon startup/open
author: nostar
body: Any DLL that is loaded when a configuration is saved needs that information stored in the cfx file so that it will be reloaded automatically upon startup or open of that configuration.  Right now "Load all DLLs" needs to be executed every time busmaster is started.  An option could be added in preferences to "save dll state in configuration' in case this behavior may not be desired.
body_text: Any DLL that is loaded when a configuration is saved needs that information stored in the cfx file so that it will be reloaded automatically upon startup or open of that configuration.  Right now "Load all DLLs" needs to be executed every time busmaster is started.  An option could be added in preferences to "save dll state in configuration' in case this behavior may not be desired.

title: Busmaster ver 2.6.1
author: ads-tronix
body:
Hello,

We are using busmaster ver 2.6.1 with J1939 activated. When the transmit message windows of the J1939 is launches, we do not have access to the Msg Type BROADCAST.
Is there something to do to have access to this message type?

Thanks for your help
body_text:
Hello,

We are using busmaster ver 2.6.1 with J1939 activated. When the transmit message windows of the J1939 is launches, we do not have access to the Msg Type BROADCAST.
Is there something to do to have access to this message type?

Thanks for your help

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
Hello,
Message Type in the transmit window is populated based on the PF value in the PGN.
1) If the PF value in PGN is in the range 0 to 239 (0x00 to 0xEF), message type can only be either 'Request PGN' or 'Data'.
2) If the PF value in PGN is in the range 240 to 255 (0xF0 to 0xFF), message type can only be either 'Request PGN' or 'Broadcast'.

Please input the PGN value within the range to use message type Broadcast.
For more details, please refer http://www.kvaser.com/en/about-can/higher-layer-protocols/36.html

body_text:

When i tried to add an empty C file to the node gets an error "open C file created for LIN".

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
Hi,
Please follow the below steps.
1. Select the node, Click on 'Add New File...' button, specify the file name (do not load empty file).
2. Click on 'Open' button, new file with the name specified will be created and opened in Function editor
3. Use function editor to define handlers
4. Once handlers are defined, click on 'Build and Load' and 'Enable All Handlers'

Note: To build the cpp/c files, please install MinGW component. Link to download MinGW and document with instructions is sent to your mail id. The same will be updated in the next version help file.

Regards,
Prathiba

Regards,
Dinesh

title: Make J1939 mode as functional as CAN mode
author: glafayette615
body:
It seems to me that most of the features available in CAN mode (i.e. 11 bit identifiers) are not available in J1939 mode.  For me, and I assume many others this is a severe limitation of this tool.

Am I missing something?  Is there a workaround method to send J1939 messages, log them into a graphical signal, etc?

Is there an idea of how big the undertaking would be to make the J1939 mode function the same way as the CAN?
body_text:
It seems to me that most of the features available in CAN mode (i.e. 11 bit identifiers) are not available in J1939 mode.  For me, and I assume many others this is a severe limitation of this tool.

Am I missing something?  Is there a workaround method to send J1939 messages, log them into a graphical signal, etc?

Is there an idea of how big the undertaking would be to make the J1939 mode function the same way as the CAN?

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
Hi,
J1939 uses the 29-bit identifier defined in the CAN 2.0B protocol. To send J1939 messages please do the following:
1. Activate J1939 using J1939->Activate
2. To configure messages for transmission, use J1939 Transmit Window via menu 'J1939->Transmit'

Currently you can only log the data in to a file using menu 'J1939->Logging'.
For more details, please refer section 'J1939' in help file.

Currently I am working on the Visual Studio 2013 ultimate. I had tried to compile the solution, however I received the error regarding cstringt.h library. Then I followed recommendations concerning the WinDDK patch, but when I launched this patch, it got a notification: "patch" is not external or internal command of the running programor package file.  I assume that patch's changes hadn't been applied. Therefore, what this notification mean and what should I do in order to properly execute this patch? 

Thanks in advance)
body_text:
Hello.

Currently I am working on the Visual Studio 2013 ultimate. I had tried to compile the solution, however I received the error regarding cstringt.h library. Then I followed recommendations concerning the WinDDK patch, but when I launched this patch, it got a notification: "patch" is not external or internal command of the running programor package file.  I assume that patch's changes hadn't been applied. Therefore, what this notification mean and what should I do in order to properly execute this patch?

title: Network Statistics windows shows 99.99% busload for CAN-FD protocol, though the actual messages on the bus is less than 75% busload

body:
Used Busmaster Version: 2.5.0
Used Protocol: CAN-FD

Configuration:
Nominal Baud-rate: 1Mbps
Data Baud-rate: 2Mbps, 4Mbps, 8Mbps

Procedure to reproduce the issue:
1. Connect vector device(VN1640A) channel 1, ES523 device channel 1, Channel 2 and channel 3 in a loop.
2. Monitor the Channel 3 of ES523 device in Busmaster.
3. Monitor channel 1 of VN1640A in CANalyzer.
4. A client application will transmit a message to ES523 channel 1 and receive the same from channel 2 of ES523.
5. In the CANanlyzer network statics shows the busload as 75%
6. In the Busmaster network statics shows the busload as 99.99%

title: Channel selection/configuration not correctly saved.
author: etas-lorenz
body:
This is based on customer feedback:

I wanted to report the following misconduct which greatly reduces the usability. 
I have 2 or more ETAS BOA devices connected. e.g. ES523 and ES593-D. 
The Channel Selection dialog offers me eight channel at 
ETAS: // ETH / ES523: 9900045 / CAN: 1 
ETAS: // ETH / ES523: 9900045 / CAN: 2 
ETAS: // ETH / ES523: 9900045 / CAN: 3 
ETAS: // ETH / ES523: 9900045 / CAN: 4 
ETAS: // ETH / ES593_D: 2202375 / CAN: 1 
ETAS: // ETH / ES593_D: 2202375 / CAN: 2 
ETAS: // ETH / ES593_D: 2202375 / CAN: 3 
ETAS: // ETH / ES593_D: 2202375 / CAN: 4 
I select 
ETAS: // ETH / ES593_D: 2202375/CAN: 2 
and call channel configuration. 
After I have tested the configuration I save it. 
After a restart of the bus master and load the saved configuration
ETAS: // ETH / ES523: 9900045/CAN: 1 
is selected !?

title: [COM API] SendCANMSg Interface by Python
author: slongeri
body:
Hello,

i tried to build up a simple automation of busmaster with python 2.7 . The connection is possible so far:

import win32com.client

BUSMASTER_COM_NAME = "CAN_MonitorApp.Application"
CONF_FILE = b"Y:\apiconf.cfx"

myBusMaster         = win32com.client.Dispatch(BUSMASTER_COM_NAME)
# load configuration (can interface setup is not possible by api)
# myBusMaster.LoadConfiguration(CONF_FILE)

myBusMaster.LoadAllDll()
myBusMaster.Connect(True)
myBusMaster.StartLogging()

How to send the message with SendCANMSg ? I can't find an Input description, is there an example how to do it?

Thanks

Simon

title: Node Simulation Big Endian
author: vondraussen

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
All GUI functions properly handle big/little endian. But the node simulation is not. This is due to the used struct, which is always in machine (=intel=little) endianess. We currently discuss on how to improve this.

As a workaround you can declare a local variable, which is a copy of the message structure passed through htonl. Then you should be able to access this second structure for big endian signals. But watch for padding bits at the end of the structure. Some more hints are here:
- http://stackoverflow.com/questions/809902/64-bit-ntohl-in-c
- http://stackoverflow.com/questions/3022552/is-there-any-standard-htonl-like-function-for-64-bits-integers-in-c

comments.author: RBEI-busmaster
comments.body: Fixed the issue. Updated with v3.0.0

title: Database Editor: Issues regarding signed signals
author: GT-Derka
body:
In DatabaseEditor - CAN I created a message and added a 32-bit-signal of int-type, Scale Factor=1, Offset=0.
1. The textboxes are too small to show the whole number.
2. When creating the signal the calculated Min and Max Values are correct.
3. Back in DatabaseEditor-Window the (positive) Max Val is correctly shown as 7FFFFFFF(hex). But the Min Val is shown as 80000000(hex), which is indeed correct, but confuses the user. I would prefer a signed decimal-view. In hex-view the Min Val seems to be larger than Max Val.
4. Edit Signal... openes Signal Details again and shows wrongly 1.968.526.677 as Max Value.
   (New Desc.... still shows the correct number)
5. A thousands-separator would make the numbers readable to human eyes ;-)
6. Scale Factor does not influence Min and Max Val but it should

title: Wrong path to BUSMASTER.tlb
author: GT-Derka
body:
The BUSMASTER.rc is located in Sources/Application and references to "BUSMASTER.tlb".
But the tlb-file is now located in Sources/Application/Release. Thus the path in rc-file needs to be updated to "Release/BUSMASTER.tlb"
body_text:
The BUSMASTER.rc is located in Sources/Application and references to "BUSMASTER.tlb".
But the tlb-file is now located in Sources/Application/Release. Thus the path in rc-file needs to be updated to "Release/BUSMASTER.tlb"

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: BUSMASTER.tlb file will be created in Application folder whenever BUSMASTER.idl file is compiled. Installer script also takes the TLB file from Application path. Previously the TLB file was generated in Application\Release folder. Now we have updated the output path of the TLB file so that installer and .rc file takes from the Application folder.

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body:
Taken from latest version of BUSMASTER.vcxproj, line 986:
`<TypeLibraryName Condition="'$(Configuration)|$(Platform)'=='busmaster_debug|Win32'">.\Release/BUSMASTER.tlb</TypeLibraryName>`

So, while building the project, using the busmaster_debug-configuration as suggested in development_environment.pdf, BUSMASTER.tlb is genereated in Application/Release.
Thus the above mentioned path in vcxproj-file needs to be updated from `.\Release/BUSMASTER.tlb` to `.\BUSMASTER.tlb`

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Yes, agree. We committed the project file with proper settings for busmaster_debug mode. We will update the document accordingly.

title: Tester present under KWP in Diagnostics
author: bentearadu
body:
Tester present according with the standard is:

In UDS, $3E 00, with the suppress bit $3E 80  (Suppress bit is this checked in settings window)
In KWP is $3E 01, with the suppress bit $3E 02. If this standard is chosen, this combo box is unable to be checked, and should be able to be checked)

What should be done here is, that when KWP is chosen, tester present to be sent with $3E 01, and also to be able to check or not the combobox  and if checked this one $3E 02 to be sent .

title: Diagnostic - 11 bit Normal not working
author: bentearadu
body:
Hello, 
Firts of all, I must tell you that this is was an error of design, for which I'm totally responsable, and sorry about it.

The isse is: if you chose in "setting" window from "diagnostics", "11bits Normal", the CAN ID is open for editing. 
Now, how the program is working, is like this:
- Allows the user to enter the CAN ID for Req to ECU (Ex. XYZ), and Resp from ECU (ABC), which is correct.
- then is assuming that in booth cases, the "base address" is the same (from previus example, X00 = A00).
- then is taking the last to digits from CAN ID Req to ECU, from the main window "CAN ID", is enabling the "Send" button and is sending a command.
  Everithing is ok till here, IF the 2 CAN ID are not having 0 the last to digits, like it can happen (now I have on my desk a project like this).

In my case now, the Req CAN ID is 0x600 and Resp CAN ID is 0x500....

In this case, the tool is looking for the last two digits from booth CAN IDs in order to place them in main window under source address and target address. In the case from my example, these will be 0, booth of them.
The tool is sendin a frame if CAN ID Req to ECU is 600 (and what ever else in Resp From ECU, except something with last digits 0 - so, there will be "No responce received", even if on the buss, the ECU responded), and the Source address from main window is 0, but if the Resp from ECU is 500, then the last to digits, (in this case 0) will be placed in main window in Target address.
The tool is done in such a way, that if detects booth of them 0, to NOT enable the SEND button, and not to evaluate the response.

What should be done is that in case of 11 bit Normal, the tool should detect something written in Req/Resp from ECU box, and then, to put automaticlaly in main window in CAN ID the value from Request to ECU, and to monitor the bus for the frames with the ID inserted in Setting window in Resp form ECU. (now is evaluating the frames based in target address from main window.)
body_text:
Hello, 
Firts of all, I must tell you that this is was an error of design, for which I'm totally responsable, and sorry about it.

The isse is: if you chose in "setting" window from "diagnostics", "11bits Normal", the CAN ID is open for editing. 
Now, how the program is working, is like this:
- Allows the user to enter the CAN ID for Req to ECU (Ex. XYZ), and Resp from ECU (ABC), which is correct.
- then is assuming that in booth cases, the "base address" is the same (from previus example, X00 = A00).
- then is taking the last to digits from CAN ID Req to ECU, from the main window "CAN ID", is enabling the "Send" button and is sending a command.
  Everithing is ok till here, IF the 2 CAN ID are not having 0 the last to digits, like it can happen (now I have on my desk a project like this).

In my case now, the Req CAN ID is 0x600 and Resp CAN ID is 0x500....

In this case, the tool is looking for the last two digits from booth CAN IDs in order to place them in main window under source address and target address. In the case from my example, these will be 0, booth of them.
The tool is sendin a frame if CAN ID Req to ECU is 600 (and what ever else in Resp From ECU, except something with last digits 0 - so, there will be "No responce received", even if on the buss, the ECU responded), and the Source address from main window is 0, but if the Resp from ECU is 500, then the last to digits, (in this case 0) will be placed in main window in Target address.
The tool is done in such a way, that if detects booth of them 0, to NOT enable the SEND button, and not to evaluate the response.

What should be done is that in case of 11 bit Normal, the tool should detect something written in Req/Resp from ECU box, and then, to put automaticlaly in main window in CAN ID the value from Request to ECU, and to monitor the bus for the frames with the ID inserted in Setting window in Resp form ECU. (now is evaluating the frames based in target address from main window.)

title: libWrapper_CAN.a
author: GT-Derka
body:
From time to time I get problems while building nodes in node simulation. The Output Window shows:
"mingw32-g++.exe: error: [busmasterdir]\Sources\Application\SimulatedSystems\OBJ\libWrapper_CAN.a: No such file or directory"
Unfortunately I cannot reproduce the problem, because some time ago the build-process succeeded..

But in GCCDLLMakeTemplate_CAN I found:
    @mingw32-g++.exe -shared -Wl,--dll  "<INPUTFILE>.o"  -o "<INPUTFILE>.dll"  "<INSTALLDIR>\SimulatedSystems\OBJ\libWrapper_CAN.a"

In BUSMASTER INSTALL SCRIPT.nsi I found instead:
  File ..\Sources\BIN\Release\SimulatedSystems\OBJ\libWrapper_CAN.a

Thus there seems to be a problem with <INSTALLDIR> in "developer version"?

To clarify: The file exists at:
"...\busmaster\Sources\BIN\Release\SimulatedSystems\OBJ\libWrapper_CAN.a"
It does not exist, but is searched at:
"...\busmaster\Sources\Application\SimulatedSystems\OBJ\libWrapper_CAN.a"

Can anyone confirm the problem?

title: Error during build release configuration

I tried to compile the current bus master source environment of Microsoft Visual Studio 2012 Express and Microsoft Visual Studio 2012 Professional. Despite the fact that everything is set up however it is described in the document development_environment.pdf, the compilation result is 1676 warnings and 1 error. :VCEnd" exited with code -2147024891.   C:\Program Files (x86)\MSBuild\Microsoft.Cpp\v4.0\V110\Microsoft.CppCommon.targets". 

Have you any suggestions on how to solve the problem with the above error?

Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

title: inVoking  WinGW gCC Complier falied
author: ahnulp
body: Hello,first put tHe issue;  after install WinGW on C:\WinGW but,still not Complier SuCCess.inVoking  WinGW gCC Complier falied.  How to solVe ,?  tHanks..
body_text: Hello,first put tHe issue;  after install WinGW on C:\WinGW but,still not Complier SuCCess.inVoking  WinGW gCC Complier falied.  How to solVe ,?  tHanks..

comments.author: ahnulp
comments.body:
1- Install MinGW.

2- After installation is complete,then Right-click on "My Computer" icon. Select "Properties".

3- In the Properties Window, select "Avanced Settings" on the left sidebar (Windows 7).

4- Click on the "Environment Variables" button located at the bottom left of the "Advanced Settings" window.

5- Click on "Path" variable on the tpo list and then click on the edit button.

6- Add ;C:\MinGW\bin to the end of the string and click OK all the way to exit all the privious windows

7- RESTART your Windows!! On my computer it only worked after I restarted.

8- After system restart,run Busmaster and it should now find minGW.
i got  it.

title: Time stamps in logging have errors
author: duanemotec
body:
When I use a VS-com USB-CAN P device I see steps in the reported timestamps in the Seconds field. Example:
00:12:16:6390 Rx 1 0x172 s 8 00 00 00 00 01 F4 00 00 
00:12:16:6390 Rx 1 0x174 s 8 28 19 00 01 00 00 00 00 
00:12:16:6390 Rx 1 0x644 s 8 00 00 00 00 BF 00 14 8A 
00:12:16:6390 Rx 1 0x645 s 8 0A 5F 01 F4 00 00 00 0F   <--here it's 12 seconds
00:13:16:3840 Rx 1 0x648 s 8 04 3B FF FF FF FF FF FF <--here it's 13 seconds.
00:13:16:6400 Rx 1 0x660 s 8 7F FF 7F FF 7F FF 7F FF 
00:13:16:6400 Rx 1 0x661 s 8 7F FF 00 00 00 00 00 00 
00:13:16:6400 Rx 1 0x480 s 8 4C 54 43 20 FF FF 01 00 
00:13:16:6400 Rx 1 0x649 s 8 43 8C 4B 28 41 7D 00 10 
In addition these logs do not play back in real time - on some PCs they play slower, on other they play faster.

title: Test automation editor channel selection issues

1. Channel no. column header in test editor is not completely displayed. We need to expand it to view the column header name.

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body:
I will deal with this issue.
3.: When cfx loads, the test automation-xml loads, too. Thus in my eyes the warning message should pop up, when the cfx loads. Otherwise the user is not informed, that a problem occured, unless he starts the test automation editor. Please notice that it is possible, to start execution of test automation without starting the test automation editor.
4.: Can you provide further information to reproduce this behavior, please? I do not understand the problem.

comments.author: RBEI-Pramod
comments.body:
To reproduce follow the below steps :
1. Select kvaser virtual channels 1 and 2
2. Add a send node. Select channel no. 1
3. Add a verify node. Select channel no. 4
4. Execute it
Result say SUCCESS.
We are sending the message in channel no. 1 and response is received in channel no. 2
As the verify node channel no. is 4, the test execution should FAIL.

title: Peak-Driver works on wrong channel
author: GT-Derka
body:
I use the PEAK PCAN-USB-Pro interface with Busmaster v2.3.0.
After successful selection of PEAK USB as CAN-Driver I choose in Hardware Selection:
- Channel 1 ... CAN 1
- Channel 2 ... CAN 2

Messages I configured to be transmitted on Channel 1 become transmitted on CAN 2 and vice versa.
At the DIL_CAN.cpp in CDIL_CAN::DILC_SendMsg the function m_pBaseDILCAN_Controller->CAN_SendMsg(dwClientID, sCanTxMsg); is called correctly. The data in sCanTxMsg is also correct, especially the m_ucChannel-field carries the right information (1 or 2). But finally the message is transmited on the wrong bus, thus I suppose, the bug is a driver-problem.

Can anyone confirm the bug? Thanks in advance.
body_text:
I use the PEAK PCAN-USB-Pro interface with Busmaster v2.3.0.
After successful selection of PEAK USB as CAN-Driver I choose in Hardware Selection:
- Channel 1 ... CAN 1
- Channel 2 ... CAN 2

Messages I configured to be transmitted on Channel 1 become transmitted on CAN 2 and vice versa.
At the DIL_CAN.cpp in CDIL_CAN::DILC_SendMsg the function m_pBaseDILCAN_Controller->CAN_SendMsg(dwClientID, sCanTxMsg); is called correctly. The data in sCanTxMsg is also correct, especially the m_ucChannel-field carries the right information (1 or 2). But finally the message is transmited on the wrong bus, thus I suppose, the bug is a driver-problem.

Can anyone confirm the bug? Thanks in advance.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Is this done in Test Automation or using Tx window ?

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body: Tx window

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
IS the channels connected as loop back ? 

In this case, the frames transmitted in channel 1 will be shown as Tx for channel 1 and Rx as channel 2 in message window. The frames transmitted are received back in the other channel and hence two entries are displayed for each frame.

If this is not the case, the behaviour is not reproducible.

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body: No, there is no loop back.

comments.author: dresber
comments.body: I have the same issue with PCAN-USB Pro (Firmware 1.1) and Busmaster v2.3.0. Channel 1 and Channel 2 are interchanged.

title: Replay DELAY
author: Stefanuccio
body:
I made an acquisition of a test of 15s . By reading the file .txt the last message time is consistent , but when i replay the log file with the function "Replay" the simulation time of the message result almost twice the lenght . 
For example: i made an acquisition of the signal "KeySts" , at 0s i started the engine , at 15s i made key-off.
When i read the signal by the .txt the result is coherent with the time.
When ,instead, i start the acquisition on the graph function , the curve is in key-off status at 29s.There's a delay in the graph representation between the time simulation and the effective time acquisition.
body_text:
I made an acquisition of a test of 15s . By reading the file .txt the last message time is consistent , but when i replay the log file with the function "Replay" the simulation time of the message result almost twice the lenght .

comments.body:
We tried sending a signal every one second for 15 seconds and used replay and signal graph to plot it. The length of the duration is same as 15 seconds.

Could you share your log file as well as your configuration (.cfx) file by sending it to busmaster@in.bosch.com.

Please also send us the dbf file if possible.

comments.body: Done

comments.author: PBjore
comments.body:
Hi i'm experiencing the same issue while replaying files with a lot of data, both using simulation and peak can interface. 

With a log file recorded in System mode with a verified delay between a specific cyclic of 50ms it is played back at roughly 100ms give or take. One second takes between 1.5-2 seconds to playback. I have logfile examples if needed.

title: Change Encoding of Test-Automation-Setup.xml to UTF-8
author: GT-Derka
body:
Most XML-Files are encoded in UTF-8, thus Busmaster should also create UTF-8-Files.
To reach this goal it is necessary, to specify the encoding when creating the file.

The parser used to read the file recognizes the used encoding (both UTF-8 and UTF-16) already correctly. No changes necessary here.

This Issue is already solved in Pull Request #700

title: ERROR instead of ERRORS in Test-Automation-Setup.xml
author: GT-Derka
body:
When saving the Test-Automation-Setup to *.xml and Failure Classification ERROR is selected at verify-entity, "ERRORS" is written into the XML-file.
"ERROR" would be right.

Thus in future ERROR should be written, but to reach downwards compatibility both ERROR and ERRORS should be accepted when reading the file.

I will deal with this issue.

title: Does VerifyResponse work?
author: GT-Derka
body:
I'm trying to get the idea of the VerifyResponse-Test step in Test Automation, but I'm not able to get anything to work.
The general idea of verification in test automation is to "Send" a message, "wait" some time and "verify" the answer, right?
To verify there are two possibilities in busmaster:
- verify
- verifyResponse
  "Verify" checks, if the signal-value equals the expected one, independent from time
  "VerifyResponse" checks, if the signal-value equals the expected one AND the message was RECEIVED within the specified time ("Wait Until"). Please correct me, if I'm wrong.
  I used "verify" successfully several times, but the result of "verifyResponse" is always "SUCCESS COUNT 0".

Is there any working example, which shows the usage of VerifyResponse? I studied the SampleTestSetupFile.xml, but there is no explanation at all.
It would be great, to have a XML-TestSetup, a NodeSimulation-Cpp-Code and the expected results of the test.
Thanks in advance.

  "VerifyResponse" checks, if the signal-value equals the expected one AND the message was RECEIVED within the specified time ("Wait Until"). Please correct me, if I'm wrong.
  I used "verify" successfully several times, but the result of "verifyResponse" is always "SUCCESS COUNT 0".

Is there any working example, which shows the usage of VerifyResponse? I studied the SampleTestSetupFile.xml, but there is no explanation at all.
It would be great, to have a XML-TestSetup, a NodeSimulation-Cpp-Code and the expected results of the test.
Thanks in advance.

comments.author: GT-Derka
etas-lorenz
comments.body: I looked in the function and have to agree that how it's currently implemented is wrong. If WaitForSingleObject is successful the string "SUCCESS" is displayed. If not, the conditions currently lead to the message "No Message  has Recieved". The path in which a "SUCCESS COUNT" is shown is unreachable. That's a bug.

comments.author: RBEI-VENKAT
comments.body: - Currently Test Automation editor is registered with DIL layer as CAN_MONITOR_NODE.So it will receive the messages sent by CAN->Tx window as Tx Messages and VerifyResponse never validates the signals with those messages. - To verify the functionality of VerifyResponse we need to send the Messages either from Node simulation or from External ECU(or BUSMASTER). - I think we have to register Test automation as a Simulation node instead of Monitor Node which is proper way of registering simulation ECUs in BUSMASTER.

title: Aborting renaming Test-Setup and Test-Setup deletes name

body:

title: Gateway
author: Stefanuccio
body:
I would like to know if there's a way to setup a gateway transmission by the software , through can case .
So that , income message on can case will be completely substitute with the one defined by the user, and then sended to the network.
body_text:
I would like to know if there's a way to setup a gateway transmission by the software , through can case .
So that , income message on can case will be completely substitute with the one defined by the user, and then sended to the network.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: Yes. CANcase is supported via the hardware driver "Vector XL". You have to put BUSMASTER in front of your ECU, so that any traffic to and from the ECU is forwarded through BUSMASTER. You can then write a node simulation program in C++ to do the forwarding resp. the manipulation of selected frames and signals.
