title: Transmitted messages are shown twice
author: GT-Derka
body:
To reproduce:
- (Create new Busmaster configuration)
- Configre a new message to be transmitted on CAN-Channel 1 (e.g. triggered by key-event)
- select Vector XL as CAN-Driver
- Hardware Selection
  - Channel 1 - CAN 1
  - Channel 2 - CAN 2
- do not connect any hardware (!)
- CAN->Connect (no "missing hardware"-warning/error is displayed)
- start sending messages
- press configured key
- message is displayed in message-window (Channel 1 - Tx) AND
- message is displayed at the very same time in message-window (Channel 2 - Rx)

Due to the missing hardware the message cannot be transmitted successfully and particularly cannot be received, even if Busmaster says so.
When selecting Simulation, the message is only shown as Tx on Channel 1, which is the correct behavior.
body_text:
To reproduce:
- (Create new Busmaster configuration)
- Configre a new message to be transmitted on CAN-Channel 1 (e.g. triggered by key-event)
- select Vector XL as CAN-Driver
- Hardware Selection
  - Channel 1 - CAN 1
  - Channel 2 - CAN 2
- do not connect any hardware (!)
- CAN->Connect (no "missing hardware"-warning/error is displayed)
- start sending messages
- press configured key
- message is displayed in message-window (Channel 1 - Tx) AND
- message is displayed at the very same time in message-window (Channel 2 - Rx)

Due to the missing hardware the message cannot be transmitted successfully and particularly cannot be received, even if Busmaster says so.
When selecting Simulation, the message is only shown as Tx on Channel 1, which is the correct behavior.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
Usually, when the hardware is not connected, the hardware selection will fail. Only when there is a hardware connected, the channel selection dialog will be displayed. In the above use case, I assume you have installed Vector libraries which provides virtual channels and that's the reason the channel selection dialog is displayed even if the hardware is not connected. In this case, channels are connected by a virtual loop back between channel 1 and 2. Hence, the frames transmitted in channel 1 will be received in channel 2 and vice versa.

The description of the channels will be displayed in the channel selection dialog. For virtual channels, the description will be displayed accordingly. Please let us know if the display in channel selection dialog shows an actual channel.

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body:
Thank you, for your response.
I thought selecting "Simulation" would simulate the Bus-acces, selecting a driver would really influence the bus. But you are right, I installed Vector-libraries and thus the driver switches to virtual channels.
The problem was, that I previously used hardware and saved the configuration. Later I started Busmaster without connected hardware and without any information the vector-driver changed from physical to virtual channel.
So in my eyes the issue can be closed but an information, that virtual channels are used (at least in the trace-window) would be helpful.

title: Busmaster outside visible screen
author: GT-Derka
body:
To reproduce:
- Start busmaster on a laptop with additional second monitor
- Place the busmaster-window on second monitor
- close busmaster
- remove second monitor
- start busmaster

Icon of Busmaster is shown in taskbar, but Busmaster itself stays at the old position (second screen) -> not visible if only one monitor is present

My solution to get Busmaster visible again was to change the MainWnd.left-property in the windows-registry at HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\RBEI-ETAS\BUSMASTER_v2.3.0\MainWnd
body_text:
To reproduce:
- Start busmaster on a laptop with additional second monitor
- Place the busmaster-window on second monitor
- close busmaster
- remove second monitor
- start busmaster

Icon of Busmaster is shown in taskbar, but Busmaster itself stays at the old position (second screen) -> not visible if only one monitor is present

My solution to get Busmaster visible again was to change the MainWnd.left-property in the windows-registry at HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\RBEI-ETAS\BUSMASTER_v2.3.0\MainWnd

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
When the second monitor is removed (disconnecting the monitor cable from laptop or from monitor) have the same effect with the above steps which is the application is displayed in the primary window when launched.

In the other scenario where the busmaster is launched and placed in the second monitor and the configuration saved, if the second monitor is removed the icon will be still in the task bar. If the icon in the task bar is selected, then the application will minimize once and will be displayed again in the primary monitor. This behavior is observed with MS excel also.

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body:
You are right, minimizing busmaster via icon in taskbar and maximizing again, pulls the window back onto the single available screen. But is this your solution to the issue?
I would expect, that the busmaster is directly shown on the first monitor, if no second monitor is available - independently of the behavior of MS Excel.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: It is the default behavior of Windows. There is no separate handling done for the connection and disconnection of the second monitor.

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body: Checking the current screen-resolution when busmaster starts and a validation of the saved, old window-position would solve this issue in a nice way, wouldn't it?

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body:
Even MS Excel 2010 does not show the behavoir of Busmaster. Maybe we talk at cross-purposes?
Once again: I start Busmaster, drag it onto the second monitor and close it again. The current position of the busmaster window is stored to the windows-registry.
Then I disconnect the second screen by removing the cable => Only one monitor left.
Now I start Busmaster which shows up at the old position. Due to the monitor is now disconnected I cannot see the Busmaster-window. I can only get it back to the visible area by using your workaround (minimizing and maximizing).
The solution to this Issue would be, to check the available screen-size and check, if the stored position of the window is within the available screen. If so, the second monitor is still present and everything is fine. If not, the second monitor is removed and Busmaster have to show up on the first monitor.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: My opinion on this is also that this is something that needs to be improved. Users would expect that the window opens in any case and doesn't come up hidden outside screen boundaries.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Issue fixed and available with v2.6.3

title: Need update for "development manual" document on website
author: vsupacha
body:
As a beginner for the BUSMASTER development, I have tried to follow the procedure as described in the "development manual" document downloaded from the official URL: http://rbei-etas.github.io/busmaster/. This should be normal for a beginner to start developing any project.

I have found that the document is already obsolete with several frustrations as follows:
1. The document still refers to Visual Studio 2008 Express which is not compatible with the latest BUSMASTER.sln file (VS 2012 Express).
2. There is no reference to [busmaster_debug] environment. The missing content has caused me several hours to resolve error messages. 
3. Git for Windows is referred as the tool for cloning from GitHub. Though, I have quite a better experience with the official GitHub for Windows.
4. Document states about a patch with a batch file for DDK. However, those files from clean installation of DDK 7.1.0 seem to have the mentioned patch applied already. In addition, the batch file requires 'patch.exe' from MinGW with msys-base package with addition PATH configuration.

My machine is Windows 7 - 64 bit with Visual Studio 2012 Express and DDK 7.1.0 without IXXAT runtime files. There are 8 error messages for the clean build: five from the missing of vcinpl.h in IXXAT, one from the problems of starting BusEmulation, two from the missing of Ws2_32.lib from Microsoft SDK 6.10.

![error_list](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2390702/2756415/30eacb12-c976-11e3-8feb-ba8237049f68.png)

title: Replaying big logfiles?
author: SVABSE
body:
Hello,

I have som logdata recorded during a whole day running a CAN-system resulting in up to 5 logfiles of size 50MB. Replaying one of this files is no problem. But when collecting all files in a single big file for replay Windows reports "Unhandled exception" . I guess the file (appr. 250MB) is to big.
Is there a way to replay the recorded 50MB files sequentially?

Thanks in advance!
Regards
body_text:
Hello,

I have som logdata recorded during a whole day running a CAN-system resulting in up to 5 logfiles of size 50MB. Replaying one of this files is no problem. But when collecting all files in a single big file for replay Windows reports "Unhandled exception" . I guess the file (appr. 250MB) is to big.
Is there a way to replay the recorded 50MB files sequentially?

Thanks in advance!
Regards

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
First of all an unhandled exception should not occur. This is something we have to fix.

To replay multiple log files in a sequence is an interesting feature request that we should think about. Currently it's not implemented.

comments.author: SVABSE
comments.body:
I did some more investigation on replaying big log files. I don't know what the size limit of a logfile for replay is (might depend on physical memory) but an "File to big" information before data parsing starts would be great.

An implementation of a progress bar for the data parsing process should be available.

When loading a file that is "to big" to be replayed, busmaster seems to complete the load process. Data on the CAN-bus is displayed in the message window but there is no data being replayed.

The data parsing process takes very long time. To load a 50MB logfile from HDD takes 1.5 min on an ordinary one year old laptop. Would be nice to get it accelerated if possible.

comments.author: SVABSE
comments.body:
I tried to attach the dump file of an unhandled exception as reported earlier.
When it happened I tried to replay two log files of size 100MB each. The memory consumption according to the Windows task manager reached above 80% of physical memory.
If you like to receive the dumpfile please provide me with an eMail address.

comments.author: RBEI-VENKAT
comments.body: Please send file to BUSMASTER@in.bosch.com

title: Node simulation starts even if handlers are disabled
author: GT-Derka
body:
In this example the DLL contains a timer which periodically transmitts a CAN-Message.
After starting busmaster v2.3.0 and clicking on "Load all DLLs" the Output Window confirmes that the dll is loaded successfully. If a click at the Connect-Button follows, the transmission of the frame starts immediately although the "Start/Stop timer handler(s)" is not pressed. A click on this button activates only the button. A second click deactivates the button and stops the transmission.
Thus the timer handlers should stay deactivated when busmaster is connected (or the button needs to become activated as well).
The message handlers stay deactivated when busmaster is connected until the user clicks on "Enable Message handler(s)". This is in my eyes the correct behavior.
body_text:
In this example the DLL contains a timer which periodically transmitts a CAN-Message.
After starting busmaster v2.3.0 and clicking on "Load all DLLs" the Output Window confirmes that the dll is loaded successfully. If a click at the Connect-Button follows, the transmission of the frame starts immediately although the "Start/Stop timer handler(s)" is not pressed. A click on this button activates only the button. A second click deactivates the button and stops the transmission.
Thus the timer handlers should stay deactivated when busmaster is connected (or the button needs to become activated as well).
The message handlers stay deactivated when busmaster is connected until the user clicks on "Enable Message handler(s)". This is in my eyes the correct behavior.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
Yes, I agree that the initial behavior of the timer handles is incorrect.

title: Textual description of messages not in database malfunctions
author: GT-Derka
body:
After clicking CAN->Message Window->Configure -> NDB I added a new Message Display Attribute, e.g.
Message Text: test
Message ID: 0x102
Message Color: Red
When transmitting the message 0x102 it is colored as defined but the text in the Message-Column in Message Window is still "0x102" and not "test".
When the message is defined in the database the correct name is displayed and the color corresponds to the defined color at the DB-tab.
body_text:
After clicking CAN->Message Window->Configure -> NDB I added a new Message Display Attribute, e.g.
Message Text: test
Message ID: 0x102
Message Color: Red
When transmitting the message 0x102 it is colored as defined but the text in the Message-Column in Message Window is still "0x102" and not "test".
When the message is defined in the database the correct name is displayed and the color corresponds to the defined color at the DB-tab.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
We changed the behavior in the past so that the message name columns always shows the Hex ID, if no database is loaded. That's probably the reason why this is now inconsistent with the message windows configuration NDB thing.

Option 1: IMO it doesn't make sense to have multiple features to give messages a name. Hence, we should remove the message text option in NDB.

Option 2: We should correct the message column content. It has to have information in the following priority:
- Name from Database, if available
- Message Text from Configuration NDB, if available
- Hex ID

What option do you prefer?

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body: Maybe it would be nice to have the possibility to give the message a name, without creating a database. But I don't see a real use case, so in my eyes Option 1 would be ok, if the documentation becomes adapted, too.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
For consistency in Option 1, we should shift all configurations possible in the NDB configuration to the Database Editor, including message attributes for colorization. This is quite some work.

Hence, we should start with Option 2 as an intermediate step towards Option 1.

title: Buffer-Tab is missing
author: GT-Derka
body:
To circumvent the problem, that busmaster displays all messages sent from an "busmaster"-node as Rx I tried to associate the node as owner of the message window, according to #262 .
But in Busmaster v2.3.0 the Buffer-tab does not contain any combo box to do so.

Is there any possibility to mark messages (sent from a node simulated by busmaster) as Tx?
body_text:
To circumvent the problem, that busmaster displays all messages sent from an "busmaster"-node as Rx I tried to associate the node as owner of the message window, according to #262 .
But in Busmaster v2.3.0 the Buffer-tab does not contain any combo box to do so.

Is there any possibility to mark messages (sent from a node simulated by busmaster) as Tx?

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
The current behavior should be that messages received by BUSMASTER and for which the node simulation message event is raised are marked as Rx.

Everything sent by the node simulation using SendMsg(...) should be marked as Tx.

If the behavior would be corrected as just described, would this solve this issue?

comments.author: GT-Derka
comments.body:
Thank you for your quick reply.

To clarify: I took a CAPL-Code to transmit a message cyclically, converted the code to C++ using the BUSMASTER CAPL_2_CPP Converter. Then I added this file to a simulated node. Finally the message becomes transmitted but the direction is marked as Rx.
My problem is, that these messages should be marked as Tx.

=> Yes, it would solve this issue, if everything sent by the node simulation is marked as Tx. (But I don't use the SendMsg(...)-function.)

According to #262 I thought your solution is not possible, but the user has "the freedom to choose owner of the message window", using the combo box (which I cannot find) in the "buffer"-tab.

Both options are acceptable for me - the first one (without changing the owner of the message window) seems to be more logical to me.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
What function are you using, if not SendMsg(...)?

The buffer tab function is just an idea, and not yet implemented. I first have to think about it, whether this is a proper and user friendly way.

etas-lorenz

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
Filter works with the following steps:
1. Configure filter (Stop or Pass) using CAN --> Filter Configuration menu
2. Launch Configure Replay dialog using CAN --> Replay --> Configure menu and add filters using the Filters button
3. Enable filters using the menu CAN --> Replay --> Enable Filters
4. Follow the same steps for Message Window and Logging to apply filters

body_text:
Im trying to use perl package in advance of test automatisation with BusMaster.
$ex->Connect(1); //command is working,
$ex->LoadAllDll(); //is also working,
$ex->EnableDisableHandlers(0); //but it is not working

GoOnline() is not working too.
So Iam not able to enbaling handlers for CAN communication.
Is there any information about it?

comments.author: RBEI-VENKAT
comments.body: The function EnableDisableHandlers() is defined as, EnableDisableHandlers(int bEnable, eHandlerType); where, enum eHandlerType is defined as, eHandlerType { all = 0, timers = 1, key = 2, message = 3, error = 4, } So, to Enable all handlers the function call should be, $ex->EnableDisableHandlers(1, all);

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: GoOnline() function is meant for J1939

comments.author: AdamBorcsok
comments.body:
Thank you for the fast response, Im trying again, but handlers are still not changing state
$ex->EnableDisableHandlers(1,0);
I set a time delay between every command (between connect, load dll, and enable handlers), but no any changes.

Anyway, I think, the help page should be updated with bEnable parameter in BusMaster v2.2.0
where the description is the following:

EnableDisableHandlers : To enable or disable DLL handlers
Description
This function will enable or disable different handlers depending upon the argument passed to it.

Inputs
Enum eHandlerType { all = 0, timers = all + 1, key = timers + 1, message = key + 1, error = message + 1 }

Cause of failure
Invalid Enum type.

Parent topic: COM Interface API Listing

title: It should be possible to link an external *.dll in a NodeSimulation like the Vector CANalyzer Tool does it with "Extensions CAPL dll´s "
author: mgiaco

comments.author: mgiaco
comments.body:
I need a file open dialog in the simulated systems.
Has anybody an idea how i can achieve that?
I tried to use GetOpenFileName but therefore i need the libcomdlg32.a can I add this lib anywhere? 
When i change the DLLMake file it doesn´t work because the file is generated new each build so i cannot overwrite this.

thanks for the help

comments.author: RBEI-VENKAT
comments.body: DllMake will be generated every time the dll was build based on the template file GCCDLLMakeTemplate_CAN distributed along with BUSMASTER Installer.. So, to link the library please edit the template provided in {BUSMASTER_InstallerPath}/GCCDLLMakeTemplate_CAN In this case the Template should be, --- #Make file for building DLL DllFile.dll : ObjFile.o @mingw32-g++.exe -shared -Wl,--dll ".o" -o ".dll" "\SimulatedSystems\OBJ\libWrapper_CAN.a" -lcomdlg32 ObjFile.o : g++ -x c++ -I"\SimulatedSystems\include" -c ".cpp" -o ".o" -mwindows ---

comments.author: mgiaco
comments.body: thanks that´s cool i didn´t see that. So with this it should also be possible to use or other stuff in the simulated system.

title: Channel selection in simulated systems (Peak)
author: Bufarrio
body:
My project has 5 simulated systems, every system corresponds to  different CAN module, 2 of these 5 have a different Baud rate. These 5 CANs are connected to the micro through  a 3 PCAN-USB Pro (Peak) (2 channels by each one). How could I assign the correspond channel?

The only way I've found is to open 5 different busmater windows with its correspondent configuration.

In other words, my project has the systems A,B,C,D and E. Every system has to be connected to its corresponding micro. A to micro A, B to micro B, etc. How could I assign what system corresponds to its channel? Now I have 5 different configuration, A configuration, B configuration, etc.

Thank you.

title: Classic iconset lost in 2.2
author: bluehangel
body: Classic iconset lost in 2.2
body_text: Classic iconset lost in 2.2

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Classic icon set is deprecated from v2.2.0 onwards.

comments.author: bluehangel
comments.body: Why ? The new icons are too big and ugly, they don't match the OS rules.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
The old icon set seemed to have been copied from other locations. Hence, it wasn't homogenous from look&feel and I'm not sure about the license. The new icon set is proper in these regards. In addition maintaining two icons sets creates additional maintenance efforts.

From the technological perspective, we can separate the icon set in an own compiled resource files that can be maintained independent of the rest of the project. Would you or do you know somebody, how would volunteer as maintainer for the old icon set? This includes creating icons, whenever new features require it. We can then distribute the icon set along with newer releases.

title: [COM API] enums and structs don't have GUIDs
author: rolandkoerner
body:
The enums and structs defined in IdlStructure.h don't have UUIDs associated to them. Though it doesn't seem to be a problem when using the COM API in Visual Studio Projects, the widely used Python library win32com has a problem with that - at the moment i can't find a way to use those methods that expect records as arguments.
Is it possible to add those UUIDs to the Record definitions for the next build?

With kind regards,
Roland
body_text:
The enums and structs defined in IdlStructure.h don't have UUIDs associated to them. Though it doesn't seem to be a problem when using the COM API in Visual Studio Projects, the widely used Python library win32com has a problem with that - at the moment i can't find a way to use those methods that expect records as arguments.
Is it possible to add those UUIDs to the Record definitions for the next build?

With kind regards,
Roland

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: That's just a small issue, hence we can/should do it with v2.3.0. Tomorrows v2.2.0 is already in the last stages of testing.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Updated with v2.3.0 commits

title: Support for BOA v1.4, v1.5 and v2 in parallel for FlexRay and LIN
author: etas-lorenz
body:
We've already extended the BOA support for CAN. It supports all stable versions in parallel and automatically selects the newest.

We should do the same for FlexRay and LIN. At least for FlexRay I've seen that newer interfaces are only supported via BOA v2 and are not available via BOA v1.5, which BUSMASTER speaks today.
body_text:
We've already extended the BOA support for CAN. It supports all stable versions in parallel and automatically selects the newest.

We should do the same for FlexRay and LIN. At least for FlexRay I've seen that newer interfaces are only supported via BOA v2 and are not available via BOA v1.5, which BUSMASTER speaks today.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: BOA 1.4 nd 2.0 support added for FlexRay with v2.3.0 commits

title: Emulated key presses not detected by key handler
author: BAH-DwightFalkofske
body:
Hello,

I am attempting to use Busmaster to demonstrate vehicle communications for a kids engineering event.  I want to use an Xbox controller, remapped to keypresses, to control routines in Busmaster.  I have the key handler routines running perfectly from a standard keyboard, but the emulated keypresses from the Xbox controller are not detected by Busmaster.  (I have confirmed that the Xbox key press emulation works in text editors, such as MS Word and Notepad.)  

Is there any settings or adjustments that can be made to get Busmaster to detect the emulated keyboard?

Any help or insight you can provide is appreciated.

Thanks!
Dwight
body_text:
Hello,

I am attempting to use Busmaster to demonstrate vehicle communications for a kids engineering event.  I want to use an Xbox controller, remapped to keypresses, to control routines in Busmaster.  I have the key handler routines running perfectly from a standard keyboard, but the emulated keypresses from the Xbox controller are not detected by Busmaster.  (I have confirmed that the Xbox key press emulation works in text editors, such as MS Word and Notepad.)  

Is there any settings or adjustments that can be made to get Busmaster to detect the emulated keyboard?

Any help or insight you can provide is appreciated.

Thanks!
Dwight

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
Hello Dwight,

we discussed this today during our developer's telco. We are using AutoIt for GUI testing, which also simulates keypresses. This works for us.

Can you send us a link to the Xbox key press emulation software? We'll try to find out what is different and whether we can modify BUSMASTER to work with it.

Thanks,
Tobias

comments.author: BAH-DwightFalkofske
comments.body:
Tobias,

Thank you for taking time to look at this.

We have tried several Xbox Controller to keyboard emulators, of which we like this one the best:
Total Game Control - http://www.digitaltransforms.com/

I've tried several different ones, but none have been able to emulate to Busmaster.

I appreciate any help you can provide.  Our demo for the kids is on Saturday.

Many thanks!
Dwight Falkofske

---

Sent: Tuesday, February 18, 2014 4:19 AM
To: rbei-etas/busmaster
Cc: Falkofske, Dwight [USA]

we discussed this today during our developer's telco. We are using AutoIt for GUI testing, which also simulates keypresses. This works for us.

Can you send us a link to the Xbox key press emulation software? We'll try to find out what is different and whether we can modify BUSMASTER to work with it.

Thanks,
Tobias

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
Hi Dwight,

currently we use the function "PreTranslateMessage" to detect key presses. We look for alternatives. However we are already in the testing phases for our next release on Friday. So we need some look to manage it till Friday/Saturday. Let's press thumbs :-)

Bye
Tobias

comments.author: BAH-DwightFalkofske
comments.body:
Tobias,

Thanks for the insight.
We eventually found a third-party emulator that worked with Busmaster.
Xpadder - http://www.xpadder.com/

I'm glad our demo will continue as planned!

Many thanks!
Dwight

comments.body: We are using Windows 7 and found that putting the emulator into Windows Compatibility Mode helped the problem.  When we tried it on a computer with WinXP, it had no issues.  I hope that helps to isolate the cause.

title: In Node simulation when you try to Build a simulated system which is already loaded during runtime an error message is displayed.
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Load a cfx.
2.Create a simulated system and add node.
3.Add a cpp file and add any handler.
4.Build and Load. Enable handler.
5.Execute the handler.
6.Now click on Build.
An error message is displayed as "File is not properly built.Please Unload the dll and try again".
body_text:
1.Load a cfx.
2.Create a simulated system and add node.
3.Add a cpp file and add any handler.
4.Build and Load. Enable handler.
5.Execute the handler.
6.Now click on Build.
An error message is displayed as "File is not properly built.Please Unload the dll and try again".

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: This is the intended behavior. Following is the functionality Build and Build and Load buttons when the Dll is already loaded: Build: Build will try to compile the file. If the dll is already loaded, the dll has to be unloaded first before compiling. Build and Load: This will first unload the dll, build and load the dll These options will be removed in one of the upcoming versions and compile and loading of dll will be taken care by BUSMASTER application directly without any user intervention.

title: J1939 message handler is not executing for PGN Id range.
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Create a cfx.
2.Activate J1939.
3.Add a sim file or create a new sim file. Add node and add cpp file.
4.Add a message handler for PGN ID range (For ex 0x100 to 0x500)
5.Build and Load. Enable message handler.
6.Connect. 
7.Transmit a frame with PGN 0x400.

Message handler is not getting executed at this point

title: J1939  Acitvate/Deactivate icon is not getting changed to deactivated state when you change hardware/Driver selection
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Load a cfx.
2.Select CAN->Driver selection->Vector XL virtual channels.
3.Now connect. Activate J1939.
4.Select J1939 transmit window. and transmit any message.
5.Now donot close Transmit window of J1939.
6.Disconnect CAN.
7.Select CAN->Driver selection->Kvaser CAN virtual channels.

You can observe that J1939 will enter to deactivated state, but toolbar still shows activated state andmenu item for Activate/Deactivate is still shown as "Activate" instead of ( J1939->Deactivate).  When you click on Activate/Deactivate J1939 toolbar, it is not changing back to Deactivated state.You need to click on it twice to disable it.

title: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC
author: etas-japan-yamamoto
body:
BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC if "\BUSMASTER_v2.0.0\JPN" folder exists.
If the folder was renamed BUSMASTER starts up in English mode.
At least the issue occurs on Win7 64bit Japanese machine, other types of OS are not tested.
body_text:
BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC if "\BUSMASTER_v2.0.0\JPN" folder exists.
If the folder was renamed BUSMASTER starts up in English mode.
At least the issue occurs on Win7 64bit Japanese machine, other types of OS are not tested.

comments.author: etas-japan-yamamoto
comments.body:
V210 has the same error. 
Japanese customers want to use the latest version in Japanese mode.

comments.author: etas-japan-sakurai
comments.body:
Same issue is still available at V2.5.0.

The workaround (=removal/rename of "JPN" folder) works. If all Japanese PC cannot run BusMaster, there's no reason to add "JPN" folder in the package, I think. My proposal is just to remove it until fixing it.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: Yes, I agree that this is reasonable. I'll discuss this with my developers today.

comments.author: etas-japan-sakurai
comments.body:
### CORRECTION

V2.5.0 is trying to show some of items in menu (ex. Properties etc.) in Japanese, even if "JPN" folder was removed. So I rolled back to V2.4.0 -- it's fine now. Menu has been shown in English.
Therefore, please be careful: just removing "JPN" folder is not really good option.
(Mr. Lorenz: Thank you for your immediate response.)

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: I've just discussed this with my developers. A fix is planned. Instead of removing the JPN folder we can also give these "Language Packs" as installer option. So customers can choose whether this should be installed or not. The same is actually true for German language pack as well.

comments.author: etas-japan-sakurai
comments.body: Just FYI -- V2.6.1 works on my Japanese version of Windows 7 64bit, without the workaround above (=removal/rename of "JPN" folder), with Japanese Language Pack which was automatically installed together.

title: Busmaster crashes when you execute any handler, disconnect and change hardware selection to any other hardware and click on Load all button and then click on Enable All handlers.
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Load a cfx.
2.Select Vector XL virtual channels.
3.Go to CAN->Configure Simulated systems.
3.Add a sim file. Add a node and add any handlers say message handler (for all Rx messages and add send function for the handler).
4.Build cpp file. Donot close Configure Simulated system window
5.Now click on Load All toolbar and then on Enable All handlers toolbar.
6.Execute handler for some time.
7.Now Disconnect.
8.Change hardware to simulation by selecting CAN->Diver Selection->Simulation
9.Now Connect.
10.click on Load All toolbar and then on Enable All handlers toolbar.

Application crashes when you repeat the same procedure few times.

title: Memory leak when open Test Automation Editor screen many times
author: RBVH-Thinh
body:
Step to re-produce.
1- Open BusMaster 2.0 program. The Test Automation Editor will be opened automatically.
2- Go to menu File > Exit to close Test Automation Editor
3- Go to menu File > Test Automation > New Editor to open a new Test Automation Editor
4- Go to menu File > Exit to close Test Automation Editor
5- Perform step 3 to step 4 many times, the Test Automation window will continue to remain instead of closed properly. Memory leak will occur after open/close may screen.
Refer to image in enclosed file.

title: annoying debug rebuilds with F5
author: PatRuelle
body:
I had previously build v1.7 with VS2008. I had downloaded v2.0 and rebuild it with VSX2012 for both release and busmaster_debug. Both builds had an error in that UDS_Protocol.lib could not be found. Since UDS support is schedule for v2.1, I removed "$(SolutionDir)/BIN/Libs/$(OutDir)UDS_Protocol.lib" from both projects via:
link > All Options > Additional Options
When I went to debug with F5 it reported that  FLEXRAY_ETAS_BOA - busmaster_debug Win32 was out of date. I let it build it; several projects were rebuilt. This happens every time I do an F5 even though I haven't changed anything. Any ideas what is causing this? The rebuilds with every F5 is both annoying and time consuming.

title: Node simulation procedure
author: somavikram
body:
I referred to the help document provided for node simulation. I didnt get clear understanding on adding node. For adding node dll required which dll should be added there. how should that be generated?..

And once i load a sample dll and try to add my cpp file(capl to cpp converted) i get an error of unable to include mingw compiler, though i made proper environment variable settings.

Need input on the above settings too.
body_text:
I referred to the help document provided for node simulation. I didnt get clear understanding on adding node. For adding node dll required which dll should be added there. how should that be generated?..

And once i load a sample dll and try to add my cpp file(capl to cpp converted) i get an error of unable to include mingw compiler, though i made proper environment variable settings.

Need input on the above settings too.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
Adding node dll option is to be used when already the dll is created. If it is a new node that is being created, then skip the adding node dll option and directly create the CPP file in the next step.

Please download and install MinGw as updated in help file with C and C++ options selected. If it is already installed, please type gcc in the command prompt and check if the following message is displayed:

gcc: fatal error: no input files
compilation terminated.

Please let us know your inputs.

comments.author: somavikram
comments.body:
![mingw](https://f.cloud.github.com/assets/6032371/1631586/5acb6584-5768-11e3-9309-a4a7cdab0591.png)
![mingw_busmaster](https://f.cloud.github.com/assets/6032371/1631597/cac9a260-5768-11e3-8bf0-33e0efad4c80.png)

Please find the error attachments..
help me in resolving..the above issues

comments.author: celsoluiz81
comments.body:
1- Install MinGW.

2- After installation is complete,then Right-click on "My Computer" icon. Select "Properties".

3- In the Properties Window, select "Avanced Settings" on the left sidebar (Windows 7).

4-  Click on the "Environment Variables" button located at the bottom left of the "Advanced Settings" window.

5- Click on "Path" variable on the tpo list and then click on the edit button.

6- Add ;C:\MinGW\bin to the end of the string and click OK all the way to exit all the privious windows

7- RESTART your Windows!! On my computer it only worked after I restarted.

8- After system restart,run Busmaster and it should now find minGW.

comments.author: RBEI-VENKAT
comments.body:
Issue solved after installing MinGW.
So, closing the issue.

title: FlexRay : Messages are not in expanded stated when we switch to append mode and back to overwrite mode.
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
Messages are not in expanded stated when we switch to append mode and back to overwrite mode.
1. Create new configuration.
2. Add frames and connect
3. Now enable message interpretation.
4. Expand a message.(msg window will be overwrite mode)
5. Now disable overwrite mode and enable it
We can observe that message interpretation is disabled and message is not expanded
body_text:

title: Support message interpretation in Non-Overwrite mode
author: AnilChoudhury-Eaton
body:
Hi,
Its getting difficult to analyze the J1939 messages in the message window.

I am only able to enable message interpretaion in overwrite mode. This only helps in follow changing data parameters.

But when I want to analyze various commands and responses that are exchanged, then i can't use overwrite mode. It only shows the last set of messages. I need message interpretation in this mode so that I can analyze all requests and their respective responses.

At least this can be enabled automatically when the communication is disconnected.

Regards
Anil Choudhury

title: Crash Loading/Importing database file

body:
I was given a database file to use for a CAN application. When I open the database file the application freezes and does not recover. Looking through the help file an 'import' feature is discussed. The sample DB file I used (built using busmaster):
//*****************************_BUSMASTER Messages and signals Database ***_**************************//

[DATABASE_VERSION] 1.3

title: BUSMASTER crash after Start/stop timer handler(s)
author: mjohannes
body:
Busmaster v1.9.0
Win XP SP 3

Busmaster crashes after starting all timer handlers via toolbar node simulation.

I simulate a system with 17 nodes. Each node got time handler, message handler or both. After connecting to the bus, all .dlls are loaded. Now klicking on the "Start/stop all timer handler(s)" button BUSMASTER will crash. If i enable the message handlers first via the toolbar, BUSMASTER will not crash.

Starting timer handlers in the configure simulated system window leads to the same effect. If i'm trying to start the handlers via the context menu in the configure simulated system, nothing happens. I got to start the handlers first with the buttons in the toolbar (message handler first, as described above).
Sometimes a message appears "DBGHELP.DLL not found". dbghelp.dll is installed. I did this http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/ms679294(v=vs.85).aspx  to fix it, but the error still occures.

Enableling each handler manually in the node detail window, everything works fine.

Sincerely 
Matthias Johannes

title: Support for multiplexed signals in CAN messages
author: etas-lorenz
body:
At least in CAN multiplexed signals are used quiet often. This is currently not supported and hinders some customers to use BUSMASTER.

Background: In a CAN message a signal can be defined as multiplexor. This multiplexor selects which multiplexed signal is transmitted in the same frame.

The CANdb formats supports multiplex configurations.
body_text:

title: Direct DBC support
author: etas-lorenz
body:
I propose to implement direct support for DBC format without intermediate DBF format. 

Support for data handling usually consists of a data model and load/save functionality from/to the data model. The data model should already be very similar for DBC and DBF, so can probably remain unchanged. Only the load/save functionality must be rewritten.

Advantages:
- We can get rid of the DBC>DBF>DBC format converts. This reduces our maintenance efforts.
- The user experience improves as no format conversion is necessary anymore.
- The compatibility with the DBC format should also increased with this.

Disadvantages:
- Migration effort for the load/save functionality necessary.
- Migration effort for the database editor necessary.
- Migration effort for the CAPL importer necessary (DBC->struct.h).

title: The result text of the converter in case of fomat converters should be same for all conversion types
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Open Format Converters dialog.
2.Select Other Converters tab.
3.Select DBC to DBF conversion.
4.Add a dbc file. Click on convert. You can observe that result of the conversion is shown as "Conversion completed." 

5.Select ASC TO LOG conversion.Add input .asc file. Click on Convert.
You can observe that Conversion result is being shown as "Conversion Completed Successfully"

Then result message of conversion should be same for all conversion types.

title: m_ulTimeStamp does not seem to contain the complete timestamp into
author: ghi9fe
body:
I am creating a c script to run on BUSMASTER. I can get everything to work apart from timestamp.
I want to write the CAN massage inthe Trace window withthe same timestamp as the real CAN message in the  Message Window. This way I can easily link the messages. But when I try to Trace for example RxMsg.m_ulTimeStamp, I do not see all the info. 
So the time stamp in Message Window is 16:10:30:3663. But writing RxMsg.m_ulTimeStamp as decimal gives me 60083, and in Hex eab3.
How can I write the complete message tiemsatmp in the Trace window?

title: DBC to DBF converter creates wrong result
author: mcrae-beg
body:
I use Busmaster V1.8.1. When I tried to convert a DBC to a DBF the minimum, maximum, offset and scale factor was wrong in the DBF-file. Afterwards I have created a very simple DBC file with CANalyzer that includes only one message to test the converter again. The DBC-file looks like follows:

BO_ 1 Message: 6 Vector__XXX
 SG_ signal1 : 27|12@1+ (0.045332,0) [0|185.63454] "-"  Knoten

After using the converter the DBF file looks like:
[START_MSG] Message,1,6,1,1,S,
[START_SIGNALS] signal1,12,4,3,U,4095,0,1,45332,0,-,,Knoten
[END_MSG]

That means the offset of the DBF file is 45332 and not 0.045332.

The bugreport says: 
MSG_ID: 1   MSG_TYPE: S     MSG_NAME: Message
    SIG_NAME: signal1, STATUS: Invalid Max or Min value., ACTION: Value Set to default

title: Node Simulation - Messages are not send
author: mcrae-beg

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: SendMsg API call in Node Simulation is actually a Tx frame which will be sent to the hardware if connected. For Message window, frames received from hardware or node simulation is considered as a received frame and is displayed with Rx flag.

title: Extended id/CAN id displayed in the message window is not proper when a message is transmitted through  node simulation J1939, provided PGN parameters are specified in message structure.
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Activate J1939
2.Select J1939->Node Simulation->Configure
3.Add/create a simulated system and add a node.
4.Add timer handler and give delay as 1000ms.
5.Define message structure for J1939 in the handler.
6.Define PGN parameters such as PDU Format,PDU specific,Reserved bit and Data page.
7. Also define parameters such as Source Address and Priority.
8.Build and Load.
9.Connect and Enable handler.
10.You can observe that message that has been configured in simulated system is transmitted with the given delay. But CAN ID field is not proper. The CANID value that is being displayed is not proper according to PGN parameters given.

6.Define PGN parameters such as PDU Format,PDU specific,Reserved bit and Data page.

8.Build and Load.
9.Connect and Enable handler.
10.You can observe that message that has been configured in simulated system is transmitted with the given delay. But CAN ID field is not proper. The CANID value that is being displayed is not proper according to PGN parameters given.

comments.author: RBEI-VENKAT
comments.body: 1.Regarding "(Message Not Found)" Error: D_RQ_MS, D_RS_MS are the database messages defined in CANoe database(.dbc) file.You have to associate the dbc file while converting.Use "Add DBC File" button to associate the same. 2. Regarding "(Unsupported Function)" Error: From CAPL Documentation, fileReadArray,fileReadInt,fileWriteInt are Obsolete and they are replaced with getProfileArray, getProfileInt, getWriteInt Simultaneously and they are supported in BUSMASTER. 3. Regarding (Unsupported Key Handler) Error: Some CANoe Key Handlers are not directly supported in BUSMASTER.Those Key handlers has to be mapped to the BUSMASTER supported keys.While Converting a dialog box (Named Key Mapping) will displayed where you can map the unsupported keys to BUSMASTER supported keys.

title: PGN and PGN names  displayed in message window are not proper when Messages are transmitted by message handler in J1939.
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Launch Application
2.Activate J1939. Associate a J1939 Database
3.Select J1939->Node simlualtion->Configure.
4.Add a sim file and add cpp file to it.
5.Add a message hanlder for PGN Id  say for ex 0x400 and  Add 10 "SendMsg" functions inside the message handler.
4.Build and Load. Enable message handler
5.Connect and transmit PGN 400.
You can observe that immediately after recieving this PGN all the 10 messages configured in message handler are transmitted and displayed in message window.

But PGN id and PGN name of corresponding messages are not displayed properly. PGN is being displayed as 0x000000 for all the messages. A default name is being shown for all the messages in the message window.
body_text:
1.Launch Application
2.Activate J1939. Associate a J1939 Database
3.Select J1939->Node simlualtion->Configure.

4.Build and Load. Enable message handler
5.Connect and transmit PGN 400.
You can observe that immediately after recieving this PGN all the 10 messages configured in message handler are transmitted and displayed in message window.

But PGN id and PGN name of corresponding messages are not displayed properly. PGN is being displayed as 0x000000 for all the messages. A default name is being shown for all the messages in the message window.

title: Hardware Channels are getting retained from the previous cfx when we load a configuration.
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
Hardware Channels are getting retained from the previous cfx when we load a configuration.
1. Create one configuration. Add one vector channel. Save the cfx.
2. Now create one more configuration. Add both the channels of vector. Save the cfx.
3. Load the cfx created in step 1.
   we can observe that the hardware channels configured in step  2 is retained.
   This happens only if we two cfx are configured with same hardware.
body_text:
Hardware Channels are getting retained from the previous cfx when we load a configuration.
1. Create one configuration. Add one vector channel. Save the cfx.
2. Now create one more configuration. Add both the channels of vector. Save the cfx.
3. Load the cfx created in step 1.
   we can observe that the hardware channels configured in step  2 is retained.
   This happens only if we two cfx are configured with same hardware.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Fixed in v2.6.3

title: BUSMASTER version is not properly logged in the J1939 log file.
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
BUSMASTER version is not properly logged in the J1939 log file in the following scenario.
1. Create a configuration file.
2. Activate and add a log file in overwrite mode.
3. Connect and transmit a "Request Address" message.
4. Disconnect. Open the log file. Version in the log file will be proper.
5. Save the configuration and close the application.
6. Launch the application.
7. Activate, Connect and transmit "Request Address" message.
8. Deactivate and open the log file.
   We can observe that the Version in the log file is incorrect.
body_text:

title: J1939 messages are not logged when we close the application without saving the configuration
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
J1939 messages are not logged in the log file when we close the application without saving the configuration.
1. Activate J1939 and configure a log file.
2. Connect and transmit some messages.
3. Disconnect and check the log file. Messages will be logged.
4. Close the application. Select 'No' in the Save Config dialog.
   Now open the log file. We can observe that messages are not logged.

v1.8.1
body_text:

title: dbf format description?
author: ebroecker
body:
Is there any description of the .dbf-fileformat?
Does .dbf support multiple transmitters of the same frame (BO_TX_BU_ in .dbc)?

Background of this question is my attempt to write some universal canmatrix-utility (https://github.com/ebroecker/canmatrix) which supports among other features the conversion between several canmatrix-formats (dbc, dbf, kcd, xls, arxml).
body_text:
Is there any description of the .dbf-fileformat?
Does .dbf support multiple transmitters of the same frame (BO_TX_BU_ in .dbc)?

Background of this question is my attempt to write some universal canmatrix-utility (https://github.com/ebroecker/canmatrix) which supports among other features the conversion between several canmatrix-formats (dbc, dbf, kcd, xls, arxml).

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
Multiple transmitters support for the same frame is not available at present. 

Following is the DBF file format assuming the version is 1.7.0 and a CAN database file.

//*****************************_BUSMASTER Messages and signals Database ***_**************************//

[DATABASE_VERSION] 1.3

[PROTOCOL] CAN

[BUSMASTER_VERSION] [1.7.0]
[NUMBER_OF_MESSAGES] 1

[START_MSG] Message Name, Message ID, Length, Number of Signals, Data Format, Frame Format
[START_SIGNALS]
Signal Name, length, byte number, start bit number, signal type, Max value, Min Value, Byte order (1-Intel), offset, factor, Unit \
[VALUE_DESCRIPTION] Description Name, Description Value
[END_MSG]

comments.author: ManfredUrban
comments.body:
The description is not up to date
If you convert a Vector database with multiplexed messages, multiplexers are also to be seen in dbf database in the last position of the definition. This position is missing in the actual description.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: Multiplexed messages are not yet supported. This feature request, also mentioned in issue #587 and issue #676, is still open.

comments.author: pedro-caetano
425J
comments.body: @RBEI-Saravanan I am also looking for documentation of the dbf file format. I would be very grateful for an explanation of what could serve as a reference.

title: Cannot receive messages with CANCaseXL
author: davemole
body:
I'm using BUSMASTER v1.8.1 with a CANcaseXL.  I've installed the Vector driver (V8.3) and the XL Driver Library (V8.5.3).  Within BUSMASTER, I'm able to select and configure the Vector XL hardware.  If I transmit a message from BUSMASTER, it's successfully received by my device.  If I transmit a message to BUSMASTER from my device, I get an error frame on BUSMASTER.  The baud rate is correct, and the Rx and Tx LEDS on the CANCase are blinking green in all cases.

If I use the same set-up, but switch to a ValueCAN interface instead of a CANCase interface, I'm able to send and receive without error.
body_text:
I'm using BUSMASTER v1.8.1 with a CANcaseXL.  I've installed the Vector driver (V8.3) and the XL Driver Library (V8.5.3).  Within BUSMASTER, I'm able to select and configure the Vector XL hardware.  If I transmit a message from BUSMASTER, it's successfully received by my device.  If I transmit a message to BUSMASTER from my device, I get an error frame on BUSMASTER.  The baud rate is correct, and the Rx and Tx LEDS on the CANCase are blinking green in all cases.

If I use the same set-up, but switch to a ValueCAN interface instead of a CANCase interface, I'm able to send and receive without error.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Please check with latest version 2.1.0 and let us know your feedback.

title: .def file is not created when new node is added to simulated system
author: davemole
body: I'm trying to create a simulated system.  My node builds successfully, but will not load because the .def file does not exist.  Should the def file be created automatically when the node is added to the simulated system, or do I need to create it manually?
body_text: I'm trying to create a simulated system.  My node builds successfully, but will not load because the .def file does not exist.  Should the def file be created automatically when the node is added to the simulated system, or do I need to create it manually?

comments.author: RBEI-VENKAT
comments.body:
.def file will be created automatically if .cpp file is generated from Node simulation editor.
Other wise we have to create it manually.
Can you give us the steps how you generated the node simulation files and BUSMASTER version?

comments.author: davemole
comments.body:
Thanks for the reply.  I have BUSMASTER V1.8.1
1. Click CAN -> Node Simulation -> Configure
2. Right Click -> New Simulated System
3. Right Click -> Add Node
4. Click Add New File...

I just discovered that if I save my simulated system files into a root directory, the def file is created automatically.  If I save the simulated system files into a user directory, the def file is NOT created.

With simulation saved in C:\Projects\Busmaster, I have the files:
Busmaster.sim
ECM.cpp
ECM.def

With simulation saved in C:\Users\firstname.lastname, I have the files:

Busmaster.sim
ECM.cpp

---

 From: Venkatanarayana Makam notifications@github.com
To: rbei-etas/busmaster busmaster@noreply.github.com 
Cc: davemole davemole2@yahoo.com 
Sent: Tuesday, August 13, 2013 9:59 AM
Subject: Re: [busmaster] .def file is not created when new node is added to simulated system (#559)

.def file will be created automatically if .cpp file is generated from Node simulation editor.
Other wise we have to create it manually.
Can you give us the steps how you generated the node simulation files and BUSMASTER version?
—
Reply to this email directly or view it on GitHub.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
This issue is not reproducible with the steps mentioned above. When the file is created the .def file will be automatically created.

Is there any other information you could send to us ?

comments.author: davemole
comments.body:
After moving the simulation from the user directory to the root directory, the problem went away, so I haven't spent any more time investigating.  I can tell you it was consistent.  If the *.sim file was saved in a user directory on my computer, the *.def file was not created.  I repeated the process multiple times.  My guess was that it was either a permission issue or a "long path name" issue.  I'm willing to help if there's anything you'd like me to try.

On Tuesday, October 22, 2013 8:23 AM, RBEI-Saravanan notifications@github.com wrote:

This issue is not reproducible with the steps mentioned above. When the file is created the .def file will be automatically created.
Is there any other information you could send to us ?
—
Reply to this email directly or view it on GitHub.

title: Graph Window gets reset after some time
author: etas-lorenz
body:
This is based on customer feedback.

The graph resets about every minute.  So when go back to review the data, it's not possible to see into the past before the reset. Changing the buffer size of the graph changes the frequency of this happening, but it does not eliminate the issue.

title: (Incremental) Search function for signal graph "select element" dialog.
author: etas-lorenz
body:
This is based on customer feedback.

If you have a large CAN database, selecting the right messages and signals in the "Select Element" dialog of the signal graph functionality takes time. This can improved by a (incremental) search field that acts on the signal elements.

This might be true for other signal selection dialogs as well.

title: "All Key Handlers" menu is checked even though the handler is disabled.
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
"All Key Handlers" menu is checked even though the handler is disabled.
1. Add a key handler
2. Build and Load the cpp file
3. Enable the key handler. "All Key Handlers" menu will be checked
4. Save the configuration
5. Unload the file. "All Key Handlers" menu will still be in checked state
6. Again Build and Load the cpp file.
   We can observe that "All Key Handlers" menu is checked even though the Key handler is disabled.

title: Maximum value of the PGN for J1939 messages
author: bentearadu
body:
Maximum PGN for J1939 messages should be FFFF, not 3FFFF, due to the fact the last 2 bites from the byte 3 are not used, (values 3, 2, 1, 0 does not have any effect on the final ID)

See SAE J1939. EDP should be 0 and also DP should be 0 in order for the CAN 29 Bit to be J1939 based.
body_text:
Maximum PGN for J1939 messages should be FFFF, not 3FFFF, due to the fact the last 2 bites from the byte 3 are not used, (values 3, 2, 1, 0 does not have any effect on the final ID)

See SAE J1939. EDP should be 0 and also DP should be 0 in order for the CAN 29 Bit to be J1939 based.

comments.author: RBEI-Prathiba
comments.body:
Maximum PGN for J1939 messages is 3FFFF as PGN is of 18bit.
Please refer BUSMASTER help "J1939" Section for more details about PGN.

comments.body: Fix for this issue is released with v2.2.0

title: Error time displaying the relative time non-overwrite mode
author: bentearadu
body:
When a cyclic message is sent from "Configure Transmission Messages" window, and the "relative time" display mode is chose, and also "overwrite mode" is Enable, the cyclic time is correct (e.g. 2 seconds)
If the "overwrite mode" is changed to Disable, then the time is not shown like it suppose to.

This error happens only if there are data received from the real ECU.

print screen files were sent Saravanan.

title: GCC installation: Hint missing that C++ must be explicitely enabled by the user.
author: etas-lorenz
body:
The GCC installation must be done manually and links are given sufficiently. That's ok.

However it's important to give the user a hint that the C++ compiler has to be enabled manually (disabled per default). Otherwise the node simulation build step complains about the missing g++ compiler.
body_text:
The GCC installation must be done manually and links are given sufficiently. That's ok.

However it's important to give the user a hint that the C++ compiler has to be enabled manually (disabled per default). Otherwise the node simulation build step complains about the missing g++ compiler.

title: Signal Graph badly corrupted.
author: JohnGC
body:
Several issues;
- Manual axis scaling will only scale X or XY but not Y only.
- Trace colour incorrect - see attached screen capture.
- Trace images become corrupt drawing lines up to the top or bottom left hand side for several minutes - see attached.
- Trace colours sometimes change when clicking on the description in the Element list.

This is on a Win 7 Home 64-bit.
One instance.

title: Automatically load Node Simulation DLLs from the project configuration
author: etas-lorenz
body:
This was discussed in the telco today, following the discussion of issue #496.

The project configuration contains also the Node Simulation programs. Currently these DLLs need to be loaded manually. The user would save some steps, if the DLLs would be loaded automatically either on project load time or on connect time.

title: the graph window configuarotion -buffer size 50000 at most
author: pangzitong867
body:
Question1:I can export the graph to the CSV format docment when i disconnect the CAN bus and click the "export"button.The configuarotion for the buffer size is 50000 entries at most.Sometimes the graph refresh to a new one and discard the former data,maybe it is due to the small buffer size.I did not know whether the buffer is full or near to the top capacity.

Can you enlarge the buffer size to record more data and show the capacity percentage so I can disconnect the bus in advance if the buffer capacity is near to 100%.

Question 2:The ECU transmits the engine speed and throttle position at different cycle time.One is 10 ms and the other is 50 ms.Can you give an optional option to select the sample cycle time,so I can record the engine speed and throttle position at the same cycle time -50ms.
body_text:
Question1:I can export the graph to the CSV format docment when i disconnect the CAN bus and click the "export"button.The configuarotion for the buffer size is 50000 entries at most.Sometimes the graph refresh to a new one and discard the former data,maybe it is due to the small buffer size.I did not know whether the buffer is full or near to the top capacity.

Can you enlarge the buffer size to record more data and show the capacity percentage so I can disconnect the bus in advance if the buffer capacity is near to 100%.

Question 2:The ECU transmits the engine speed and throttle position at different cycle time.One is 10 ms and the other is 50 ms.Can you give an optional option to select the sample cycle time,so I can record the engine speed and throttle position at the same cycle time -50ms.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
Answer 1: Yes, currently the graph data is kept in a buffer, which is not a ring buffer. So if it's full, the graph is reset. In my opinion, instead of changing the buffer size, we should find a way to implement a ring buffer.

Answer 2: IMO the data is stored whenever data is received. This is a useful behavior. Setting a cycle time for the capture is a new feature.

title: Text not updated in splash screen
author: prasooncheenan
body: The text in busmaster installer/startup splash screen still shows copyright 2003.This can be updated.
body_text: The text in busmaster installer/startup splash screen still shows copyright 2003.This can be updated.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
Yes and no.

Copyright year: The copyright years usually list the most important functional changes. 2003 refers to the old in-house tool. BUSMASTER started in 2011. Therefore I propose: Copyright 2011.

Copyright owner: The initial version was 100% owned by RBEI. Since then every change is jointly owned by RBEI+ETAS. Therefore we should list both: "Copyright 2011 ETAS GmbH and Robert Bosch Engineering and Business Solutions Ltd".

Copyright mark: Personally I would like to see the software owned by a developer foundation. Therefore I don't like the copyright to be obviously placed in the splash screen. We should remove it there and just leave it in the "Help->About" window.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: BUSMASTER is currently based upon MFC (version 4.2 in the Express/WinDDK version). This is an technology is not state-of-the-art and hinders migration onto other platforms (embedded, linux, ...). Therefore there is the intention to migrate the backend non-GUI functionality from MFC to C++11. This is especially regarding several C... classes, not only CString.

comments.author: liguangcheng
comments.body: oh woo, I understand ,but what should I do? replace parts of file with what? I still need a help

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: Sorry. Now I understand your question. We use WinDDK to add the MFC functionality missing from the Visual Studio Express versions. The MFC however is old and contains several bugs. Therefore we provide a patch file that automates the process to patch your WinDDK/MFC installation. Please have a look at "Documents/1 Development Environment/files/WinDDK_7600.16385.1_patch.bat". This should apply all mentioned changes in one step. Does this help?

comments.author: liguangcheng
comments.body: oh, run the path file can apply all mentioned changes that descripted in document file in one step,is that ture? when I build the project there are many many errors,oh my gord!

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: Yes, if you apply the patch script to an unmodified installation of WinDDK (C:\WinDDK\7600.16385.1), it applies cleanly. I guess this worked for you as you now see the compilation warnings/errors of BUSMASTER. Yes, there is still some work to do... The warnings often give hints on unsafe functions. However tests have shown that this hasn't much influence on the tool currently. The work to reduce these issues is on a lower priority therefore. But we are looking for volunteers :-) It's not complicated, just a lot to do.

comments.author: liguangcheng
comments.body: sorry,I still need your help.I build the project in the Visual C++ 2008, there is a fatal error always exist,the error is "fatal error C1083: can not open include file ..\at171\atldef.h, No such file or directory".I can not understand why the error appears because I modify the Atlconv.h and atldef.h files first.please give me a suggestion that show me how to resolve the problem

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
VC++ Express requires a WinDDK installation to have MFC+ATL. The Non-Express versions already contain MFC+ATL, whoever in much different versions! In your case there is probably no need to install WinDDK and make changes to VC++ settings to pick it up.

You have now two options:
1. Try to use your VC++ Non-Express version without WinDDK, but then you eventually have to do changes to the sources codes. This is not only about the warnings that are often raised to errors in the Non-Express version, but also regarding different MFC+ATL calls. I would be happy if someone tries this way. However it's harder...
2. Install VC++2008 Express along with WinDDK. So just follow the Development Environment Documentation and BUSMASTER should compile cleanly. If not, then we have to fix this document :-)

comments.body: As described in the Development Environment documentation, you can apply the diff file to your WinDDK installation by executing the script WinDDK_7600.16385.1_patch.bat. Or unapply it by the corresponding _unpatch.bat script.

title: In J1939,when there is a simulated system present,  when you create a new configuration file, the simulated system is still retained in new configuration also.
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
Scenario1: When new cfx file is created in the same directory as that of old cfx and sim file
1.Load a cfx file for ex from location D:\Cfx_files. Activate J1939
2.Create a simulated system for ex sim123.cfx in the same directory for ex D:\Cfx_files.Add a node.Build and load.
3.Save config. Now Select File->New. Create a new config file as for ex  newconfig.cfx in the same directory for ex D:\Cfx_files\

You can observe that sim123.cfx is still retained in the new config also. 
And also you can observe that J1939 window is still retained. and when you select menu J1939, you can observe that J1939 menu item shows "Activate" instead of "Deactivate"

Scenario2 :When new cfx file is created in different location than that of old cfx and sim files.
1.Load a cfx file. Activate J1939
2.Create a simulated system for ex sim123.cfx.Add a node.Build and load.
3.Save config. Now Select File->New. Create a new config file as newconfig.cfx in different location say C:\cfx_files\ . An error message pops up as "C:\cfx_files\Sim123 was not found"

And also you can observe that J1939 window is still retained. and when you select menu J1939, you can observe that J1939 menu item shows "Activate" instead of "Deactivate"

title: Support DSRC protocol
author: RBEI-Srinivas
body:
Dedicated Short Range Communication (DSRC) offers vehicle-to-vehicle (v-v) and vehicle-to-
infrastructure (v-i) communications between automobiles. The communication between On-board unit(OBU) and roadside unit (RSU) is wireless and the packets exchanged have to be monitored.

first I want to congratulate you to this versatile and interesting tool! This is really an alternative to Vector after little improvements. The user interface has shown major improvements since the v1.8.

But now to my problem:
I am user of the common DBC-format and have converted my file into the DBF-format. Some warnings appeared after the conversion regarding invalid MIN and MAX values.

Then I have checked the differences between both message layouts and found out, that the scale factor of the DBF signal was wrong. Every signal with a scale factor <1 in the DBC-File has been set  to 0.0 in the DBF-File during the conversion.

This difference prevents a correct interpretation of the CAN-signals, so this is very dangerous.

Attached you can find a screenshot with comparison of both CAN Message Layouts.

Thanks and Regards,
Claus
body_text:
Dear ladies and gentlemen, 

first I want to congratulate you to this versatile and interesting tool! This is really an alternative to Vector after little improvements. The user interface has shown major improvements since the v1.8.

But now to my problem:
I am user of the common DBC-format and have converted my file into the DBF-format. Some warnings appeared after the conversion regarding invalid MIN and MAX values.

Then I have checked the differences between both message layouts and found out, that the scale factor of the DBF signal was wrong. Every signal with a scale factor <1 in the DBC-File has been set  to 0.0 in the DBF-File during the conversion.

This difference prevents a correct interpretation of the CAN-signals, so this is very dangerous.

Attached you can find a screenshot with comparison of both CAN Message Layouts.

Thanks and Regards,
Claus

title: DBC to DBF converter: Signal scale factor gets truncated
author: etas-lorenz
body: The DBC to DBF converter truncates signal scale factors at the comma. That means that a signal from the DBC database with scale 0,25 is converted to the DBF database with a scale factor of 0. This leads to wrong values.
body_text: The DBC to DBF converter truncates signal scale factors at the comma. That means that a signal from the DBC database with scale 0,25 is converted to the DBF database with a scale factor of 0. This leads to wrong values.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: While this issue is regarding the import, we already fixed the same for the export in #378.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
This is related to scale factor and offset factor.

The problem seems to be the used conversion function (atof), which is language-dependent.

title: Node Simulation OnTimer() Handler does not stop after disconnect
author: MasterAndre

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: This sounds like a bug that we need to address in the next release.

comments.author: RBEI-AshwinRU
comments.body:
Hi Andre,
          Connect/Disconnect will not have any effect on Timer handlers. You can start a timer handler even in disconnected state or stop the same. This is the intended behavior. If there is any deviation from the above behavior, please let us know.

Regards,
Ashwin

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
We discussed this in the telco today.

Most other tools automatically enable all handlers on connect and disable all handlers on disconnect. This is the expected behavior. We can keep the current behavior as an (non-default) option.

title: Hex/Decimal mode of display is not getting saved with the configuration
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Configure some messages in the TX window.
2.Connect and transmit. 
3.Disconnect. Now click on the icon Hex or decimal display.
4.Connect and transmit again.

You can observe that messages are displayed in in message window in decimal format.

5.Save the configuration.
6.Close application and start Busmaster again.
7.Connect and transmit.

You can observe that messages are getting displayed in hex format, even though it was set to decimal mode and saved.

title: Network statistics and Node simulation windows are  getting retained when we load different configuration files
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
V1.8.0 Beta

Scenario1 (Network statistics window)

1.Start Application
2.Click on Show/Hide Network Statistics window tool bar.
3.Save the configuration and Load a different configuration file.

You can observe that Network statistics window is retained in the new configuration file.
1. Now close the Network statistics window. and save the configuration.
   5.Load any other configuration file. 
   You can observe that Network statistics window still retained even though it was closed in the previous configuration file.
   It is retained irrespective of whichever configuration files we load.

Scenario2(Configure Simulated systems window)

1.Select CAN->Node Simulation->configure.
2.Save the configuration and Load a different configuration file.

You can observe that configure Simulated systems window is retained in the new configuration.

title: Node Simulation Dll is not unloaded properly when we try to create a new configuration.
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
Node Simulation Dll is not unloaded properly when we try to create a new configuration.

Scenario 1:
1. Create a CAN simulated system with key handler. Add a trace message to it.
2. Build and load the file.
3. Enable Key handler.
4. Now create a new configuration. Save changes message pops up. Click on 'Yes'. And also Save simulated system message appears. Click on 'Yes'.
5. Create a CAN Simulated system in new config file.
6. Now press the key declared in the key handler in step 1.
We can observe that the key handler from the previous config is getting triggered.

Scenario 2:
1. Create a CAN simulated system with key handler.
2. Build and load the file.
3. Enable Key handler.
4. Now create a new configuration. Save changes message pops up. Click on 'Yes'. And also Save simulated system message appears. Click on 'Yes'.
5. Now load the previous config file using menu File-->Recent Configurations.
6. Try to "Build and Load" the dll.
The file doesn't gets loaded and a error message is displayed in the output window.
body_text:
Node Simulation Dll is not unloaded properly when we try to create a new configuration.

Scenario 1:
1. Create a CAN simulated system with key handler. Add a trace message to it.
2. Build and load the file.
3. Enable Key handler.
4. Now create a new configuration. Save changes message pops up. Click on 'Yes'. And also Save simulated system message appears. Click on 'Yes'.
5. Create a CAN Simulated system in new config file.
6. Now press the key declared in the key handler in step 1.
We can observe that the key handler from the previous config is getting triggered.

Scenario 2:
1. Create a CAN simulated system with key handler.
2. Build and load the file.
3. Enable Key handler.
4. Now create a new configuration. Save changes message pops up. Click on 'Yes'. And also Save simulated system message appears. Click on 'Yes'.
5. Now load the previous config file using menu File-->Recent Configurations.
6. Try to "Build and Load" the dll.
The file doesn't gets loaded and a error message is displayed in the output window.

title: Message Window Menu's like "Time Mode", "Overwrite" should be separated based on the protocol message window..
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
Installer : 1.8.0 Beta

Message Window Menu's like "Time Mode", "Overwrite" etc. should be separated based on the protocol message window.
For ex. Select CAN->Message Window->Overwrite
The overwrite option is enabled for both CAN message window and J1939 message window which is incorrect.
The following "Message Window" sub menu's should be separated out based on the protocol.
1. Overwrite
2. Time Mode
3. Interpret
4. Show Last
5. Reset Columns
body_text:

title: J1939 Node Simulation "All Event Handlers" menu is checked and disabled by default.

J1939 Node Simulation "All Event Handlers" menu is checked and disabled by default.

title: More than 1 OnBus_Connect / Disconect appear in .cpp converted code
author: bentearadu
body:
If the .can file contain in the "Environment" tab envars which contain the words "Start", or "StopMeasurement", then more that 1 OnBus_Connect or OnBus_Disconnect appear in the converted .cpp file.

Since, by now, the environmental variables are not supported, I propose that all the code from "on envar" should be commented.

An example files were sent by e-mail to Saravanan.

title: Junk value is populated when we enter out of range block delay without auto update
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
Junk value is populated when we enter out of range time delay without auto update.
1. Open Tx window.
2. Uncheck autoUpdate.
3. Select Time delay between blocks.
4. Enter time delay as 123456.
5. Validation pop up message appears. Click on "OK".
   We can observe that Time delay value is populated as 10 which is incorrect.

Now check Time delay between messages.
Follow step no. 4 and 5.
Time delay value is populated as 1234 which is incorrect. It should be 12345

title: Busmaster crash - absolute path in cpp file
author: bentearadu
body:
The error is always reproducible.
1. Create a project on the folder c:\FirstFolder
2. Add a simulated node
3. Include in that node "#include c:\FirstFolder\FileName.cpp"
4. Compile the code. -> All errors, are shown in output window
5. MOVE the project from "C:\FirstFolder" to "C:\SecondFolder\"
6. Compile the code.
   7.a. An error window will appear "Following file(s) are either not properly build or loaded: C:\FirstFolder\FileName.cpp" -> Press OK
   7.b. If double click in Output Window on the "Error in opening output file: 3", the following window will appear with the message "No Editor found. Please open the editor using simulated systems window". -> Press OK
7. A new window will appear "Unhandled exception in BUSMASTER. Would you like to save a dump file? -> Press YES/NO (the file is saved/or not)
8. Busmaster crashes.

OS: Windows 7, 64 bit
Busmaster Version 1.7.2 (the is also in 1.7.1)

These might be the exact steps for the issue #404 ?

(Mail sent to Saravanan with the dump file)

title: Busmaster crash
author: ewgenijkkg
body:
Hello. I compiled the current checkout of Busmaster with VS 2008 in release mode. When I start Busmaster and close it afterwards, it crashes with the following output. I did not change anything in the project apart from

1) Removing the compiler optimization
2) Including libxml2-2.7.8.win32 and iconv-1.9.2.win32 libraries

HEAP[BUSMASTER.exe]: Invalid address specified to RtlFreeHeap( 015C0000, 034C2AE8 )
Windows has triggered a breakpoint in BUSMASTER.exe.

This may be due to a corruption of the heap, which indicates a bug in BUSMASTER.exe or any of the DLLs it has loaded.

This may also be due to the user pressing F12 while BUSMASTER.exe has focus.

The output window may have more diagnostic information.
The program '[4792] BUSMASTER.exe: Native' has exited with code 0 (0x0).

Currently BUSMASTER comes with v3.3.2.0.

Genaugenommen ist das Smart-Projekt zwar schon mehr oder weniger abgeschlossen

Aber mittlerweile gehts mir halt auch darum, ein Bindeglied zwischen Charger und BMS zu schaffen,
und das vor allem auch selber konfigurieren zu können. Da ist noch viel zu tun!
