title: Debugging Busmaster
author: ewgenijkkg
body:
Hello. In the development environment manual I read (page 12 Busmaster->Build) that 

"The application can be built and run in release mode only. This means debugging the application is not
possible"

On the next page I read 

"Busmaster_Debug Configuration
This is a new configuration created and customized to enable the users to debug the source code. This will be
used during the development phase to debug the source. Debug format is set to Program Database (/Zi) and .pdb
file generation is also enabled. This is not supported for DMGraph and Format Converters."

So, what is true now? :)
body_text:
Hello. In the development environment manual I read (page 12 Busmaster->Build) that 

"The application can be built and run in release mode only. This means debugging the application is not
possible"

On the next page I read 

"Busmaster_Debug Configuration
This is a new configuration created and customized to enable the users to debug the source code. This will be
used during the development phase to debug the source. Debug format is set to Program Database (/Zi) and .pdb
file generation is also enabled. This is not supported for DMGraph and Format Converters."

So, what is true now? :)

comments.author: Arunkumar-Karri
comments.body:
Hello,

Debugging mode itself is directly not supported by VC++ Express 2008 version for MFC applications. 

Later, we came up with a work around to enable the debug mode for BUSMASTER with introduction of "Busmaster_Debug" configuration mode. So, the above statements needs to be updated.

comments.author: ewgenijkkg
comments.body: OK, thank you for clarification.

comments.body: Fix for this issue is available with v1.7.3 with update to Development Environment document.

title: Messages are not getting transmitted in the following scenario with or without Auto update enabled.
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
V1.7.3 Beta

1.Start Application.
2.Load any configuration file. or create new configuration
3.Select Configure Tx messages.
4.Add two message blocks with few messages with cyclic TX mode.
5.Enter time delay as 1000msec
6.Connect and transmit. Messages are getting transmitted with 1000ms delay between blocks.
7.Now disconnect.
6.Go to Configure Tx messages.
7.Change the time delay between blocks to 1000000msec ie 1000sec.
8.Now connect and transmit.
 All the Messages in the first block get transmitted. It is waiting for 1000sec for the next block to be transmitted. 
9.Now disconnect.
10.Go to configure Tx messages.
11.Change the delay between blocks to 1000msec.
12.Connect and transmit.

You can observe that messages are not getting transmitted.
Issue:2
13. Disconnect. Go to Configure Tx messages.
14.Change the value for delay between blocks to some max value say  111111 or 111112 msec.
15.Close the window.
16.Connect and transmit. You can observe that messages are not getting transmitted.
17.Disconnect and Select Configure Tx messages.
 You can observe that delay between message blocks is changing to some junk value as 2147483647 msec
body_text:
V1.7.3 Beta

1.Start Application.
2.Load any configuration file. or create new configuration
3.Select Configure Tx messages.
4.Add two message blocks with few messages with cyclic TX mode.
5.Enter time delay as 1000msec
6.Connect and transmit. Messages are getting transmitted with 1000ms delay between blocks.
7.Now disconnect.
6.Go to Configure Tx messages.
7.Change the time delay between blocks to 1000000msec ie 1000sec.
8.Now connect and transmit.
 All the Messages in the first block get transmitted. It is waiting for 1000sec for the next block to be transmitted. 
9.Now disconnect.
10.Go to configure Tx messages.
11.Change the delay between blocks to 1000msec.

title: Busmaster always tries to access Windows 7 system configuration
author: tonyathome
body:
Busmaster 1.7.2 under Windows 7 (32 bit): 
After installation Busmaster always crashed, when I tried to save the configuration file.
I tried the Windows 7 problem solving utility and got the suggestion to start Busmaster in Windows XP legacy mode. I followed the suggestion. Since then saving the configuration works.
There is one issue left: Every time when I am starting Busmaster, the Monitor is gets greyed out and  Windows 7 asks for confirmation that this program is allowed to change my system configuration. Is this really necessary? As a result, the Busmaster icon on my desktop also got this blue and yellow shield assigned by Windows.

comments.author: Arunkumar-Karri
comments.body:
This is the default behavior of User Account control on Windows 7 to run a program in Administrator mode. 

If required, these notifications can be disabled by clicking on "Change when these notifications appear" option in the user account control dialog which pops up when clicking on BUSMASTER shortcut and disabling notifications.

Other way to open this window. Type UAC in Windows search bar.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
Yes, BUSMASTER doesn't support getvalue and putvalue capl-functions. 

At present, BUSMASTER doesn't support environment variables and the functions getvalue and putvalue work on environment variables. Hence the conversion is not supported.

Could you give us some input about how the environment variables, getvalue and putvalue functions are used in the capl file ?

title: Feature request: masking in databases
author: SperkLabs
body:
Some protocols have fields in either the CAN ID or the message data that determine message type. Also, some protocols have fields in the ID that do not determine message type, such as priorities and addressing.

For J1939, part of this is handled by using a J1939 database. However, some proprietary J1939 messages use the first 1 or 2 bytes to further specify the message type, and then different message types need to be parsed differently.

I propose that for databases, masks (care / don't care) can be set up for both the message ID and portions of the data.
body_text:
Some protocols have fields in either the CAN ID or the message data that determine message type. Also, some protocols have fields in the ID that do not determine message type, such as priorities and addressing.

For J1939, part of this is handled by using a J1939 database. However, some proprietary J1939 messages use the first 1 or 2 bytes to further specify the message type, and then different message types need to be parsed differently.

I propose that for databases, masks (care / don't care) can be set up for both the message ID and portions of the data.

comments.author: noblevirk
comments.body:
I also support the above proposal. I am currently using controllers that uses propriety format, in which same PGN is used for multiple messages. The messages are differentiated by the priority and sender. In addition to signals within a message can be further identified based upon the byte1 and byte2 values.

I tried implementing the J1939 based database, but it classify multiple messages as one type, even when they have different priority and command bytes.

comments.author: alesiem
comments.body:
I would rate this major improvement since it is important if you work with dynamic node addresses that are part of the CAN ID. 
Please have add this feature.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
This is the intended behavior of the toolbar replay button. 

This is because of the following functionality :
1. Add more than one log file for replay in Replay Configuration window
2. Select interactive replay
3. Connect to the network. Multiple replay windows will be displayed corresponding to the number of log files added for reply. If 2 log files are added then 2 interactive replay windows will be displayed on connect.
4. The replay toolbar functionality works based on the active interactive window. So making it general will not work.

Hence the tool bar is disabled.

comments.body: Closing the issue as this is an intended behavior of BUSMASTER Replay module.

OS Windows: 7

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
In BUSMASTER, if 2 databases having same message name are associated, messages in the first database will be overwritten by the second database message details and the first one will not be retained.

This is the intended.

comments.author: bentearadu
comments.body:
use case:
1. Take 2 databases and a message declared in booth of them, with the same ID, Name, Signals. (message named TestMessage)
2. Add these databases to the project 
3. The files database_name_1_Unions.h and database_name_2_Unions.h are automatically created
4. Build the project
5. The following error will appear: 
                 - database_name_1_Unions.h : Error: Declaration of the "typedef union TestMessage_ TestMessage_" in conflict
                 - database_name_2_Unions.h : Error: "TestMessage_" has a previous declaration as "typedef union TestMessage_ TestMessage_"
                 - database_name_1_Unions.h : Error: Declaration of the "typedef union TestMessage TestMessage" in conflict
                 - database_name_2_Unions.h : Error: "TestMessage" has a previous declaration as "typedef union TestMessage TestMessage"

comments.author: bentearadu
comments.body:
Did you need more info about this?
The error is still there in version 1.7.2

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
As discussed during our joint telco today.

Similar to include guards (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Include_guard), we should enclose each message definition with a "message guard":
#ifndef MessageName_xxx
#define MessageName_xxx
typedef union
...
typedef struct
...
#endif /\* MessageName_xxx */

xxx should be a hash function for everything that is enclosed (message name, signals, types, ...).

That way we prevent identical messages contained in more than one database to create problems during the compilation of node simulations. However if the messages are different but just have the same name, then the compilation will still intentionally break.

comments.author: Arunkumar-Karri
comments.body: Fix for this issue is available with v1.7.3.

title: CAN FD support in Node Simulation
author: etas-lorenz
body:
I have had a look at programming the simulated systems for sending CAN FD messages. At the moment it is not possible to program CAN FD messages because the CAN message format does not support CAN FD.

At the moment the following is CAN message structure is available
       unsigned int  m_unMsgID       ; message id
       unsigned char m_ucEXTENDED    ; Extended identifier yes/no
       unsigned char m_ucRTR         ; True/false (remote rquest)
       unsigned char m_ucDLC         ;Datalength 0-8
       unsigned char m_ucChannel  ; Channel 1 or 2
Then I have fields for
        unsigned char      m_aucData[8]; Data (up to 8 bytes)

But in order to send CAN FD frames I need to define them. So I need in addition
Fals to set CAN-FD on off
New DLC codes to represent 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 48 or 64 bytes.
Ability to increase data to 64.

In addition, the changed stuffed bits and CRC methods need to be implemented in the CAN FD message send.

Can this be implemented in BUSMASTER. If so, then when?

       unsigned char m_ucRTR         ; True/false (remote rquest)
       unsigned char m_ucDLC         ;Datalength 0-8
       unsigned char m_ucChannel  ; Channel 1 or 2

title: Message transmission issue with BOA
author: RBEI-Shweta
body:
Note1:If we select the BOA hardware and change the Baud rate value of channel 1  to 5000 and channel 2 to 50000,then messages are not transmitted.

Note2:In BOA hardware baud rate value is not updating properly .

"1.Select BOA hardware and select channel 1 value as 500000bps and channel 2 value as 500000bps
2.Function -->Test automation -->Editor
3.Create new test setup file and test case
4.Select Send and verify nodes
5.In Function -->Test automation -->Executor, if we verify the result ,it shows the result as ""Success""
4.Now change the baud rate value as channel 1 value as 50000bps and channel 2 value as 50000bps
5.Save the configuration and verify in test executor, it shows the result as""Fail""

title: Tx window configuration is autosaved when we switch between 'Time Delay Between Blocks' and 'Time Delay Between Messages' when 'Auto Update' option is unchecked.
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
Tx window configuration is autosaved when we switch between 'Time Delay Between Blocks' and 'Time Delay Between Messages' when 'Auto Update' option is unchecked.
1. Open Tx window.
2. Add a block and add 4 messages to the block.
3. Select Auto Update.
4. Check and Set 'Time Delay Between Blocks' as 1 ms.
5. Connect and Transmit. We can observe that messages are transmitted with 1 ms delay
6. Now Disconnect and uncheck Auto Update option. 
7. Uncheck 'Time Delay Between Blocks'. 'Time Delay Between Messages' will be checked  automatically. Set the delay as 2000 ms.
8. Connect and Transmit.
We can observe that messages are getting transmitted with 2000 ms delay even though the Autoupdate option is unchecked.
body_text:
Tx window configuration is autosaved when we switch between 'Time Delay Between Blocks' and 'Time Delay Between Messages' when 'Auto Update' option is unchecked.
1. Open Tx window.
2. Add a block and add 4 messages to the block.
3. Select Auto Update.
4. Check and Set 'Time Delay Between Blocks' as 1 ms.
5. Connect and Transmit. We can observe that messages are transmitted with 1 ms delay
6. Now Disconnect and uncheck Auto Update option. 
7. Uncheck 'Time Delay Between Blocks'. 'Time Delay Between Messages' will be checked  automatically. Set the delay as 2000 ms.
8. Connect and Transmit.
We can observe that messages are getting transmitted with 2000 ms delay even though the Autoupdate option is unchecked.

title: Database: Floats are stored with comma instead of point on a german system
author: FlorianBr
body:
Hello everybody!

I am trying busmaster on a german Windows 7 machine, and have a problem with saving databases. The signals values for offset and scale factor are stored in the DBF-file with a "," like it is done in germany. That messes up parsing the file it seems, because that is also the separator for the entries.
When I manually open the file in a text editor and replace these commas with a full stop, it works.

The original signal line in a dbf-file looks like this:
[START_SIGNALS] Test,8,3,0,U,255,0,1,0,000000,1,000000,0,

But should look like this:
[START_SIGNALS] Test,8,3,0,U,255,0,1,0.000000,1.000000,0,

Is there a way to fix that? Some kind of setting I haven't seen?

Thanks!
body_text:
Hello everybody!

I am trying busmaster on a german Windows 7 machine, and have a problem with saving databases. The signals values for offset and scale factor are stored in the DBF-file with a "," like it is done in germany. That messes up parsing the file it seems, because that is also the separator for the entries.
When I manually open the file in a text editor and replace these commas with a full stop, it works.

The original signal line in a dbf-file looks like this:
[START_SIGNALS] Test,8,3,0,U,255,0,1,0,000000,1,000000,0,

But should look like this:
[START_SIGNALS] Test,8,3,0,U,255,0,1,0.000000,1.000000,0,

Is there a way to fix that? Some kind of setting I haven't seen?

Thanks!

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: It's not complicated to fix it. We'll not make it with the release tomorrow, but the one after should be possible.

comments.author: pkrieger
comments.body:
Hello,

I've a similar problem on a german Win 7 64bit, when using the converter dbc -> dbf all factors < 1 (the one with a decimal separator) are not converted.
I also can't edit the signals - when opening the "Signal Details" on a open Database and typing in 0.5 on the factor field it will be set to 0.0 after closing (typing a "," isn't possible in this field).
After changing the decimal separator in the Windows System Control and restart Busmaster, everything works fine. But if I change Back to the german "," as separator in Widows Settings, also all assosiated Database signals files with a scale factor with a decimal separator wont work (-> factor set to 0) - in "Message Interpretation" Window and all other "physical value" fields are 0.

Hope this helps, thanks!

comments.author: Arunkumar-Karri
comments.body: Fix has been identified for this and will be available in milestone 1.7.2.

title: Cannot build a debug version
author: DavidPfander
body:
Hi,

I', currently trying to build a debug version of the busmaster software. I configured my environment as stated in the setup pdf. I successfully (!) created a release build. However, I couldn't create a debug build, due to a large amount of errors (http://pastebin.com/HXmM9AgN, I unfortunately use a german version of VS2008).  I tried both, the Debug and the busmaster_debug configuration.

Even when creating a release build I have to compile DIL_Interface and Utils before I can compile the whole program. Also, I get thousands of warnings during the compilation (of the release version as well as the debug verion).

Is it possible that I have still omitted some necessary steps in setting up my environment?

Any advice would be greatly appreciated.
body_text:
Hi,

I', currently trying to build a debug version of the busmaster software. I configured my environment as stated in the setup pdf. I successfully (!) created a release build. However, I couldn't create a debug build, due to a large amount of errors (http://pastebin.com/HXmM9AgN, I unfortunately use a german version of VS2008).  I tried both, the Debug and the busmaster_debug configuration.

Even when creating a release build I have to compile DIL_Interface and Utils before I can compile the whole program. Also, I get thousands of warnings during the compilation (of the release version as well as the debug verion).

Is it possible that I have still omitted some necessary steps in setting up my environment?

Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
Many of these warnings are because of outdated compiler settings, type conversion problems, unsafe function usage. We are aware of this. It's not difficult to fix it, however it's the amount of warnings that still needs time to process.

In addition we are in the progress of converting MFC classes to STL classes. This will help in the end to become platform independent and to enhance the user interface.

We appreciate any help that we can get ;-)

Coming back to the issue: AFAIK building a debug version should be possible. So to me this sounds like an update of the development environment documentation.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Since Visual Studio Express Edition is used, Debug version is not supported. busmaster_debug is one configuration that is added which enables debug information in the Release build for debugging purpose. We will update development document as well as suggested.

comments.author: GT-Pfand
comments.body: Thanks for your feedback. I tried to build with busmaster_debug, however, that build configuration doesn't build (even though the release configuration builds successfully).

comments.author: GT-Pfand
comments.body: One reason why debug and busmaster_debug cannot be build is that the libraries that are checked-in in Sources/BIN/Release are missing for the other configurations.

comments.author: GT-Pfand
comments.body:
I was finally able to build a debug build succesfully. Apart from copying the libraries from Sources/BIN/Release (as stated above) I had to deactivate the usage of precompiled headers for the CAN_MHS project.

I still have to build two times for one successful build. There seem to be dependency issues in the build process.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: We will do all the settings required for successful build and will upload the files. Also, if build fails, try select build solution another time without rebuild.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Added all required files.

title: In Node simulation for CAN , when you select Save as option for a simulated system which contains message handler and save it with a different name, message handler is not working
author: RBEI-Anita
body:
1.Go to Configure->simulated systems. Right click on CANBUS and add simulated system say s1.
2.Add a message handler say m1  to  simulated system say  s1. In message handler m1,define structure for a message and transmit (use SendMsg(sMsg) ). 
3.Build and Load.Enable handler.It is working.
4.Now unload, right click on the simulated system s1, and select save as and give name as s2. 
5.Now delete s1. 
6.Right click on CAN Bus and add simulated system s2. It contains all handlers present in the s1. Message handler (m1) is not working in this scenario.
Now add simulated system s1 again, message handler (added previously)is not working here also.

title: #pragma pack missing from header file generated by 1.7.0
author: SperkLabs
body:
The header file generated by version 1.6.4 contained #pragma pack lines. The header file generated by 1.7.0 does not.

A message containing a 16-bit value that was not aligned on a 16-bit boundary was not formed correctly by the new simulated system. After I added the #pragma pack lines by hand and rebuilt it, it worked correctly.
body_text:
The header file generated by version 1.6.4 contained #pragma pack lines. The header file generated by 1.7.0 does not.

A message containing a 16-bit value that was not aligned on a 16-bit boundary was not formed correctly by the new simulated system. After I added the #pragma pack lines by hand and rebuilt it, it worked correctly.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
GCC displays the below arning :

"warning : malformed pragma pack(pop[, id])' - ignored [-Wpragmas]"

This pack was removed considering this issue. Have you used "#pragma pack(pop,8)" to make the file compile properly ?

If this is the case, we can introduce again the same. 

We are using GCC 4.6.1 version.

Please let us know the GCC version which you are using.

comments.author: SperkLabs
comments.body:
I have GCC 4.6.2 right now. I don't recall what version I had before, I think it was 3.x,y something, and I got that warning.

Yes, I have used "#pragma pack(push,8)" and "#pragma pack(pop,8)", and it results in the "malformed pragma..." warning you listed above. After I read http://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gcc/Structure_002dPacking-Pragmas.html, it seems that the pop does not need the 8; it's just an argument required for the push.

So now I have manually added "#pragma pack(push,8)" and "#pragma pack(pop)" and it compiles without the warning, and works correctly.

comments.author: SperkLabs
comments.body: Sorry, I probably shouldn't have closed this issue, since it's still present, and many databases won't work correctly without the #pragma pack.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: Yes, we also concluded yesterday that our code generator should output the correct pragma statements. As soon as the fix is uploaded, we can close this issue.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body: Added pragma pack staement

title: Busmaster can not find ValueCAN3 if serial# >120000
author: ScottGarabedian
body: Tested two ValueCAN3's with different serial numbers, one below 120000 and one above 120000.  Also tested two different laptops with two different versions of Busmaster (1.6.5 and 1.7.0).  Both versions could find the ValueCAN3 with serial number below 120000, but not above 120000.  Both laptops were Windows 7, 64-bit.
body_text: Tested two ValueCAN3's with different serial numbers, one below 120000 and one above 120000.  Also tested two different laptops with two different versions of Busmaster (1.6.5 and 1.7.0).  Both versions could find the ValueCAN3 with serial number below 120000, but not above 120000.  Both laptops were Windows 7, 64-bit.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
There were also reports with serial numbers >39999.

This problem is caused by older versions of the Intrepid driver.

BUSMASTER delivers the driver in an older version.
To get rid of them, delete the icsneovi.dll and the etasneovi.dll from the applications folder.
Then the system-wide installed versions are used.

There is another advantage: The user can download/upgrade/install the drivers independently of BUSMASTER.

As we have similar issues with all other drivers as well, I proposed to drop them all from the installation: #350.

title: Compatibility with PCAN USB Pro
author: JessicaSchulz
body:
Official information from PCAN is that there is no difference in driver nor API between the devices PCAN-USB and PCAN-USB PRO. Nonetheless only the PCAN-USB device is recognized.

Apparently BUSAMSTER Software is requesting  the "name" of the Hardware via  "GetHwParam". The devices just return different names.

The Name PCAN-USB PRO should be added to the Hardware list in the code.
body_text:
Official information from PCAN is that there is no difference in driver nor API between the devices PCAN-USB and PCAN-USB PRO. Nonetheless only the PCAN-USB device is recognized.

Apparently BUSAMSTER Software is requesting  the "name" of the Hardware via  "GetHwParam". The devices just return different names.

The Name PCAN-USB PRO should be added to the Hardware list in the code.

title: CAN messages are not being transmitted when J1939 cpp file is unloaded.
author: RBEI-Pramod
body:
CAN messages are not being transmitted when J1939 cpp file is unloaded.
1) Creat and associate a DB,
2) Configure TX window. Add the DB msg and a non DB msg.
3) Connect and Transmit.
4) Activate J1939 and Go online.
5) Create New J1939 Simulated System.
6) Build, Load and enable all handlers.
7) Unload the Dll.
8) Disconnect.
9) Connect and transmit CAN msgs.
We can observe the msgs are not getting transmitted.
Note: If there are two msgs in Tx window, only one will be transmitted and sometimes both the msgs will not be transmitted.

title: Unable to initialise handler functions for execution
author: SperkLabs
body:
I have a simple engine simulation that listens to J1939 TSC1 messages and sends EEC1 messages on intervals. It worked in version 1.6.4. Version 1.7.0 was unable to import it, so I started from scratch, and pasted the old C code into the new editor window.

It builds successfully, but the "Handler Details" section of the window shows no handlers, even though they are properly set up in the editor.

When I try to load the DLL, I get the error message "Unable to initialise handler functions for execution"

Machine has Windows 7 64-bit, and MinGW32 4.6.2.
body_text:
I have a simple engine simulation that listens to J1939 TSC1 messages and sends EEC1 messages on intervals. It worked in version 1.6.4. Version 1.7.0 was unable to import it, so I started from scratch, and pasted the old C code into the new editor window.

It builds successfully, but the "Handler Details" section of the window shows no handlers, even though they are properly set up in the editor.

When I try to load the DLL, I get the error message "Unable to initialise handler functions for execution"

Machine has Windows 7 64-bit, and MinGW32 4.6.2.

comments.author: RBEI-AshwinRU
comments.body:
Hi,
  Can you please send us the .cpp file to busmaster@in.bosch.com. We will verify the same at our end.

Thanks,
Ashwin

comments.author: SperkLabs
RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
Is this file created in J1939 simulated systems in v1.6.4 ? 

Latest versions save protocol information in the file. It will be like /\* PROTOCOL [CAN] _/ at the start of the file.
The file shared by you contained protocol as CAN and hence it is not allowing to import in J1939 simulated systems. If it is changed as /_ PROTOCOL [J1939] */ then the file can be imported.

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
We created a database to define the messages and signals used in the Engine.cpp file. We are able to import the file and dll successfully in CAN simulated systems. Handlers are also enabled. Please specify some more information.

Or it may be related to #306. Please check if this matches the scenario described there.

comments.author: SperkLabs

title: Remove APIs that are installed by the driver setups anyway
author: etas-lorenz
body:
We currently deliver several vendor APIs as part of the BUSMASTER installation in %ProgramFiles%\BUSMASTER. Each on its own is not sufficient for the hardware access, but an additional Driver Setup/Installation is always necessary.

APIs and drivers are all available as separate and often combined installers from the vendors nowadays. They install versions on the system that are often more modern than the ones we deliver with BUSMASTER today. They can also be upgraded/downgraded by the user more-easily.

Thus we should not deliver these APIs any more. Otherwise we risk that several (incompatible) APIs are installed and used by the applications. Due to the current procedure, we have/had a couple of issues already. I remember at least: #348, #328, #120, and #5.

The following APIs are installed in Windows System32:
- Kvaser: canlib32.dll (installed by canlib.exe and/or kvaser_drivers_w2k_xp.exe)
- Peak: CanApi2.dll (installed by PeakOemDrv.exe)
- Intrepid: icsneo40.dll (installed by DriverSetup.exe)
- Vector: vxlapi.dll (installed by xl_lib_.zip/Setup.exe and Vector_Driver_Setup__.zip/setup.exe)

The following APIs are installed in other locations:
- MHS-Elektronik: mhstcan.dll (installed by TinyCan_221.exe in %ProgramFiles%\tiny_can\can_api)
- ETAS ES581: ETASneo40.dll (available via ETAS BOA v1.4.5 in %ProgramFiles%\ETAS\BOA *)

So all APIs that we deliver and install in the BUSMASTER folder today, are nowadays available either in System32 or in other locations on the system.

AFAIK the only required changes are:
- Remove all mentioned DLLs from the BUSMASTER installation.
- Add path information (%ProgramFiles%\tiny_can\can_api) to TinyCAN LoadLibrary call. Anything else (all LoadLibrary calls) seem to be correct already.
- Check/ensure again for each supported API, that in case it is not available, we point the user to our hardware support wiki page. He'll find their the links to the above mentioned installers.

APIs and drivers are all available as separate and often combined installers from the vendors nowadays. They install versions on the system that are often more modern than the ones we deliver with BUSMASTER today. They can also be upgraded/downgraded by the user more-easily.

Thus we should not deliver these APIs any more. Otherwise we risk that several (incompatible) APIs are installed and used by the applications. Due to the current procedure, we have/had a couple of issues already. I remember at least: #348, #328, #120, and #5.

The following APIs are installed in Windows System32:
- Kvaser: canlib32.dll (installed by canlib.exe and/or kvaser_drivers_w2k_xp.exe)
- Peak: CanApi2.dll (installed by PeakOemDrv.exe)
- Intrepid: icsneo40.dll (installed by DriverSetup.exe)
- Vector: vxlapi.dll (installed by xl_lib_.zip/Setup.exe and Vector_Driver_Setup__.zip/setup.exe)

The following APIs are installed in other locations:
- MHS-Elektronik: mhstcan.dll (installed by TinyCan_221.exe in %ProgramFiles%\tiny_can\can_api)

title: Intrepid ValueCAN Serial number > 10000
author: DrumAliens
body:
We have just taken delivery of some new Intrepid ValueCAN devices and they have stopped working with Busmaster. We have had discussions with Intrepid and there is no difference in the Hardware just that they have increased their serial numbering scheme. Basically they have run out of numbers below 10000

Using the older ValueCAN's (with the lower serial number) we can could connect through the ES581 hardware selection on Busmaster. With Single and Dual channel devices.

Intrepid have also given us new drivers icsneo40.dll, vxlapi.dll and vcand32.dll. I have tried putting the  icsneo40.dll, vxlapi.dll in the c:\Program Files\BUMASTER directory and selected the Intrepid neoVI hardware option but this just reports that "Query is succesful, but no device found".

Any help or ideas would be appreciated

Paul
body_text:
We have just taken delivery of some new Intrepid ValueCAN devices and they have stopped working with Busmaster. We have had discussions with Intrepid and there is no difference in the Hardware just that they have increased their serial numbering scheme. Basically they have run out of numbers below 10000

Using the older ValueCAN's (with the lower serial number) we can could connect through the ES581 hardware selection on Busmaster. With Single and Dual channel devices.

Intrepid have also given us new drivers icsneo40.dll, vxlapi.dll and vcand32.dll. I have tried putting the  icsneo40.dll, vxlapi.dll in the c:\Program Files\BUMASTER directory and selected the Intrepid neoVI hardware option but this just reports that "Query is succesful, but no device found".

Any help or ideas would be appreciated

Paul

comments.author: RBEI-Saravanan
comments.body:
This error will be displayed when the driver is detected successfully but the hardware is not detected.

Could you please check in the device manager whether the device is detected and listed ? Also please reinstall the hardware drivers and check once again.

There is no change in BUSMASTER in hardware detection.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
As described in the hardware support page in the wiki, the hardware selection in BUSMASTER first tries to load ETASneo40.dll, and then icsneo40.dll. So exchanging only the second file is not sufficient. You also have to delete the first file.

Or just select "ETAS BOA". Since BOA v1.4.5 the ES581 is supported.

comments.author: DrumAliens
comments.body:
OK I have got it working by using Lorenz's method. I just renamed the ETASneo40.dll and selected in hardware IntrepidCS NeoVI.

I also tried the ETAS BOA this appeared to work, in that it didn't complain about the hardware and allowed me to select a channel. However, BusMaster just hung when I clicked on the connection button

Paul

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: Ok, then I'm closing this issue.

title: Node Simulation: Individual activation/deactivation of Handlers from out of function
author: etas-lorenz
body:
This is based on customer feedback.

Currently Handlers can only be activated or deactivated on a global base, e.g. all Message, Timer, Key, or Error Handlers.

For certain use cases it is beneficial to (de)activate specific Handlers only, e.g. to (de)activate parts of a rest bus simulation on key press.

I am looking forward to see this great improvement soon, so I am able to use open source software for my daily work.

J. Lichterfeld

title: Problem with database editor and Big Endian signals
author: etas-lorenz
body:
When defining a signal in the database editor with more than 8 bits and Big-Endian, BUSMASTER gives a warning "End bit of signal is out of message length".

Reproducable as follows:
Setup a CAN message with 8 Byte Length, Standard Frame.
Add a signal with Type "unsigned int", Byte Index: 0, Start Bit: 0, Length > 8, Byte Order: "Motorola (Big-Endian)".
body_text:
When defining a signal in the database editor with more than 8 bits and Big-Endian, BUSMASTER gives a warning "End bit of signal is out of message length".

Reproducable as follows:
Setup a CAN message with 8 Byte Length, Standard Frame.
Add a signal with Type "unsigned int", Byte Index: 0, Start Bit: 0, Length > 8, Byte Order: "Motorola (Big-Endian)".

comments.author: Arunkumar-Karri
comments.body:
Hi,

For a Motorola (Big-Endian) signal, the byte order starts in reverse. i.e if message length is 8, and a Big-Endian signal with 64 bit length has to be added, the Byte index should be set to 7 instead of 0.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body: Ah, correct. Or if a put the signal on the first two bytes, the lowest start bit is 8. Closing this issue...

title: Support for Intrepid version of vxlapi
author: etas-lorenz
body:
This is based on customer feedback.

There is special version of the vxlapi.dll (Vector XL driver) available from Intrepid. It acts as a replacement of the original Vector XL Driver Library and allows Vector-only applications to use Intrepid interfaces anyway. This is required and done by certain US OEM diagnostic applications (the Intrepid Vehicle Spy Getting Started Guide mentions Ford). The Intrepid version of vxlapi.dll would allow a parallel usage of the US diagnostic application with BUSMASTER. So we should support it.

The vxlapi.dll implements the functions from the original vxlapi.dll in version 6.7. Certain functions that we check for were implemented in later versions, so BUSMASTER unable to load the driver library.

The solution is to remove the load and check in CAN_LoadDriverLibrary of the following functions:
- xlGetLicenseInfo
- xlSetTimerRateAndChannel
- xlSetTimerBasedNotify
  There are some more functions that are not implemented in v6.7, but we don't check them anyway:
- xlOemContact
- xlCheckLicense
  These functions are most likely not used by BUSMASTER and can be removed safely.

My tests show that the Intrepid vxlapi.dll gets loaded then and when selecting "Vector XL" as Hardware Interface, the ES581 channels can be used.

The vxlapi.dll implements the functions from the original vxlapi.dll in version 6.7. Certain functions that we check for were implemented in later versions, so BUSMASTER unable to load the driver library.

The solution is to remove the load and check in CAN_LoadDriverLibrary of the following functions:
- xlGetLicenseInfo
- xlSetTimerRateAndChannel
- xlSetTimerBasedNotify
  There are some more functions that are not implemented in v6.7, but we don't check them anyway:
- xlOemContact
- xlCheckLicense
  These functions are most likely not used by BUSMASTER and can be removed safely.

My tests show that the Intrepid vxlapi.dll gets loaded then and when selecting "Vector XL" as Hardware Interface, the ES581 channels can be used.

title: Ungewollter Firmware Downgrade bei HW Init mit       ES581.3von 0.83 auf 0.71
author: hukarl
body:
Ungewollter Firmware Downgrade beim Ausführen des Init Befehls
In der INCA Hardware Umgebung bei der ES581.3 von 0.83 auf 0.71.
Vermutlich wegen alter Neo DLL bei Busmaster?
es sollte die hier sein: 
       ETASneo40.dll VErsion:35179
      Diese Datei muss hier liegen:
      1) d:\ETASData\HSP\Configs\1.9.7.0\ES500Systems2\
      2) c:\ETAS\ETASShared10\TgtSvr\
      3) c:\Windows\System32\
      Und den key hier ausschalten=0, dann sollte nix mehr gepacht werden von INCA:
      \\Registry\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\intrepidcs\VSpyCE\
      EnableFirmwareUpdate=0

Kundensetup
      1. HSP 9.7.0 0.83 Upgedatet
      2. Inca Hardware init
      3. downdate
      4. Im HSP Fenster sieht man wieder die alte Version. 0.71

 Die Datei ist mehrfach auf dem Rechner vorhanden.
 Busmaster ist ebenfalls installiert.
 Im Printsceen sehen sie drei unterschiedliche Versionen.
 Können Sie mir sagen, welche ich verwenden soll.
body_text:
Ungewollter Firmware Downgrade beim Ausführen des Init Befehls
In der INCA Hardware Umgebung bei der ES581.3 von 0.83 auf 0.71.
Vermutlich wegen alter Neo DLL bei Busmaster?
es sollte die hier sein: 
       ETASneo40.dll VErsion:35179
      Diese Datei muss hier liegen:
      1) d:\ETASData\HSP\Configs\1.9.7.0\ES500Systems2\
      2) c:\ETAS\ETASShared10\TgtSvr\
      3) c:\Windows\System32\
      Und den key hier ausschalten=0, dann sollte nix mehr gepacht werden von INCA:
      \\Registry\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\intrepidcs\VSpyCE\
      EnableFirmwareUpdate=0

Kundensetup
      1. HSP 9.7.0 0.83 Upgedatet
      2. Inca Hardware init
      3. downdate
      4. Im HSP Fenster sieht man wieder die alte Version. 0.71

 Die Datei ist mehrfach auf dem Rechner vorhanden.
 Busmaster ist ebenfalls installiert.
 Im Printsceen sehen sie drei unterschiedliche Versionen.
 Können Sie mir sagen, welche ich verwenden soll.

comments.author: etas-lorenz
comments.body:
I've discussed the unexpected downgrade/upgrade issue also last week with some other people with BOA experience.

So first of all, here is the link to the function where the DLLs get loaded:
https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/blob/master/Sources/CAN_ICS_neoVI/CAN_ICS_neoVI.cpp#L3091
As you see BUSMASTER first tries to load ETASneo40.dll and then ICSneo40.dll.

Both files are installed in the BUSMASTER application folder. Some of these files can also be found in the BOA directory, the Windows System32 directory, and the Intrepid Vehicle Spy directory, sometimes in different versions.

To make it ever more complex: When Windows detects that another application already uses a library with the same name, the same would be used for BUSMASTER and vice versa. For that reason it can make a difference in which order you start your applications. I guess this is what you discovered.

I've no final clue what the best overall solution is. Maybe we should decouple the driver installation completely from the BUSMASTER installation and should give the user the choice to install it separately. System32 is in the standard library search path, so BUSMASTER should find it there also. It would also helpful when BUSMASTER outputs the library version and possible the path also. What do you think?

title: Hidden Close and Update buttons in Tx Message Window.
author: etas-lorenz
body:
This is based on customer feedback.

In the Configure Transmission Messages window, the buttons Update and Close are hidden when the window is resized smaller. But these buttons are important because if the user cannot see them, then he can only close the window using the x button, which cancels all the changes. (This was my original problem, I would make changes and they were removed. It was only when I made the window really big did I see them).

Please redesign the windows to make it impossible to hide these buttons. This is standard windows behavior.

title: No RTR frames in the database?
author: josch222
body:
In the database it is not possible to define RTR messages.
I have an  TX-RTR message which is answered by the DUT with a message that has the same identifier.
It should be possible to define 2 messages with a different name but same identifier, but only if one of them is a RTR message.

To make it a bit more complicated: The RTR has a DLC >0 but zero databytes are sent. The DLC represents the length of the triggered response message.

title: MHS Tiny-Can IV-XL is not found with other FTDI device present
author: josch222
body:
When I try to select the "MHS TinyCAN" in menu Configure -> Hardware Interface
the Trace Window shows "Listing hardware interfaces failed" in case any other FTDI device is present on the USB-bus.
If I disconnect or deactivate all of them (except the Tiny-Can IV-XL) in the MS-Windows device manager it will be found and works.
If I reconnect other FTDI devices the Tiny-Can IV-XL remains usable until I select Configure -> Hardware Interface -> Simulation. Trying to reselect fails until all FTDI devices are removed.
Entering the serial number of the Tiny-Can IV-XL in the Configure -> Controller dialog does not help.

OS is Windows XP SP3, FTDI driver version is 2.8.24.0
This problem shows up on 2 different PCs, one of them was cleaned from all FTDI-related stuff and the newest drivers where installed again.

comments.author: josch222
comments.body:
The problem seems to be solved, at least in a very short test, with the following procedure:
1. Replace the mhstcan.dll which comes with Busmaster 1.7.2 with the one which is installed with the
TinyCan_222.exe from the MHS website in the Download section: 
http://www.mhs-elektronik.de/index.php?module=download&action=list
2. Select the hardware interface in Busmaster, Configure -> Hardware Interface -> "MHS TinyCAN"
3. Sleect baudrate and insert the serial number of your Tiny-Can IV-XL (it's printed on top, in the lower left)
in the Configure -> Controller dialogue.

Now the "Connect" works without error Message and the blinking LED indicates a working connection.

title: Baudrate configuration should use bps, rather than kbps
author: etas-lorenz
body:
The baudrate configuration is currently set with a unit of kbps.
Some standard baudrates such as 83.333, 62.500, or 33.333, cannot be set adequately.

Please change the baudrate configuration to a unit of bps.
